lines.x: Is everybody here?
Look behind you. The whole street belongs to us... an awe-inspiring pack of wolves.
Good. No one should miss the Carnevale.
The weapons?
The secret weapon caches have been opened by the Don's orders. Guns, bombs, staves, crossbows... everything is fair game.
Good! But remember the most important thing—keep your mask on.
Nobody cares who's behind the mask, which famiglia they're from. Nothing you do will be punished.
Carnevale—Siracusa's greatest festival!
Cut down our foes! All the famiglie that oppose us will be swept aside! Nothing shall stand in our way in Siracusa!
What about the officials of the city hall? They're even worse than the other famiglie, those slugs that keep babbling about their lofty ideals.
The courthouse is right there behind us. They can sit and watch in that proud white tower of theirs... or get a crossbow bolt in their throat if they don't behave.
I like the sound of that!
Do you smell it in the wind? It's the smell of blood, just waiting for a chance to spill...
Tonight, there is no Grey Hall, there are no laws, no rules, no order. Tomorrow, there will be a new order in Siracusa, starting with this new city—
Alright, I think the speech has gone on for long enough.
I'm—er... Noemi! Trainee officer at the Nuova Volsinii Police Department!
Look around you! There is nowhere to run!
According to the New Urban Management Act, even members of the Dodici Famiglie must undergo a background check and standard registration prior to entering Nuova Volsinii.
Your previous acts of violence are on record, and the violence that you are planning will not go unpunished. Illegal ownership and usage of weapons is a serious crime!
Where do you think you are? Here, er, *ahem*...
Nuova Volsinii has no place for famiglie!
Cut!
I thought I had it all memorized. I don't know why...
There are only a few days until the Carnevale, Noemi. We need to finish this soon.
Sorry, I didn't think that my first task as a trainee would be shooting a movie...
Are you nervous because of the camera, or because the scene in front of you looks real?
If it's the former—look at these blokes here. I'm nervous that they'll let slip the names of their favorite pizzerias while trying to say their lines.
I worked at my papa's pizzeria before I started police school...
Anyway, you look good on screen and have a decent memory. You're the best one for the job.
If it's the latter—you're a graduate of Siracusa's first police academy. You're gonna have to stare into the eyes of the wise guys at some point. It's your job.
B-Both, I guess...
I promised Papa that I'd finish my training and become a cop. I'll get my job done, whatever it is!
Please let me have another go!
Alright, Venezians, here we go for another take.
*Moan*—
3, 2, 1, action!
...What do you think?
What answer would you like to hear?
Maybe the actors playing the mafiosi should act more intimidating? Show just how far Nuova Volsinii has come?
Or maybe you don't like how Venezia Trucking is involved in the filming? But at least they have not attempted to challenge Nuova Volsinii's New Urban Management Act.
They're just providing 'employees' as extras. They didn't even ask for ad space in the promotional video...
You know I'm not doing this because I like it.
I don't know, you look pretty happy in the limelight.
Signore! It's almost time to shoot your speech!
Be right there!
I-I'll make sure they know I'm just the Acting Mayor...
Not for long, though.
A grand festival, formal inauguration as mayor, a promotional video, your face in every corner of the city, so that no one would miss it...
We've talked about this, Lavinia.
We've done a lot in this new city. We've founded a new city hall, enacted the New Urban Management Act, and put into place policies that exclude the mafioso order...
But we have yet to face the big questions head-on—from Signora Sicilia, and the other famiglie that have designs on the new city.
Is there a better time than now?
Here, at the one-year anniversary of Nuova Volsinii, we have invited all citizens, even friends from abroad, to come to Nuova Volsinii.
We have revived the word Carnevale, which has not been used in the last half-century, and given it new meaning, in order to demonstrate...
This country can and will change the old things that bind it, and it starts with this city.
But how will painting a target on your back contribute to all that you've said?
We need a banner...
And the famiglie of Siracusa are waiting for a target that they can attack and harm.
Just because the famiglie have kept a low profile doesn't mean they've given up on controlling this city. They have their eyes on you.
...I'm not afraid of them.
I won't let them have their way, not again...
I know how you feel, Leon, but what we need right now is not a reckless declaration of war.
You need to protect yourself. I don't want to see you...
Signore! Are you ready?
C-Coming!
The trucks will have to be returned to the union as soon as the shooting is done. They're needed for the festival preparations.
I know, half a day is as much as the union can give us. Don't worry, it's all been arranged.
I need to go. A lot to do at the courts.
Is there trouble?
Nothing major. Some issues with a few companies' registrations.
Their nose for loopholes in the law would put my law school classmates to shame.
About dinner—
There's leftover pasta in the fridge. Help yourself.
No... what I mean is...
I promised to join Demetri for dinner tonight... I can't avoid him forever.
Take care of yourself, Leon.
Keep an eye on the camera's indicator light, Signore. We can begin when you're ready.
Alright.
Good evening, citizens.
It is my honor to speak to you today as a citizen of Nuova Volsinii.

lines.st: I bring you an invitation—
Welcome to a new era for Siracusa.

lines.x: H-Here!
I had to walk five blocks and stand in a queue to buy this salami. And this wine is the best from Nonno's collection... he's not in any shape to drink anymore.
Good, little tailor, you're a good kid.
There's so little to do at night in Nuova Volsinii. In the old days, on a night like this... hehe.
(Cocky whistling)
(Awkward whistling)
Eh, cut it out, Luchino! It's so damn dumb.
I warned you last time when you tried to show off the jargon you learned from movies—
Imitating a mafioso doesn't make you one. Besides... just get real, and stick to tailoring.
...Yes sir.
Alright, you got your wine and salami. I got to go.
Going already? Thought you'd be pestering us for stories about the famiglia again.
Nonno found out that I was hanging out with you. He doesn't want—
Ha! And you want to be somebody?
What are you doing outside, Luchino?
The cops are stepping up their patrols ahead of the Carnevale. Don't let them catch you roaming on the streets.
...Nonno's calling.
That old man—
Everybody respected us and feared us back in the day. Now we're letting an old tailor like Umberto walk all over us.
Sure, follow the rules of wherever you end up, but the rule of Nuova Volsinii is to throw all the old rules out.
Even big famiglie with a seat in the Grey Hall, like the Saluzzo, have to keep their tail firmly between their legs here... how times have changed.
'Saluzzo'...
Er, I'm going in.
N-Nonno.
...The suit's already packed. You remember where to deliver it to, right?
Yeah.

Come back right away if the customer doesn't have any requests after trying it on.
I know this is a special client, and I know you've always wanted to meet the 'real VIPs'.
*Cough*—
I know, I know, you've said it many times.
I'll get your medicine on the way and come back as soon as I've made the delivery. Don't worry, just get some rest.

lines.st: Change is happening around us.
Slowly, but surely. We can all see it.
We are taking back what should have been ours all along. Our lives, our courage.
The burden of history weighs heavy. Sometimes, we find our feet mired in the past.
But we will keep walking. We will never stop.
We are still strong.

lines.x: I came here first! You're cutting the line!
Which company do you represent, sir?
I... I'm with the Truckers' Union. I have important documents that I need Judge Lavinia to take care of today. I have an appointment with her!
How important? Will it bring hundreds of millions of florins in investments to Nuova Volsinii?
Well, no...
Then it's nothing particularly urgent.
I suggest that you remain patient while important business is being discussed behind those doors, involving numbers that you cannot even fathom.
That's the biggest contribution you can make to the city.
I must remind you, Judge Lavinia, that the law is founded upon the principle of fairness, and no one should receive special treatment.
It is difficult not to suspect that you frequently target companies under the Saluzzo banner out of your own prejudice against us.
Did you say prejudice?
It is past nine in the evening, signore. Did you think I volunteered to do overtime, unpaid, and go through the ordeal of sitting in a room with you, just so I could satisfy my 'prejudice'?
Here is Saluzzo Vino's previous submission for their new winery, which lists one hundred and thirty security personnel.
Here is the revised submission, which instead lists thirty logistics personnel, fifty winemakers, and fifty technical personnel.
You don't have to respect me, signore, but the least you could do is respect the New Urban Management Act, and have a modicum of common sense.
...What will it take for you to stop breathing down our necks?
Easy. Nuova Volsinii welcomes any honest business with open arms, but there is no place here for any dishonest famiglia.
Saluzzo can sell his wine to Leithanien, Columbia, even Ursus for all I care...
But not a single shelf in Nuova Volsinii will carry wine bearing the label of Saluzzo's new winery until it passes the qualification audit.
I'll give it to you, Judge Lavinia. Not one government or court employee has dared talk to us like this in the entire history of Siracusa.
The law of Nuova Volsinii bends for no one.
Get used to it, or things are only going to get harder for you.
I've already wasted too much time with Saluzzo wines today, signore. Kindly remove yourself from my office, and remove your men from the door, because they're in the way of that lady outside.
I heard the impertinence that you showed towards her. She deserves an apology.
...Hmph.
Good evening, Judge Lavinia! Sorry to take up your time, but please, take a look at this!
It's the dispatch record of the Truckers' Union in recent days, recording the driver, cargo, route, and time of every dispatch.
There's so much that it took quite a while to put together... I would like you to review it.
Thanks. It's very detailed... but why are you asking me to review it?
You said logistics around the Carnevale is really important, and to be on guard of smugglers...
Yes, but it was just a reminder... besides, you're the president of the Truckers' Union, Eirene. You can make your own judgment.
Oh... you recognize me?
It's not like we haven't met before... the most diligent driver in Nuova Volsinii, and the most trusted leader of the Nuova Volsinii Freight Workers and Truck Drivers Mutual Aid Association.
It's always going to be just 'Truckers' Union' to us...
Without workers and drivers like you, there would be no Nuova Volsinii. You should all be proud of yourselves.
T-Thank you.
I'll take the report. Thank you for your vigilance; I trust your judgment... is there anything else?
Well... the list of new truck drivers that we submitted last week hasn't been approved yet.
I would like to provide my assurance for one of the names on the list...
You understand, Eirene, why we're very thorough about background checks for truck drivers...
Of course! I know... logistics is very important to Nuova Volsinii. We can't give the famiglie a chance to get their foot in!
A Reproba from Ursus with almost no social relations and whose past is virtually unknown... You do see my concern, right?
I-I promise, she has nothing to do with the mafia. And she's a great driver!
Her resume is like this because she's had some hard luck... she really, really needs a job right now!
I'm thinking, Judge Lavinia... the laws of Nuova Volsinii protect us from the mafia. When our citizens need help, perhaps...
Oh, I don't mean to pressure you! You should deal with it whatever way you see fit—
You're too wound up, Eirene.
I can see that you're trying to keep your distance from me.
I, I really like you and Signor Bellone, Judge Lavinia, I really do!
Hehe, thanks.
Same for Sommer.
Sommer? The driver who came with you to Nuova Volsinii? The one always with a lighter in his hand...
You remember! He's my best friend.
We came to Nuova Volsinii a year ago. Back then, City Hall's notice about the Truckers' Union had been up for half a month, but no one dared to apply, fearing mafia reprisals.
Sommer and I read it a few times and were about to leave when you and Signor Bellone stopped us.
You stood before the notice longer than anyone else.
...We came because we couldn't stay in Sicilia.
I remember what you said to me... I, I don't mean to make this awkward, but my life would be very different without you.
I know you don't want the famiglie—I mean, 'companies' to interfere in Nuova Volsinii's logistics, and I know they haven't given up on it.

The result, however, is that the Truckers' Union has become the only legal transportation organization, and it is growing by the day...
All our drivers have something in their past that they're not proud of, before they came to Nuova Volsinii...
Anyway, we're all looking for a fresh start, a second chance.
That's why you don't want to be seen to be too close to City Hall or the courts.
...Yes.
I know, there are many things that will take time to get there.
But I promise you that the mafia will never get a foothold in the Truckers' Union.
And I voted for Signor Bellone when he ran for mayor! Everyone can see how well he’s done as Acting Mayor for the past year. I'm sure—*grumble*.
Oh...
Haven't had dinner yet?
I-I'm fine! I'm not hungry yet...
I still have some pizza here, if you don't mind that it's gotten a little cold.
I-I can't possibly...
Think of it as helping me out. It's too much for me to finish on my own, and we can't very well talk about work on an empty stomach, can we?
Besides, there is no greater crime in Siracusa than letting good, authentic pizza go to waste.
If you put it that way... I guess it would be rude to refuse.
Tell me more about the present state of the Truckers' Union, Eirene.
Oh, uh... okay!

Average pizza sauce last month is up five times from the previous months...
Oh, I mean, dispatches...
And this pizza sauce is really good.
Maybe we should finish our dinner first.

lines.st: A friend once said something that really left an impression on me—
'Our power comes from the very lives we have created.'
Our power will protect all that we have built.

lines.x: That's the fifth slice of pizza and third glass of juice that you just eliminated. Are you that hungry?
Shut up and drink this espresso.
It's so damn sour!
If we screw up this mission because this swill makes me throw up, whose head am I gonna give the boss, yours?
Live with it. I just picked it up off the shelf.
Nobody uses this frontage road anyway. Besides, it's just transporting a bunch of 'tires', what could go wrong?
No need to get all worked up about an unimportant job like this.
I'm tired of listening to your complaints for the past two months, Gambino.
Haven't you figured out our new boss yet? Trying too hard to show off in front of him will only have the opposite effect.
Fresh meat like us, we gotta be patient and wait for our chance.
Well, ain't you the model employee, Capone.
I thought you would at least show a little bit of, I don't know, shame? There's hardly any place for a Sicilian in Siracusa these days.
I'm not Sicilian anymore. It's your shame, not mine.
I just want to get my Nuova Volsinii citizenship sooner rather than later. All the work I put in over the past six months, all the guarantee money I paid, and I'm only halfway to the required points—
Halfway?! You've got fifty more points than I do? Have you been doing—*gasp*—community service while I wasn't looking?
Shut your crap, Gambino. We're on our last chance and you know it.

Keep your eyes sharp on the street. It should be coming any time now—
You got any pizza left? Give me one.
Cazzo, it's cold, how do you even manage to eat this...
Ach... I'm late.
You're late, Leon.
I'm sorry, it's been a really busy day.
Still working on your inaugural speech?
Well, that too... We're planning a birthday party for an entire city. It's not quite the same as planning a party at home, is it?
I'm surprised... you still remember.
Of course I do. Did you think I was going to forget how you bought ginger ale instead of beer for the banquet, and pocketed the difference to buy a car?
Well, I guess that DID happen.
You sound... distracted, Demetri.
Do I? It's nothing. I guess it's been too long since we last saw each other.
Take care on your way, Leon.
Might have taken that as a threat, if we weren't in Nuova Volsinii.
Enough joking and hurry up. I'm waiting at the seat by the window.
Leontuzzo hangs up.
He has not seen the man on the other end of the phone for a year now. He knows what he wants and how to decline his offer.
Leontuzzo shakes his head at the thought of the reunion, which promises to be a less-than-pleasant one.
A beam of light shines on his face.
What the—who drives like that?
Wait, that vehicle—
Hey, what's going on with my steering wheel—
Keep your distance! It's dangerous!
Out of the way! Make way for the stretcher!
Signore! Can you hear me?
Hang in there! The ambulance is almost here!
Where... am... I...
Stay still, signore! You've lost a lot of blood—
Who is... that...?
The last thing Leontuzzo remembers before he loses consciousness is a blurry yellow silhouette—
A taxi.
The Carnevale has brought countless visitors from Siracusa and beyond to Nuova Volsinii.
This taxi does not seem different from any other that carries visitors across the city.
Except for the great, strange shadow that it wears.
Not cast by the towering buildings, nor a trick of the neon lights. It is the shadow of a wolf.
The taxi slithers through the city like a ghost, carrying a wolf from the wilderness.
Slower, Lappland.
What? The ageless, deathless Signore dei Lupi, afraid of being thrown out of a car?
It's your car I'm worried about. Keep driving it like this, and it'll fall apart.
We managed to get it fixed when it was just a bunch of scrap metal lying out there in the wilderness. It's strong, trust it.
Or do you not trust my driving skills? I don't fool around with my own life... except when necessary.
You don't want the police to find you and throw you in jail, or the mafia to find you and throw you into a meat grinder, do you? At least, not yet.
We're getting better at reading each other's minds, Zaaro.
We still have a Carnevale to go to, after all.
I thought you would scorn the Carnevale for lacking in revelry, seeing all the colorful lights and balloons that line the streets.
Of course not.
Carnevale had been a thing for centuries, dating back to when Siracusa was just a backwater province of Leithanien.
The Lupo of Siracusa found that they were still restless and uneasy, even after getting their own city, and the order of the famiglia.
And so, for a few days in a year, the famiglie run amok in the city, settling scores, fighting over territory, or simply engaging in wanton violence.
Assault, arson, robbery, murder—everything was fair game as long as you had your mask on.
The first thing Signora Sicilia did when she came to power was to abolish the festival, but Nuova Volsinii has persuaded her to permit its revival.
They hope that reviving the festival without the brutality and cruelty would give this blank slate of a city new meaning, and a sense of belonging for the people who have come to seek out a new life.
Is that not Carnevale—a wonderful thing?
Have we gotten into poetry now? Your showmanship is beyond mortal help, Lappland.
Just excited about it.
I promise that the reborn festival will be an unforgettable one for everyone—the Lupi included.
I hope you remember our agreement. This round is coming to a close. Do not disappoint me, Lappland.
As I said, our battle will continue for thousands upon thousands of years.
Well, strap in, and hang on—
What are you shipping?
Wine.
Waybill number?
What's the company?
Sorry. Don't know what you mean.
The goods you're hauling there. What corporation owns them? How's that hard to understand?
Haha. You must be new. Doesn't understand how things work around here.
S'alright. Listen and learn. You should ask, 'What famiglia are you with?'
You...
Sorry, signore. What's the company?
...Saluzzo Vino.
Right. Your information checks out. Go on ahead.
Don't look at me like that, and don't try to bring the old ways over here. Look at the sign above the port.
You're in Nuova Volsinii.

lines.st: The Metropolitans

lines.x: Boss, you came just in time. I got something to discuss with you.
We're not taking any trucks out today. We're gonna fix up the roads around camp.
If Sandro blows another tire next time he backs up, he'll have no one to blame but his own bad luck, haha.
Also, the custom-made tent has arrived. We're planning to set it up near the western exit.
The new campsite is huge. There's room for the awning, water purification system, and barbecue grill. It's in a relatively quiet area, which should help the new recruits adjust more easily.
I also added an outdoor projector screen so that the people in the camp can enjoy a little entertainment.
When I have time, I'll go rent some Wrankwood films... I know what you're gonna say—no mafia flicks.
By the way, I heard city hall has already started building a new resettlement area?
Haha, well, they'd better hurry up—otherwise, I might not want to move out when the time comes.
Right now, the trucks can only park in the camp. Everyone has to live in the trucks. It's really inconvenient, especially for guys with families...
But at least we don't have to deal with those big Families anymore. These trucks belong to us. Standing together to protect them gives us a sense of security.
Right.
Sometimes I look at the camp over there and think, 'Who would believe that this was a barren wasteland just a year ago?'
By the way, boss, why didn't you come back last night?
You all distracted me. I almost forgot what I was doing here!
Bring me the district heads—Sandro, Ginter, Sommer...
Sommer isn't here.
Huh? Isn't he off work today?
I told him so many times already, I get why he wants to bring Guri to Nuova Volsinii for treatment as soon as possible.
Guri is my little sister too. I told him I'd do my part to help. I told him to stop taking extra long hauls all the time.
Boss, don't get mad. It's probably not what you think.
What about the unlucky loner?
She isn't here either. I haven't seen her since last night.
That's weird. Whatever. Gather up everybody still in camp. We need to help Judge Lavinia investigate some things.
The sun is shining brightly.
The cordoned-off area has turned the bustling street quiet. Bloodstains mingle with the tire tracks on the ground as police officers rush back and forth.
Nuova Volsinii is not as rainy as the other cities in Siracusa. Without the rain to obscure it, the accident scene stands out all the more strikingly.
...Have you made any more progress?
The surveillance around here is broken, so we could not obtain any video footage. We were forced to scour the area for physical evidence.
Here you can see the tire marks left by Signor Bellone's car and the other vehicle that has since left the scene.

The collision occurred at a corner where there's a bad blind spot. There's also a traffic light there though, so the two cars shouldn't have collided. And yet, both veered oddly at almost the same moment—
That's when Signor Bellone's car was struck by the other vehicle, which quickly fled the scene.
It may have been a routine traffic accident, but we cannot exclude the possibility that it was...
Yes.
Have you learned anything new about our mystery vehicle?
It's just as we concluded last night—a large box truck of some sort.
I see. Delivery trucks, shuttle buses, fire trucks, specialized construction vehicles... 'Box trucks' of this size are rare in Nuova Volsinii.
I've already asked Eirene to help me investigate. The truck drivers are much better equipped than we are to find clues.
Judge Lavinia, Signor Bellone...
The fractures and internal bleeding have been addressed through surgery, but the severe concussion has left the patient in a coma, at least for now.
In a sense, we can't say the patient is out of mortal danger yet.
Will... will he recover?
As a doctor, I will not promise anything... However, as a citizen of Nuova Volsinii, I sincerely hope Signor Bellone makes a full recovery, and I'll do my best to make that happen.
I wish you the best of luck, Judge Lavinia.
They did it. They made a move...
Judge Lavinia, should we go grab some of the mafiosi, and then—
Don't even think about it.
That's the famiglie's way; it is not our way.
Forgive me! But I think if anyone had a reason to harm Signor Bellone, it would be one of those famiglia bastards!
We shall find the culprit, no matter who they are. They must be brought to justice.
But who could it be?
The Saluzzos?
The Venezias?
The Medicis?
The Leotis?
Lavinia turns her gaze to the restaurant on the corner.
The restaurant is small, and the seats by the window face the middle of the road.
Demetri invited me to dinner tonight and I promised him I'd be there. I can't keep avoiding him forever, Lavinia.
Leon, what is wrong with all this...?
Signor Antonio, I've already trimmed the stray fur on your ears and tail. The next step is applying the conditioner.
This might take a little while. Feel free to close your eyes and rest for a spell.
No problem. You just do your thing.
Is this your first time here?
Yes, yes it is. Carle said if your own personal nurse had not fallen ill... well, in any case it's my honor to serve you today.
Don't be nervous. You're doing a great job.
Thank you. Heh.

Our family has spent generations studying Lupo fur. We started as traveling craftsmen and eventually opened the first grooming center. It's been hundreds of years, and we've gathered plenty of expertise along the way!
Lupo fur is generally coarse with inner and outer layers, while Perro fur is much finer and softer. At first glance, they may seem similar, but in reality, they're worlds apart.
That's why I specially formulated this conditioning treatment for you. Its blend of rich botanical essences can help firm up the fur while still retaining key nutrients.
What do you think? Do you like it?
Of course. You've put so much care into this whole thing. Why wouldn't I be happy with it?
Signor Antonio, you are kind and gentle. You're not at all as scary as Carle said...
I think there must have been some misunderstanding. I'm just a simple businessman. Who would do business with a scary man?
Haha... By the way, Signor Antonio, would you like to try a semi-permanent styling for your ears and tail?
That way, their shape will be more defined and look better. Plus, no one will be able to tell you aren't a Lupo—
Oh... hold on.
It sounds like you have strong opinions about my ears and tail.
Well, I heard Carle say you wanted to look more like a Lupo.
Hah. You mean to say that I, as a lowly Perro, should rather look more like my own race?
What kinda beastshit are you talking?!
Carle?
I... I just wanted to show Signor Antonio here—
That's enough, shut your face! Get outta here!
Scusa, Antonio...
Don't worry about it. Not your fault.
Here's our shipping invoice for this month.
Antonio quickly scans the invoice and puts an 'X' on the signature line.
A batch of 'tires' is arriving at the warehouse today, and you're in charge of inspection, aren't you? Why are you still at the estate?
I'll head over right away. Don't worry, nothing will go wrong.
Where are we...? Wait...
Lemme go! Why did you bring me here?
Quiet, ragazzo!
Keep yellin' and I'll color the rest of your face to match that black eye!
Capone, keep an eye on him. I gotta go tell the boss.
Boss, we got a problem.
There was a car accident at the port last night. Leontuzzo got hurt.
The Acting Mayor! That's big news, but what does it have to do with us?
The truck that hit Leontuzzo was hauling our 'tires'.
It drove off before we could even answer.
The man paces the room, his heavy footsteps drowning out the sound of his labored breathing.
He walks over to the large oak cask and quickly downs three cups of brandy.
Antonio, right now you should be...
Wait, wait, wait. I despise impatience. It clouds our judgment.
Were there witnesses at the scene?
Just one. Capone and I brought him back here.
Good work.
Now, listen to me carefully:
First, keep an eye on everyone in the household. If anyone goes somewhere they shouldn't be going or is doing a job they shouldn't be doing, bring them to me immediately.
Keep a close watch on the other famiglie in the city—the Saluzzos, the Medicis, the Leotis... Figure out which famiglia seems the most nervous. They're the ones who made the move on Leontuzzo.
I want to know who, at this particular moment in time, is so eager to upset the apple cart.
And the truck from the accident... are we just not gonna look for it, or?
Go look for it, but don't do anything too drastic.
As long as the truck itself wasn't defective, what does it matter who was driving, what cargo was being transported, or how it's connected to Venezia Trucking?
Go and reassure some of our other 'friendly' truck drivers. Those long-haulers are more prone to losing their composure than the average man.
Alright. I'll get right to it.
Wait a sec, Carle.
Gambino, you handle this. Bring some of the boys with you.
You got it, boss.
Antonio, I—I'm sorry. It was my mistake.
No one could have predicted that sort of thing would happen.
You're supremely talented, Carle. You've been in this famiglia longer than I have. You oversee half the vehicle repair stations in Montelupe.

If I were you, I'd probably be wondering myself: how ever would that Perro, Antonio, get so many opportunities, getting to manage the business of the new city on his own, if he hadn't married the old man's daughter?
I... don't...
Listen to me, Carle. All the cities, including Nuova Volsinii, have to follow the rules. Those who do not follow the rules... well... they don't get to walk away with decency.
This is what the Don told me, and now I'm telling it to you.
Please give me another chance, Antonio.
The Don should arrive in Nuova Volsinii in the next day or two. I don't want him to be bothered with this nonsense, got it?
O—Of course, Signore! Thank you!
Wait a second. There's one more thing we need to discuss.
That grooming specialist I had just now. I really don't like him.
I got it. I'll fire him at once.
No, no. I don't mean that I dislike his work. I don't like him, personally.
...I got it. You won't see him again.
Did you find it?
Nope. We looked through several parking lots but didn't find any trucks that looked like they had been in an accident.
The Truckers' Union owns thousands of vehicles, and almost none of them have been idle these past few days. If we checked them one by one, we wouldn't finish until after Carnevale was over!
Boss, Judge Lavinia asked you to help find clues, but now you're targeting the Truckers' Union? It's almost like you think one of us hit the Mayor's car...
When it comes to big box trucks in Nuova Volsinii, who is more involved than us?
There are plenty of other groups involved! There are the vehicle factories, the repair stations, the Nuova Volsinii Port...
Besides, none of our trucks passed through that intersection at the time of the accident!
Alright, Pedro. Obviously, I do not believe the folks from the union would do something like that. This is just for peace of mind.
Signor Bellone and Judge Lavinia are both good people, are they not?
Proving that we had nothing to do with this will save them a lot of time and effort. It'll help the police solve the case faster and find the real culprit!
That's true...
By the way, have you gotten in touch with Sommer? He's covering several districts in the south, so having him check things out would be quicker.
Not yet.
Strange... Never mind, let's focus on what's at hand first.
Alright, we'll keep searching.
Who is it?
Eirene? It's... it's me.
Sommer, you've been here, in the camp?
Didn't you hear what we were talking about? Why didn't you come out?
Why are your clothes all torn... and what's with the scratches on your face?
What's going on?
Last night...
That truck that hit Leontuzzo last night.... don't tell me...
Yes... I was the driver...
I've sent Pedro and the others away, so no one will be coming to the camp for now.
Eirene, you were with Judge Lavinia last night. Do you know how badly Leontuzzo is injured? Is he...?
They were able to rescue him from the crash site last night, but his condition is not good.
The trucker slumps into the chair like a deflated balloon, covering his face with his dirty gloves.
He's still so young, and such a good man... Please, don't let anything happen to him—ah, I really...!
What happened, actually?
Was it an accident?
I, I don't know. I was just driving along, and all of a sudden, my steering wheel wouldn't turn right.
I didn't have time to react and crashed the truck. By the time I came to, it was all over...
But... even if you panicked at the moment, you shouldn't have left a critically injured person by the roadside and driven away!
Do you realize that if first responders had arrived just a few minutes later, Leontuzzo would have...
I know you're not like that.
Sorry, Eirene. I really wanted to help him. I know I should have helped him. I'm not a bad guy.
But, but I had to drive off.
Why?
Sommer, talk to me!
What was in that truck?
Eirene's voice starts to tremble. The driver, slumped in his chair, looks pale.
I'm sorry, Eirene...
Sommer, who were you driving that truck for?
Venezia Trucking... Famiglia Venezia.
The silence hangs in the air for what seems like forever. Then, Eirene finally speaks.
Is this because of Guri?
Don't try to justify it, Eirene. I know I made a huge mistake.
Why? Why go to them rather than seek help from the Truckers' Union?
You're the most senior trucker in the union. You know how unique our situation is in Nuova Volsinii—both City Hall and the famiglie are watching us like hawks.
Eirene, we all know how expensive Oripathy medication is.
Besides, once I bring Guri to Nuova Volsinii, between settling her in and getting her into a good school... who knows how much all of that will cost?
I'm here too, aren't I? I can help. You're the one who saved me back in Sicilia. Guri is my sister too.
Eirene, the union can't help me fix this.
So you're gonna do it by yourself...?
Goods shipped in from other cities or shipments between local merchants, every item we touch must be registered in advance.
In the past six months, I've personally reviewed every single schedule, and we've passed all routine inspections. How could you alone have managed to—could it be that...?
I'm not doing this alone, Eirene.
So none of you know where Sommer went?
Is it possible he already ran back to Sicilia under cover of darkness? I mean, he did hit the Acting Mayor's car, you know!
The police are going to throw everything they have at this case. Will we be exposed? Smuggling contraband is a red line for City Hall, especially since we were doing it to help you...
No, no. This has nothing to do with Venezia Trucking.
What? You mean, you're gonna tie up all the 'loose ends'?
'Tie up loose ends'? Spoken like a true wise guy. Haha.
But no, I'm not. Relax. Do you see me carrying any weapons? Just keep your mouth shut, and everything will be fine. Capisce?
But what if the cops start breathing down our necks?
We've been hiding this stuff from Eirene and getting sweated by City Hall all this time. If something really does happen, you can't just wash your hands of all this!
(Shrugs)
If you really wanna pick nits here, we are even less to blame.
Venezia Trucking has never violated the 'New Urban Management Act', nor has it ever tried to infiltrate the Truckers' Union or interfere with Nuova Volsinii's logistics system.
What do you mean?
Sommer wants to bring his daughter here to treat her for Oripathy, right?
He already found a good hospital, but he can't afford to pay for the treatment. Instead, he's using union trucks as private hospital vehicles to help the Logistics Department transport goods.
Venezia Trucking is at the bottom of the list, when it comes to that Hospital's financial backers. We have no connection to Sommer, apart from being in the same city.
No. That's not true.
And you, you're just a drunk who can't even sit up straight in the driver's seat half the time. You had to secretly hire someone to drive for you just to keep your job.
We signed a service agreement, I'll have you know! I'm just a driver-for-hire trying to make some extra cash.
I was sick! They had to put me on painkillers!
You got some kind of leverage over every single truck driver you approach.
Nuova Volsinii values the rule of law. We can go to court and present the evidence piece by piece. Then we'll see whose hands are clean.
You—you people are even scarier without weapons!
Really? I'm glad you say that, because I used to think I looked like a dork quoting laws like that.
Alright. Calm down everyone.
Enough threats. Let's just fix this. You guys go find our 'tires', and we'll handle the rest.
...How long has it been?
They found me six months ago. I reckon that they'd already dealt with a few dozen other drivers before then.
What choice did we have? Running even one shipment for them pays more than we earn in a month.
Besides, if we don't cooperate, they have plenty of ways to push us right out of this city.
After Signora Sicilia took power, she implemented all these reforms, in all of Siracusa's cities. The cunning guys adapted to the new rules far faster than we did...
Are you speaking for yourself, or for others?
Eirene, I beg you, help us!
I promise you! Once Carnevale is over, we'll never smuggle anything for those goons ever again!
You can turn me in, but please, you have to help the others. They really had no choice but to—
....The list of names.
What?
Don't try and fool me. If you guys were doing this stuff, you had to have made a list of names. Give it to me.
...At least tell me how many of you there are. Then I'll try and see what I can do.
Sommer puts down the lighter he has been clutching and begins searching through his pockets.
Sommer, why do you still have that lighter? I thought you quit smoking.
It was a gift from you. I'm used to carrying it around.
Actually, I found it lying around near the supermarket. There's no way I could afford to buy such an expensive lighter.
Do you remember? After Guri's mother passed away, you took over that supermarket, managing the business while raising your child. Then one day, you found me, homeless, at your doorstep.
You always say you just took in a delivery driver and cashier who didn't ask for a salary, but I know you actually gave me a place to stay—a home.

Later, Famiglia Tocco set their sights on us. They were just a small family, but even after we stocked their products, they wouldn't let up. They threatened us, demanding we remove their competitors' items from the shelves.
We told them we'd consider their request, but then that same night, a huge fire broke out in the supermarket, which is how Guri contracted Oripathy.
That's all in the past. Why are you bringing it up now?
I found it. Here you are.
Carmelo, Gianni, Ruggiero. What a surprise.
...Sorry.
Don't say any more, Sommer. Go to the storehouse. There is a wooden crate behind that truck that's being repaired. Bring it to me.
But that's...everything you own, isn't it?
Alright... alright.
Sommer, what you don't know is, I found that lighter right next to the scene of the supermarket fire.
We came to Nuova Volsinii because we had nothing.
Everyone makes mistakes at some point...
But I can't let you destroy all of this.
Judge Lavinia.
I'm here to see Leon.
I need to ask you all a favor—do not disclose Leon's condition to outsiders. This info is strictly need-to-know.
Sure. I understand.
It seems I'm in the right place.
Who are you looking for? This is a special ward. You can't just barge in here!
No, it's ok. Let her come in.
Well then, I'll get going. Please find me if you need anything.
...Texas.
Long time no see.
Long time no see.
I just grabbed some millefoglie and macaroni downstairs. Have a bite if you're hungry—I'll keep an eye on Leontuzzo for now.
I received your invitation.
We never heard back from you. We assumed you weren't planning to return to Nuova Volsinii.
I was busy.
Croissant is busy pushing Bison into organizing some new international project, Sora is only halfway through her tour of Siracusa, and Exusiai—who knows where she's run off to this time?
I've been managing Penguin Logistics myself this whole time, with far fewer staff and no fewer orders.
Besides, these crowded celebrations really don't excite me. I prefer just stopping by when I'm on a delivery, assuming it's on the way.
Then what brings you here today?
What can I do? I have a boss who loves a good crowd. One night, he happened to spot that invitation and had me pack my bags on the spot.
He's very interested in the 'themed party' you're hosting. He cleared his calendar, shut down 'The Ends of the Earth' for two weeks, and dragged me here all the way from Lungmen.
Relax, Lavinia. You seem so tense.
Leon, he...
I've been briefed on it.
It wasn't an accident?
The police are still investigating. I just got back from the scene... I don't think it was an accident.
Persons of interest?
Yes.
The usual suspects?
Uhuh.
Why?
On the night of the Carnevale parade, Leon was supposed to officially announce his appointment as mayor of Nuova Volsinii at the front of the procession.
It's not a strange decision, per se, but he did seem a bit too eager.
Even if he abandoned his family, he is still Bernardo Bellone's son. One year is not enough for people to forget the turmoil Bernardo once stirred in Volsinii.

Staying behind the scenes makes it easier for him to keep the government functioning. This is the safest approach. It's much better than him changing clothes, putting down his defensive staff, and stepping back into the spotlight.
Nuova Volsinii has only been around for a year and he's barely managing to hold onto his position as Acting Mayor, even with Signora Sicilia's backing.
I told him all of this already.
...Right.
Lavinia, your comms device has been flashing for a while now.
Did they find new evidence?
No, it's a message from the court. We have a case...
That's kind of tricky?
Uhuh.
Honestly, I understand why Leon was in such a hurry.
This year, the changes in Nuova Volsinii can be seen with the naked eye. Many things that once existed only in our imagination have now become a reality.
Isn't that what Signora Sicilia wanted?
Well, but it's not enough; not nearly enough. We must show our presence to promote more changes.
So I stopped trying to persuade him, but then...
You're still the same as before—no, even more naive than back then. It's kind of nice.
Texas, do you want to come with me?
To the court?
You know I'm not interested in crowded celebrations, much less official duties involving mountains of paperwork.
I'm going to stay here and ensure Leon's safety. Right now, there's no one better suited to the job, right?
Thank you, Texas.
To be honest, if you had stayed with me in Nuova Volsinii back then—

I told you before, when my friends are in trouble, I'll come to help.
I'm here, aren't I?
Freight Workers and.....wait, what? Sorry. The name you said was too long to jot down.
'Nuova Volsinii Freight Workers and Truckers Mutual Aid Association'...we need to study the workers' unions in Victoria and Columbia.
If we want to fight back against the famiglie' threats, we first need to unite amongst ourselves. There's strength in numbers.
The shipping industry is the lifeblood of every city. To date, the famiglie have established control of this industry in every other city in Siracusa.
We can't let that happen here.
In Nuova Volsinii, trucking is not controlled by gangsters. The shipping industry belongs to the drivers and every other upstanding citizen of Nuova Volsinii.
But, why me?
I used to be a truck driver for Sommer's grocery store, albeit a good one.
In Volsinii, there are only a few government employees who haven't accepted bribes from the famiglie—you could count them on two hands.
That's because...
I'm sorry, Eirene. I privately looked into your and Sommer's past. I know about that tragic incident, and I truly apologize.
I was thinking, if you simply hate the Famiglia that set fire to your supermarket, you could have easily accepted help from another family to carry out your revenge...
But you didn't do that.
It's just a little speculation on my part. Forgive me if I offend you.
Thank you... Judge Lavinia.
Eirene, regardless of whether you are the best choice to head the Truckers' Union, I believe you are a kind woman.
Please give this invitation some serious consideration, okay?
Signorina Lavazza, please wait a moment. Judge Lavinia is still holding court. She will come see you when she is finished.
A—Alright. No rush.
Those documents you're holding, are they for Judge Lavinia? I'll give them to her.
No! At least, not yet. I'll give them to her in a little while.
As Eirene speaks, she tightens her grip on the notebook in her hand.
May I ask you, that case the judge is handling, is it some dispute between the corporations or that car crash that happened last night?
Neither.
Strictly speaking, it's not a trial, but an appeal that even Judge Lavinia might not be able to handle.
The main litigant is a Nuova Volsinii civilian, but it's a unique situation.
Judge Lavinia is the Supreme Justice of Nuova Volsinii. What kind of case would she not... can I go listen in?
Of course. You can sit and listen to the proceedings while you wait for the judge.
...I'll contact the Montelupe municipal government right away and ask them to help us catch the culprit.
In the meanwhile, we will apply for Nuova Volsinii to take jurisdiction of this case.
Do you think you'll be able to do it?
I'll do everything I can. This isn't the first change of venue request we've submitted this year.
But all your previous requests were denied, right?
...Even if they deny our request, I'll push hard to ensure my colleagues in Montelupe conduct a fair trial, so that the murderer is punished.
Fair?
Judge Lavinia, we all know what's going to happen if you do that.
They'll say it was my big brother who got drunk in the bar and started this. They'll claim the mafiosi were acting in self-defense, when a terrible accident occurred.
You know that's not what happened!
He went on a business trip to Montelupe because of Carnevale and somehow offended the mafiosi there. They beat him to death in a dirty alley...
Your Honor, I heard the mafiosi have already taken the judges and court workers out to dinner. You're telling me you can make sure there's a fair trial?
I—
My brother was a good man. He never did a bad deed in his life. We were among the first citizens to apply to live in Nuova Volsinii. Everyone loved my brother.
I am very sorry.
You don't need to be sorry. This sort of thing happens in Siracusa all the time, does it not?
Your Honor, I want revenge.

I'm going to do this my way, by a method that is tried and true in our beloved Siracusa. I'm going to sell my possessions and hire an assassin or go seek refuge with another rival Famiglia that holds a grudge against them.
But if I do that, will I be violating Nuova Volsinii's laws?
Will you pass judgment on me? Will Nuova Volsinii banish me forever?
Yes, as required by the New Urban Management Act.
Nuova Volsinii's laws are very strict and very effective. However, they only apply within Nuova Volsinii's borders.
Don't you find that to be funny?
I know it sounds cruel, but that's how the laws were written. Nuova Volsinii is a very special place, and its laws are what make it special, but they are also its greatest shortcoming.
But please believe me. I will do everything I can to help you.
No, Your Honor, my brother was wrongfully killed...
I want to... revoke my Nuova Volsinii citizenship and return to Montelupe.
How could this have happened?
*sigh* There is nothing we can do about it.
Nuova Volsinii is a special city. The New Urban Management Act, both in its content and its applicability, conflicts with Siracusa's historical traditions.
In a sense, it is completely alien to the country as a whole. This is precisely why the law must stand.
This is not the first time we've seen something like this happen this year. Sorry, I should not tell you these things.
No. Thank you for sharing this with me.
Eirene, the union can't help me fix this.
This is Nuova Volsinii. Its laws bend for no one.
The sad citizen walks by them, saying nothing. Eirene cannot read any expression on his face.
Signorina Lavazza, Judge Lavinia is handling the paperwork for this case. I'll go talk to her. You wait for me here.
Judge Lavinia, are you doing OK?
I'm fine. Where's Eirene? Didn't you say she's been waiting for me for a while now?
That's weird. She was just here.
Did she tell you anything new? Did she find the missing truck?
(Shakes head)
Knowing her, she probably discovered some new evidence all of a sudden and rushed out without saying goodbye. She'll get in touch with us soon.
By the way, the police station just called.
That Certaldo guy you were asking them to find...they located him.
Texas puts the weapon aside and opens the window.
She is a little bored.
It's the rainy season, yet there isn't a cloud in the sky. The bustling pedestrians are bathed in bright sunshine—
She almost does not recognize the plate in front of her, the one she had stepped on a year ago.
After all, last year it still belonged to Volsinii and experienced constant downpours, forcing her to spend hours every day drying her tail.
If Leontuzzo was not lying in the hospital, Texas might almost believe that Siracusa is getting better.
Boss, you stuffed those vinyl records in your suitcase... aren't you afraid they'll get scratched?
Like I got a choice!
I don't like Siracusan smooth jazz. Sounds nice and fancy at first, but it might as well be tails brushin' the dirt while a pack of wolves gnaw at each other.
Carnevale, parades, concerts... there's gonna be music everywhere. I just wanna see some old friends, I ain't out to get my ears violated.
Old friends? You mean your people are going too?
We used to go all the time. Now the Siracusans are throwin' another theme party, after all these years, I mean, they definitely gonna show up.
Hey, Texas! You say you ain't into it, but you got your bags packed quicker than I did!
I didn't have much to pack.
We won't stay very long. We'll be back as soon as it's over.
Sounds like you sure somethin's definitely goin' wrong this time.
Look, Zaaro lost his game, your deal's null n' void.
You n' Siracusa, n' those old friends, they run some games I got no interest in, but everything that needed seein' to, got seen to.
The Last Texas ain't nothin' no more. You don't got no high hopes for Siracusa, so what's it matter to you if it gets better or worse?
You don't wanna say? Or you dunno?
Forget it. I mean, I'm sayin' you can stay in Lungmen to watch the house.
Texas closes the window and pulls two letters out of her pocket.
The first is an invitation from Nuova Volsinii City Hall, stamped with the court's seal and signed by Acting Mayor Leontuzzo.
The other envelope is empty. Its front side, colored purple, has a sloppy drawing of a white wolf and a bunch of grapes on the corner.
Lappland.
Eirene leaves the courthouse as if she's fleeing something.
She doesn't dare to look back on what just happened, what she experienced, or the struggles she went through.
A taxi stops right in front of her. She does not remember whether she hailed it or not.
Where you headed?
The Trucker Camp.
Uh... never heard of it.
500 Viale Alito, Torino.
It's very far from here, on the farthest plate. It's not surprising that you've never been there.
Tough day, huh?
Uhuh...
You're the gloomiest customer I've picked up since arriving in Nuova Volsinii.
Why are you so sad? The Carnevale parade is just a few days away. Everyone is happy about their new lives in this new city, no?
Speaking of which, I love this new city.
Look at these festively decorated streets, the wonderful parties, and the carefully prepared food. There are no famiglia around, and it hardly ever rains. The air, and our tails, are always dry.
Signorina, could we cut the chatter? I don't really feel like talking right now.
What is it that's got you so upset? Let's chat about it. Maybe I can help you?
I made some mistakes. No, I should say, I failed to do some things. Or rather, I trusted the wrong people... In any case, I let people down.
Whatever. There's no way to solve my problems anyway.
No no no, in Siracusa, no problems are unsolvable.
If you can't untangle the rope, you can just cut it. If you can't unlock the chest, you can smash the lock with a hammer. There is a solution for every problem.
Many times, all you need to do is change your perspective and screw up your courage. Then you'll find that all the problems will resolve themselves.
Are you speaking from experience?
More than you can imagine.
After all, before becoming a taxi driver, my main job was helping my best friend solve problems.
Now that was someone who knew how to get into trouble, tsk.
Claiming to be able to 'solve' problems—it sounds just like something the famiglie would do.
Then you've got me all wrong.
This is a new place; the old ways do not apply. Believe me.
Look, everyone's full of energy, everyone's full of love, and also, everyone's wearing a festive mask. How fun.
Sometimes I really wonder, what are hiding behind these masks?
I don't understand what you're saying.
It's simple—wearing a mask 'imposed' on you can make it hard to breathe.
Many times, you actually know what to do; the answer is right there, crystal clear. It's just that for various reasons, we refuse to face it.
But if it could make things easier for yourself... then why not?
....Sorry, but could you stop for a second?
The driver steps on the brake.

She doesn't know why, but Eirene has the feeling that, from the moment she got in the car, the driver has been waiting for this exact instruction. She even catches a glimpse of the driver's bright smile in the rearview mirror.
I'm not going to the Trucker Camp. I want to go somewhere else.
Your wish is my command.
Your timing is impeccable, Demetri. I just opened a bottle of wine.
Did you do it?
What?
Don't play dumb with me!
That car accident that has left Leon fighting for his life. Don't tell me you know nothing about it!
Calm down, Demetri. You followed Bernardo all those years, yet it seems his wisdom never rubbed off on you.
I don't like being accused of something I didn't do, and more importantly, I don't like it when a man who needs something from me carries himself like that.
Tell me everything you know.
No, no, no. That accident? I don't know any more about it than you do.
But I want to tell you a story, a story I don't tell very often, a very personal story. So, please pipe down and listen well, alright?
A long time ago, I had a 'fratello'.
We had a great relationship. We grew up together, sharing some of the dumbest moments of our youth. However, over time, a rift started to grow between us.
I started learning to manage the family business and understanding the rules of Siracusa, but my fratello, sadly, wasn't interested in any of that.
He was interested in Victorian literature, Leithanian music, and Siracusan plays. He spent most of his time in those silly theaters.
The year I first took over the family business, I found his corpse in our parking lot.
When I found him, I was enraged. I knew he was offending people, saying stupid things. I could have taken revenge, but I let it go.
Do you know why?
Because you're a cold-blooded mafioso?
Haha, I take no offense to your crudeness... but that's not quite the right answer.
It's simple. Far too many people misunderstand the meaning of famiglia.

Mafiosi are not 'duty-bound' to each other. They invest in each other and must work hard to protect those investments. Those with a common purpose and the determination to stand together can truly call themselves mafiosi.
The moment my fratello chose a different path, he was no longer an associate of my famiglia.
What are you trying to say?
I do not like to debate with other people. Business is all about finding mutual understanding.
If you truly care about avenging your fratello, you should go to City Hall and ask for their help. Here, with me, it's strictly business.
Well, that's about all the time I have now. My calendar is all booked up. I have to go meet a client.
I will handle the matter with Leon. It has nothing to do with you.
Don't let me catch you stepping over the line.
Alberto passes a row of wine racks and returns to the long tasting table.
He hates to be late, but sometimes, with certain clients, you have to make them wait for just a little bit.
You're a tough woman to meet, Signorina Lavazza.
If memory serves, I've sent several invitations to you in the past. Even getting you to show up here today was a big surprise.
I know why you're here.
You want to hand over the truck that caused the accident, along with its cargo, to me.
Yes. That's correct.
Transferring your own issue into an issue between the famiglie. That's smart.
And that is why... I have to say no.
But, Alberto, why?
This thing has nothing to do with Famiglia Saluzzo.
I'm sure you know that City Hall has been watching us closely, ever since the thing in Volsinii. Why would we want to poke the bear now?
But this is a rare opportunity for you, no?
In just a year's time, almost every major district in Nuova Volsinii will have a Venezia Trucking sales center or repair station.
But if you have evidence that shows they are smuggling contraband, you can negotiate with them, or you could hand it over to City Hall, to smooth things over.
Whichever option you choose, it will work out well for you. Am I wrong?
All you need to do is ensure that the Truckers' Union does not get involved with the events that occurred last night. That shouldn't be too hard for you.
Why did you choose the Saluzzos?
I beg your pardon?

The Medicis' investment firm, the Leotis' pharmacies, the Genoveses' sugar mills, the Roccas' steel factories... There are countless families that have entered Nuova Volsinii by way of their front businesses.
You have many different options.
To me, they're all the same.

Us truck drivers just want to find new lives in this new city. We can't make sense of it all, nor do we care to. We do not care what happened in the Grey Hall, which famiglie have gotten stronger, which have gotten weaker, etc.
Which is why my answer is so important to you.
I grew up in Sicilia. I haven't seen my parents since I was a child.
The manager of a supermarket took me in when I was about 10 years old. He already had a daughter, but he treated me like his second one.
A little over a year ago, one of the smaller, local famiglie set fire to our supermarket, over a very minor dispute. As a result, my little sister contracted Oripathy.

Later, I heard that small famiglia was completely wiped out by another larger famiglia that had entered Sicilia, simply because they refused to cede their territory. That larger famiglia's name? Saluzzo.
That is the only connection we have to the famiglie. That's why, when I got desperate, I thought of the Saluzzos.
I only have one small request.
Tell me.
I hope that in the future, the Truckers' Union will be able to do some favors for Saluzzo Vino, favors that only the union can provide.
No—no no no. I—I think you've got us wrong.
You get a bargaining chip, and in return, you help us solve a problem. That sounds like a fair trade, right?
The Truckers' Union will no longer have any dealings with Famiglia Venezia, and it won't establish any kind of relationship with Famiglia Saluzzo. You can't...
Alberto does not reply.
Eirene shakes her head forcefully. She will not let her mind get carried away.
You're running short on time, Signorina Lavazza.
You just told me a Siracusan story, a good story at that.
So, when you choose to solve things the Siracusan way, you must remember: the minute you take a seat at that table, many things are out of your control.
Alberto opens another bottle of wine. He pours the wine in the decanter and waits for it to breathe in the air, a graceful gesture.
Eirene notices that he's no longer looking at her.
Signor Saluzzo.
Speak.
Tomorrow, I'll show you where we've stashed the truck.
We're almost there. The Port of Nuova Volsinii is straight ahead.
Whoa!
What's wrong, Noemi? You've been here before.
Do you ever get a funny feeling when you come here?
What?
What I mean is, every time I come here, I don't know where I should stand. I always feel like I'm in the way...
Thanks to Columbia's nomadic city construction technology, Nuova Volsinii, combining twelve large plates, still boasts a speed far surpassing that of other cities in Siracusa.
And the Port of Nuova Volsinii, located on the city's outer plate, occupies over 1/10th of the city's usable landmass.

A faint vibration arises from beneath Noemi's feet—the sound of cargo landships from other cities docking with the support layers. It is steady and soothing, like the beating of a strong, healthy heart.

On the ground, containers stretch out like mountains, with trucks moving through the valleys between them. Cargo is being transferred from the support layers by crane for sorting, then transported by truck to various parts of the city.
Noemi still remembers the day Nuova Volsinii commenced operations. Signora Sicilia sent out a citywide message expressing her hopes and dreams for the new city.
This is the freight hub of Siracusa, the engine that shall power the nation's future.
Noemi, we are very busy today. I wanted you to wait in the office. You're the one who insisted on following me here.
I may still be in training, but I can help out. Maybe I can even crack the case.
Well, I only started in this job three months before you. Neither of us is all that experienced.
Papa wants me to settle into this job quickly. It's a decent profession, and serving as a police officer means I'll be able to protect my family's pizzeria.
OK. OK. You already said all of that stuff to us on your first day on the job. Come on, kid. Don't you have any tact at all?
Uhh... hehe.
Alright. Let's get back to work.
There are a total of fifty-six repair stations across the city's twelve plates, and the other officers are checking them one by one. We've already dispatched a unit to the Trucker Camp as well.
The Port of Nuova Volsinii is the last place left. Almost all of the box trucks conduct business at this port.
But finding one truck in this huge place? It's searching for a needle in a haystack!
A fitting analogy, but a bit exaggerated. We've got to get a move on, or we'll be here past sundown.
Let's start here. You go east, and I'll go west. Remember, we're looking for trucks with any signs of damage. Don't overlook a single dent, ding, scrape, or scratch.
Got it!
Signs of damage... signs of damage...
How are there this many trucks in this city?
Noemi moves through the sea of vehicles. Busy trucker drivers and workers occasionally pass by, but no one spares her a second glance, or thought.
This isn't right. If someone had been in an accident, why would they stash the damaged truck in such a conspicuous place?
Wait. What are those tarps covering up over there?
Have you found anything on your end?
Not yet. What's wrong? Did you get lost already? I told you to have your coffee this morning.
I don't drink coffee. It's too bitter.
Not when you dunk in a donut or two...
C'mon, let's stay focused on the task at hand. I know the truck we're looking for is hidden around here somewhere! Get over here, quickly!
You hid the truck in the Port of Nuova Volsinii?
My guy picked up the goods here. He was only halfway out of the city when the accident happened.
It happened so suddenly. He was too afraid to drive the truck over to Famiglia Venezia, or back to the Trucker Camp.
The truck's cabin got all busted up. The damage was far too noticeable, so he had to turn back. He drove back to this port, the place he knows best.
Where exactly is the truck?
Over there.
That's...
The place where all the floats are parked.
Floats?
These giant floats are modeled after the mythical beasts from Siracusan legends. They're going to be the big surprise of the Carnevale. City Hall spent nearly six months preparing them.
They will lead the Carnevale parade across the central avenue and through the entire city. Under the gaze of all the citizens, they will bring the festive atmosphere to its peak.
The port officials have covered them with a tarp, so as not to ruin the surprise.
The driver hid the truck under the tarp?
Yes. Sommer disguised it as one of the floats.
I'm not sure exactly which truck it is. Wait for me here. I'll go talk to the dispatcher.
Why are all the floats moving all of a sudden?
Signorina Lavazza, are you going to check each and every float? You don't think that's a bit much?
No. I'm not going to...
The crane's mechanical arm tears away the tarps, and the Carnevale floats begin rolling out, one by one.
The bizarre, gigantic beasts are exposed to the daylight, starting the grand parade ahead of schedule.
Before Eirene can even react, thick black smoke begins to rise at the end of the procession.
As if unnerved by the sunlight, one of the floats bursts into flames.

It is the 'wolf,' one of the legendary deities born of the wilderness. When the twin moons are high, the wolves sneak into Siracusans' dreams, invoking their primal nature and teaching them to survive.
This wolf is a symbol of Siracusa, its mysterious legends, its long, bloody history, and the limitless future that this new city holds.
But now, the 'deity' is bathed in flames, and Siracusa's future is slowly turning to ash.
What's going on?
Noemi, tell the workers to put out the fire, now!

I'm on it!
Wait. Look at that float. Doesn't it look like it's been in an accident?
You mean, this is the truck we've been looking for, the one that hit the Acting Mayor?
That fire is getting out of control. If we wait for them to put it out, there'll be nothing left.
You think this fire was accidental?
I just think... hiding the truck among the parade floats, then setting it on fire, all just to destroy the evidence? That's both too complicated and blatant.
Or maybe... that's what they want.
It's a message, a provocation.
The fire completely engulfs the float, its flickering flames making it appear as though it were alive. The onlookers can almost hear the towering wolf, ablaze, roaring in the inferno.
A gust of wind blows through, carrying the thick smoke, which spreads like dark clouds, enveloping the entire city.
Suddenly, Noemi thinks to herself, 'maybe being a police officer is harder than it looks.'
What a huge fire... It's hard to make out whose likeness the burned wolf was modeled after.
You? Me? Zaaro or Agnese?
Not any one of us.
Humans can never imagine something beyond their cognition, and they are too fragile, always cowed by fear...
Perhaps to them, all 'uncivilized' lifeforms are like this—baring their teeth and full of menace.
Regardless of how they created that image, they clearly put a lot of effort into it. It's a shame that it was burned like this.
Your wolf pup isn't around. There's no need to act so benevolent.
She doesn't know your identity, nor the truth behind the conflict, yet she almost helped you win this round of the game.
There's no more shameless Signora dei Lupi than you, Caesar.
No.
Projekt Red, my dear girl, her hunting skills were subpar, much less so under the influence of humans.
Victory was far beyond her grasp.
Zaaro's Fang was not killed by her. Agnese's Fang has been weakened, but still lives.
And you, Vargo.
In the first round of the game, your wolf pup hid all too well. Red couldn't even pick up his scent.
I remember you once told me that this was the most satisfying choice you've made among our countless rounds of games.
Is your wolf pup lying low, waiting for the others to fight to the death so it can jump out of hiding and claim victory?
The wilderness has always been this way, has it not?
*sigh* Vargo, do you really think the others can't see through your disguise?
What if I already know where your wolf pup is hiding?

lines.st: Every hunter runs into a wolf before they go into the wastes.

The wolf says: Mulberry wood makes sturdy bows, and stones can be sharpened into deadly blades; but you must shed the clothes on you to survive, for it is the only thing that does not belong in the barrens.
No one would believe it.
So the hunter who kept his long coat on... was caught by tree roots protruding into the corner of the coat, ensnared by vines so tightly he could not move until he heard the beasts encircling him.

Another hunter covered himself head-to-toe in armor... was washed away by the river current when the water rushed into the tiny gaps between the metal plates, dragged down into the mud on the riverbed.
Only one hunter took the wolf's advice, and received a gift from it. The wolf tore off its own pelt, and placed it on the hunter.
The fur fit him so well, as if he was born with it. It helped the hunter survive countless dangers unscathed.
The pelt was so smooth and clean. When the hunter left the wilderness, not a speck of blood could be found on it.
And the hunter never took it off—

lines.x: Even the hunter's descendants had a custom of wearing clothing so fitting yet comfortable... and slowly, it evolved into the suit you commonly see on Siracusans today.
...Nonno, you've been telling me this story from when I was a baby, and I still don't get it. I've grown up and you're still telling it.
Luchino, I'm nearly done with these two suits. We just need to sew on these buttons.
You accepted this order on your own accord, so we must be meticulous in our work, no matter who the client is.
Go fetch the buttons from the back. You won't miss them, they have the Saluzzo's emblem.
Shouldn't I first bring them to Signorina Lappland for a fitting, and then we adjust the clothes afterward? As you taught me—
It's fine. That Saluzzo signorina doesn't need it, I'll decide if they need any alterations.
But she didn't request to skip the fitting...
I know what's on your mind, boy.
Sometimes our clients request complicated designs, sometimes they don't even have a picture of it in their head, but it's not our place to probe too much.
Remember, Luchino: Tailor de Montano did not gain our reputation purely from our precision tailoring.
People and materials are the same—know them before making any move.
Go get those buttons. It's time to finish this order.
Oh... okay.
Luchino looks at the two beautiful sets of attire. They are completely different to any suit his grandfather has ever made.
A magnum opus to be sure, worthy of the 'Made by de Montano' labels. He just wishes to be a part of that.
Luchino subconsciously pats his pocket. Inside are a few buttons he privately made himself, also engraved with the Saluzzo emblem.
I'll get them at once, Nonno.
...*sigh*
I kept you waiting outside for quite long, didn't I? Please come in.
You can't delay Signor Antonio's commission any longer, Umberto.
The old Don's about to arrive, if you start now you can still finish it in time before the Carnevale.
Push back your other commissions and come with me to Venezia Trucking. We won't take too much of your time.
My current commission is also one I can't disappoint.
Nonno! I heard someone come in!
Umberto, you don't actually have to make those measurements yourself.
Wanna go meet a real somebody, ragazzo?
Huh? A—A somebody? Nonno, I—
Luchino, give me the buttons.
Y—Yes!
Here.
The young tailor nervously looks at his nonno's face.
Not bad.
But not good enough for these two outfits, Luchino.
B—But I compared them so many times! They're practically the same!
Once you sew enough buttons, you'll be able to tell if they'll fit or not just by touching them. Go fetch mine.
Here, here! All here!
I—I just wanted to help you out!
Umberto, I think you're being too hard on the kid, personally.
Keep practicing, Luchino.
Yes, practice. I'll be good and get back to it once I bring back your medicine!
Signore, surely you can tell this boy is too reckless.
...You amuse me, Umberto.
Apologies, but my recent Carnevale commissions leave me no time at all to go with you.
I promise you, I'll personally go over to apologize once I'm done here.
Of course, I'll pay for any subsequent expenses as well.
You really think Signor Antonio cares about all that? All I care about, is that I can enjoy the Carnevale in peace, Umberto—
Umberto de Montano. Figured you'd be here.

lines.st: (The Vulpo Returns Home)

lines.x: You shouldn't be here, Zaaro.
You lost your Fang and this round, so you ought to return to the barrens and wait for the next round to begin.
Calm down, Caesar.
I know you have the best odds of being the alpha of this round, but there's no way I can just sit and wait.
I smell a strong human scent on you. Have you already picked up a new toy?
If you did, then why did you come find me? Were you hoping I console you, Zaaro?
I'm fed up with how you deceive and trick your little pup, Grandma.
I summoned you here to share an interesting secret—where the last unidentified Fang is hiding.
You're out of the game, yet you still try to interfere?
Outsiders make the most eager audience.
Caesar, the bloody stench beneath your merciful appearance and your thirst for victory... I've always been able to sniff them out despite your efforts.
Besides, I don't care if you're interested in my information or not. I'm merely relishing it.
Your pup has a keen sense of smell. A pity our unidentified Fang has spent decades cleaning the scent of wolf off himself.
Umberto de Montano. A pathetic tailor.
Zaaro, I guarantee you that when this round ends, you'll savor the pain of our bite.
Caesar, your anger is delicious as ever.
Unfortunately, I'm not even the first wolf to challenge our rules.
A Vulpo? You a tourist? Sorry, Tailor de Montano's all stuffed up with orders right now, so commissions are closed.
You're the boss here?
Who cares if I am or not—
He does not dare to finish his sentence.
Because he has just seen the blade in her hands.
Right. How long has it been since anyone in this city walked around freely with a weapon in their hand?
A smile remains on the woman's face, but he has a feeling: one more word from him and her blade will surely stab through his throat, even if they are in Nuova Volsinii right now.
*cough* Signora, this man is an employee of the Venezia Trucking.
Trucking? The famiglia sure has changed a lot since I left.
(A Vulpo... the scar on her face... this suit, and that blade—)
(The homicidal lunatic that defected and left the famiglia... but six years ago, didn't she already...?)
You should go, signore. Please send Signor Antonio my regards. Of course, my prior promise to you remains true.
Tch... You... I... cazzo.
(Grazie, Umberto.)
Signora Venezia, you don't seem to have changed much.
You know me?
Your photos were always on the newspapers. Dominated the headlines for a long time.
I thought you would have informed your family first about your return to Siracusa.
I left them many years ago—I came here looking for you.
Yet you're not here to kill me. There's a familiar scent on you.
Vargo?
Yes. He found me in Higashi through his connections, and we made a deal.
Are you sure you want to have our conversation while you bask in the sun and roll around the red leaves?
(Big yawn)
How else?
I wouldn't have bothered helping that wolf out if not for the tiny bit of friendship we have.
So you want me to return to Siracusa... and act as a bodyguard for someone?
To 'solve a problem'.
Do that, and you'll know the truth you've been chasing after.
Why you couldn't find the culprit who hurt your daughter despite all the heads you took, and who exactly that is.
Are you serious? You're not making up an excuse to chase me away from this place?
Hah. Your choice to believe it or not.
Other than him, I should be the only one in this entire shrine who doesn't hate you, Ingrid.
You raid the place whenever you come in here just to see him, and that makes me quite happy. Life at the shrine would be so dull otherwise.
To be honest, I kind of want to follow you back to Siracusa for a little walk.
You're talking about a deep swamp... Your stubbornness and hobby of rolling about in the grass won't like it there.
Tell him I'm going back.
Not telling him yourself?
It's for Lisa, he'll understand.
After I came back to Siracusa, I also met with the Signore dei Lupi. You, Umberto de Montano, I honestly could not have imagined he really chose someone like you if he hadn't stated your very name...
A humble tailor like myself?
Mhm.
I don't know how Vargo described me to you, but we do subtly differ in our views on some matters.
You don't look so good, Umberto.
Vargo told me the wolves always choose humans with some sort of impressive talent, but your health is quite poor.
That ragazzo who just left, he's your...
Luchino is a bright boy, but he just can't accept the fact that time is unbeatable.
I'm old, Ingrid. Simple as that.
Is Vargo well?
I can't tell with them... Did he not come to you?
We haven't seen each other for many years, I thought he'd long abandoned me.
Maybe he just didn't want to taint you with his scent and risk putting you in danger. That's my guess.
...Is that so?
To be honest, I've no interest in finding out what happened between you and Vargo.
I'm just here to solve a problem for you.
He told me how to distinguish the other Fangs. I was thinking, why not take the first move and give them hell instead?
What do you think?
It seems you can't refuse the conditions he gave you.
But this swamp is difficult to leave, once you make even just half a step into the mud.
Uh... I'll meet Signor Alberto soon. Should I explain the suit design in detail?
—I can't, Nonno will get mad if he finds out.
But if Signor Alberto asks me about it, surely I can't refuse to answer.
Becoming acquaintances with the don of Famiglia Saluzzo could be good for us...
I might even manage to get better medicine for Nonno. Or maybe I could even bring him to Columbia to see even better doctors.
Huff. Stop spiraling, Luchino.
The address from Signorina Lappland... it was this way, if I'm not wrong—
Uh... A truck?
Why is it so fast?! It'll hit the—
Hey!!
Luchino hugs the intricate box containing the suits as he stands on the street corner, and witnesses the accident happen.
A truck fully loaded with goods suddenly crashes into a car that was just about to turn at the intersection.
Glass shards scatter all over, the vehicle frames wrecked out of shape. A tire pops out from the truck.
...H—Help... Help them out!
Merda, everyone's gone to the main street for the show...
Hey, just hold on in there! I'll get you out—
Um, what are these... hidden inside the tires?
Here, Gambino! The car we had our sights on!
That was just a damn coffee break, what the... We're done for!
We were unlucky enough getting assigned the dirty work, with none of the credit...
And now something just had to happen to the goods under our eyes—
