Much worse than that, I'm afraid. Look at the car that got hit. Look familiar to you?
Why do you even care who the hell got hit—
Wait. That's. Leontuzzo's...
I gotta say, what you said about going back to Lungmen wasn't so terrible after all.
Let's get outta here before the cops and boss find out. We'll slowly settle the accounts between us after we're back there.
We toiled to get a sliver of hope here. There's no way we can just throw in the towel now, don't forget why we came here to Nuova Volsinii, why we joined Venezia Trucking!
No Signora Sicilia, no Grey Hall, and no restrictions from the other famiglie.
Who knows, we might even manage to make up a famiglia of our own, Gambino.
See the kid across the street with the box? He's the only witness—maybe he caught a glimpse of the goods stashed inside the truck too?
...I got you. Up to us to decide if he saw anything. You're such a scumbag, Capone.
Just trying to keep myself alive, and leave a good impression on the boss at the same time.
Let's go. Take him, and tell them we got the sole witness with us.
And the goods...?
That's for the recipient to think about. Don't forget, the boss stressed again and again we're just cover.
As for Leontuzzo... Hmph, hope he's fine.
Andiamo. We gotta get that kid before someone else does.
Uh, you are here to help?
Over here, someone's still inside the car—
Lights out.
I'll carry him.
The box he was carrying...
Take it with us. Don't leave any clues behind.
You sure no one saw us?
Obviously. The first thing I did here was to survey the surrounding area.
Go.
'CLOSED'.
The cafe is quiet, save for the occasional electrical 'buzzing' from the shop sign at the entrance.
'Closed for three days. Customers new and old, do vote for my daughter Ophelia at the show contest—A week of free coffee for everyone if she wins the right to light up the parade cars on Carnevale!'
A smell. It's strong.
I know you ran in here.
Sparks fly when a passing arrow strikes the dagger in Projekt Red's hand, and the impact numbs her hand slightly.

Quick reflexes. No wonder I'm not done hunting yet!
But I've got you now—
Huh?
I caught you.
Ow! If you weren't a Fang, I wanted to invite you to the pack.
Too bad Agnese wouldn't agree.
Grandma is waiting for me. I must hunt you... and then the last one.
Huh? Having second thoughts?
...No. Not anymore. I don't want to let Kal'tsit down. Grandma even more.
Best not get too close to a hunter!
Lunacub turns around as she shoots an arrow, and quickly slams into a window, shattering it and escaping into the streets.
Don't move. I won't miss this time for sure.
I need to confirm one thing with you before I drag you back to Agnese—
I see Vargo was telling me the truth.
The wolf pack is restless.
I spent quite the effort to find you two.
Though the rest will be simple to do.
You have a smell that Agnese hates, but you're not a Fang.
You know her, Projekt Red? Just show yourself, I won't shoot on sight.
No.
But her blade. Sharper than mine.
...For real?
There's no telling who'll win like this.
I know.
I will reach her first.
The red figure dashes towards Ingrid, and Lunacub quickly turns to aim at the blade-wielding Vulpo.
Not before my arrow does.
Three shots, and I'll have her cornered.
Behind you.
Trying to distract me? Tsk.
I've handled tougher fights in Siracusa with many more people on the other end.
But I'm always the last one standing. No exceptions.
Three arrows lodge into her sword sheath.
Don't treat humans like beasts. Your hunting skills won't work on me.
You can certainly continue to try. Same goes for you, 'Projekt Red'.
This is not over yet.
My blade... not sharp enough...
Come.
You can't hide from me forever. I won't wait any longer for Vargo's answers.
Are the police here actually working? I should go.
The accident, the Fangs, and the Famiglia Venezia's shadow...
I see Siracusa is welcoming me back with even bigger surprises.
I recommend the 'Red Tongue Sour'. They say it's made like how the Sami do.
Are you mad, Mane? Ordering cocktails at a winery? We'll be laughed out of here!
*ahem* Didn't we taste a similar looking one when we were in Columbia last month?
I just wanted to reminisce about our honeymoon a little, tesoro.
(Remember our undercover classes at the police academy. Sometimes we have to be conspicuous to cover the others.)
(The judge needs more time to clear bystanders from here.)
(...Are you sure you didn't pull that just because you wanted to drink it?)
I swear, tesoro!
Ssh..! Softer, Mane!
I thought his Nuova Volsinii would be much more lenient.
Signorina, why not have a drink on me? A cocktail, of course.
Let's just say it's a local custom.
(Mane, he's here...)
Signore, thank you for the generosity, but it's not your place to offer my—
I think there's been a misunderstanding. My offer was not directed at you, officer.
It's been a while, Lavinia.
Care for a drink? You've been standing in that dim corner for a long while.
I was just wondering how long it'd take before you'd pay and leave.
I don't have to worry about the bill here. The Saluzzos never treat their important guests poorly.
Besides, Alberto informed all of his wineries in advance. 'Feel free to taste the wines as you please', were his words.
I never knew you were on such cordial terms with the Saluzzos. What are you and Alberto doing together? Surely you can't be working as a bartender here?
Lavinia, I didn't come here to be interrogated.
I'm deeply concerned for Leon. That's the only reason for our meeting tonight.
'He's still in a coma', 'He's still in a critical condition'... what kind of answer are you expecting from me? Or should I say, what kind of answer are you afraid of?
The fact that you are here means his condition isn't that bad.
...I still can't meet him?
You don't have to ask a question you already know the answer to.
Leon is mi fratello. I just hope he's safe and sound.
A fratello to you? Or Bellone's traitor?
Lavinia, everyone who stayed in the Bellones has valid reasons for hating him.
I hate him too. I don't agree with his choice and how he does things today, but I wouldn't deal with him in such a despicable way.
That won't serve as valid testimony to clear you from suspicion.
Leon got into the accident right before he was supposed to meet you, and you were inside a restaurant near the scene. There is reason to suspect you.
Then, Your Honor, what will you do? Haul me into your court and slowly interrogate the truth from me? I know many of the 'customers' here today are in fact officers.
...I won't arrest you without decisive evidence. This is how the law operates, we won't act according to the 'rules' of the famiglie.
But my suspicion towards you remains.
I'll get to the bottom of this, no matter if you're innocent or not, no matter who was involved. I will get to the truth.
Demetri looks at Lavinia's determined eyes, and sighs.
You haven't changed at all, Lavinia. Still the same as your newbie judge days, daring question a famiglia don.
I never liked you that much, but... thank you for taking care of Leon for me.
Also, I still have to say: I'll also investigate Leon's accident... I want to know who the culprit is too.
Demetri fetches a nearby wine bottle and gently pulls the cork open.
The liquid swishes within. Only half remains.
Someone gifted me this bottle. I had planned to invite Leon to try it. He should have been the one sitting here...
Anyway, this glass is on me.
Can I really not see him, Lavinia?
He leans on the counter, waiting for her answer.
But the judge looks at the glass of red wine and says nothing.
Fine. No point for me to remain here.
Now would you guys excuse me?
Or are you planning to detain me under some special procedures for the famiglie under the New Urban Management Act?
There's no more famiglia here, Demetri.
...So I guess I'll go?
As you please.
As laid-back as ever, Venezia senior.
Do your men know their don is currently sitting in an ordinary restaurant right now, while they're waiting to welcome him at the port?
I just missed the food here. I didn't expect you'd be the first face to greet me.
Haha. Come, have a seat, my friend.
This restaurant isn't as ordinary as you think. I found the chef here from Sicilia. Makes the most authentic bolognese, you should try some.
The younger ones love talking about the future, which isn't a bad thing. However, some traditions shouldn't be abandoned, especially when it comes to Siracusan cuisine.
Anyone who tried innovating a new Saluzzo wine making method would surely be buried six feet under in the vineyard for fertilizer.
Hmph. That's right.
This bottle of noble rot wine is the first of our new collection. Take it as a welcome gift.
Hm... 1069. A good year.
I can find all of Saluzzo Vino's vintages and subsidiary brands on my rack, but it's been a long time since I've received a bottle from you personally...
We haven't been in contact much, Alberto.
Just a decade.
The Morettis ignored the Dodici Famiglie's ban on competing with the other famiglie to monopolize the Orignium trade, and recklessly tried to wipe us out...
Famiglia Venezia stepped in as a mediator back then, you are certainly worthy of our respect.
'Recklessly tried to wipe us out'—Maybe I'm just old and my memory's failing me...
But as I recall, it was the Morettis who vanished from Siracusa.
You swallowed all their Originium businesses, secured your seat in the Grey Hall, and that granted the Saluzzos the current status in Siracusa today.
I'm sure you've experienced more like this than I have.
Our sparse communication has been a boon to us. No one knows what Signora Sicilia would think of our meeting here.
Really?
She banned Carnevale herself, and yet it's come back from the dead... perhaps she is much more tolerant of Siracusa than we or everyone else in the Grey Hall could imagine.
Hmph.
A brand new city celebrating one of the oldest festivals. Surely no one would miss attending?
The young ones at city hall are simply doing this to declare that Siracusa will change inside-out... from here.
I just thought of the late Bernardo.
...A self-righteous fool.
Not very nice to badmouth a friend who's no longer here.
I saw trucks going in and out while the police directed traffic, and I couldn't even tell a single family member from the unmasked pedestrians...
Even the weather here is better than the other Siracusan cities.
To be honest, the famiglie might not disappear the way Bernardo wanted... but I was finding it hard to believe this was still a Siracusan city until you showed up.
The Venezias have integrated into the city well, senior.

From the city plate's construction work to the founding of the new government, your famiglia never showed up even once. Then Nuova Volsinii officially started operating, and the Venezias went quickly to work.
Anton was in charge of all that.
Your son-in-law is indeed quite capable.
Venezia Trucking has never flouted any of the New Urban Management Act rules, and even works with city hall to investigate illegal acts by the other famiglie.
The younger ones are always more eager to get with the times than we are.
But time is unable to sweep the famiglie's stain away. You know better than me what your vehicle company is doing under the table.
I suppose that's enough gossip for today. Alberto, I've been wondering when you'd finally start talking about the business at hand.
You knew what I was going to talk about?
Not many new stories in Siracusa these days.
No matter where you heard it from or whatever it is you're planning, this old man might not be able to do much.
Be it the city hall or Anton, be it a threat or offer to collaborate, I have nothing to do with it all.
Times have changed. I can't say if I like it or not, but I definitely can't stop progress. So I thought, why not leave it to the younger generation instead.
Not afraid they would screw up?
Why not wait and see?
The Venezias climbed to our current position in just three generations, and it was not because of the older generations sticking with their views.
We have Vulpos, Perros, and Lupos with no blood relations. It is precisely because we accept anyone with ideas with open arms that we are what we are today.
What I'm doing now is no different than what the old don did.
Hmph.
I came here just to enjoy the festival—but I'm happy you were the first person I saw.
But old men like us ultimately need to act with decency.
'Decency'. I see it's still a favorite in your dictionary.
That is why we Venezias have managed to stick together to this day.
The famiglia's decency frames what we should do or can't do. What say you, Alberto?
Anyone else and I would have called beastshit. But you—
I can only admit that it's 'truly valuable in Siracusa'.
Mind getting me some spaghetti bolognese?
You'd best open this noble wine now instead of bringing it back to Montelupe.
I'm craving some myself after seeing you enjoy your meal.
After all, I fear a chance for a leisurely meal like this will be quite rare in future.
Umberto never has to look at the clock when working.
He silently counts the number of times the sewing machine's needle bobbed up and down. This is his favorite way of counting time after bidding farewell to his past life.
But now, he dreads the sound of the needle.
The old man's eyes are puffy from his long excruciating day, but he forces himself to keep on sewing to keep it together.
Is that you, Luchino?
Signor Umberto?
Oh, it's you, Anna...
Yes, I'm here to collect the suit I ordered. I formally start at city hall tomorrow!
Ah, is Luchino out?
He... had something to do.
Wait here while I get your suit.
How is it?
It, it fits perfectly! Grazie mille!
No need, you paid me for this.
But you used an even better material for it...
You waited so long for city hall to get back to you, and worked hard for the position too. Of course you deserve a better suit.
Treat it as a greeting gift from us when we moved here. Neighbors should take care of each other.
We're so lucky to have you as our tailor, Signore.
Right, I know Luchino hates sitting around, and I wanted to give him this ticket to the Carnevale show contest rehearsals. It's a good seat.
Could you give it to him for me?
Haha, everyone knows you only want the best for the boy, but sometimes it's good to let him live a little.
He should enjoy his life sometimes, right?
...Grazie, I'll pass it on to him.
I gotta tell you, your boy has a keen eye.
When I was here last month queuing for measurements, he was excitedly telling me which customers were important government officials or famiglia guys.
He's so proud you're making clothes for those high-ups.
A clever boy indeed. But most of our customers—most people who look for a tailor are actually just regular civilians.
It's rare they get a reason to come here. A rare job opportunity, a beautiful wedding, or even a dignified funeral.
Everyone deserves the decency of tailoring, and we at Tailor de Montano hope to operate for a long time on this basis.
I admire you, Signor Umberto. I'm sure Luchino will be an excellent tailor in future with a great teacher like you.

lines.st: 'Luchino, hold onto my hand tight.'
'Do you want to live together with me from today onwards?'
The child had just barely turned three when Umberto found him in a farm with mud all over his face.
'Your eyes are like his... like my son.'
'You... cry...?'
The curious child extends his hand to wipe away the tears from Umberto's eyes, but leaves a filthy gray mark instead.
'What happened to your papa, Luchino, I won't allow it ever again—'

lines.x: ...You look haggard, signore. Need help?
Just age getting to me. Not getting enough rest lately.
Anna, tomorrow's your first day at work. Your brand new suit won't do all the work of impressing your colleagues. Have an early rest... Oh, feel free to get it ironed here anytime.
I... will close early today and get some rest.
Alright. Ciao, Signor Umberto. Find us anytime if you need anything.
Umberto holds the ticket in hand, and slumps before his sewing machine. He feels powerless.
After a brief silence, he dials a number.
I know there's no cause to contact you, but this is the only way I have left.
Luchino's been missing for a whole day. I can't go to the police, I'm afraid that the boy might have gotten himself mixed up in some mob matter—
If you're listening, I beg of you—Help me find him... He's the only thing this old man is concerned about... Please, Signora Venezia.
Your Honor, Demetri is gone. Should we pack up as well?
Just hold on.
Your face is kinda red, did you drink too much...?
Tell the others to continue tracking the vehicle responsible for the accident while we continue investigating the suspects.
It'll be hard, pinning someone down this way...
Indeed. Demetri, Alberto, and a 'missing driver'... We have many suspects, but I think there are other ulterior motives behind this incident.
Ever since Nuova Volsinii was founded, there have been many eyes on us—
Like that customer who hasn't left yet.
Lavinia looks at a corner.
The winery lights are bright, but the wine racks cast a shadow in the corner, with a Vulpo sitting in the dark within.
Her bottle is already half gone, and she has been keeping her distance from everyone else the whole time. So much so that no one has noticed her blade blatantly left on the table.
So that's why you didn't call it a day yet...
Judge Lavinia, yes?
To be honest, your actions today—and your scatterbrained officers are giving me a headache.
I had planned to pry any interesting info from talkative drunkards here, but it turns out—
You're the only one left I can count on.
How do you know my name, and—
Why don't you have a seat first?
Sorry, I had no intentions to eavesdrop on your conversation earlier. But a judge and a famiglia don? A classic combination not to be missed.
The Vulpo points at her ridiculously large ears and gives a wry smile.
What can I say? I don't know if a good hearing really helps.
Ah, right. Ingrid—my name.
Now we're even.
You've been staring at my blade for a long time—just a prop. I can register it at city hall if needed.
I've been away from Siracusa for too long and haven't gotten used to the new rules yet.
...Ingrid.
I think you know very well these promising officers around us won't be much help.
Are they all under you? Does Nuova Volsinii have the police and judiciary together in the same unit?
We make do with what we have in these times. They're also serious about their work.
We'll gladly escort you over to city hall for identity registration, Signora Venezia.
Sure, but after I'm done with my business.
I won't take too long, Lavinia... today I'm just a visitor doing some visiting.
Ingrid sips a mouthful of wine, and lets Lavinia inspect her blade.
You're here for Carnevale?
For my daughter. She was hurt.
Oripathy. An accident.
Any other questions? I'll be happy to answer.
Signora Venezia. I hope you don't get the wrong idea, but this isn't an interrogation.
It's alright. I've crossed paths with judges before. I have no particular opinions on the profession.
It's not your determination as a judge to crack the case, but your kind concern for a patient that has won my respect.
You know something about the accident. Even though we've been keeping it confidential.
I already told you about my sharp ears, Lavinia.
So who is 'still in a coma'? A relative? An important friend?
Sorry, but I'm not answering that.
Hmm, I see accidents do come easy in Siracusa.
No need to be so vigilant, Lavinia. I can guarantee you my return here has no plans to involve myself in any famiglia affairs.
Hm... Perhaps we may even have a chance to exchange information.
The two are interrupted by the sound of the winery door opening. The new addition stands at the entrance until the sommelier quickly steps up and humbly takes his coat.
Phew. Signora Venezia, I knew you'd be here.
Wasn't easy tracking you down... I told the old don about our run-in at de Montano, and he specifically told me he wanted to see—
Um...
The new guest looks in Lavinia's direction and shuts his mouth.
Lavinia and Ingrid stare at the flustered guest in unison, and the surrounding officers pretending to be drunks follow suit.
Signore, we've decanted your wine for you. Allow me to show you the way.
Uh, no, it's fine. I just remembered something.
Oh, please wait just a moment while I fetch you a bottle of our winery's newest—
My tuskbeast back home's giving birth today! I shouldn't miss it!
Lavinia watches him hurriedly grab his coat back from the sommelier. He puts it back on, and even tips his hat off to her before he leaves.
Early this year, that man was jailed for three months for attempting some trouble. I didn't think I'd see him out so soon.
Of course, he absolutely did it on his own and had 'no relations at all' with Venezia Trucking.
Your Honor, I'm starting to think if I should hold you in higher esteem.
The mafiosi were never this afraid of a judge in the old Siracusa.
Well, Signora Venezia, I actually prefer your attitude—'No particular opinions on the profession.'
What Siracusa should revere is the law itself, and not certain individuals.
Haha, a novel idea—though I fear I must get going now.
To meet with your former don?
I have no obligation to report every step of my trip to a judge, but I don't wish to complicate matters for you either, Lavinia.
As I said, I have no intentions of involving myself with famiglia matters.
I have more pressing things to attend to, but I'll make sure I get them done before the identity registration deadline.
Ah, you should cut down on the alcohol too.
You can't hold your liquor well at all.
Lavinia watches the Vulpo leave, and directs the eager officers with her eyes to stay put.
She knows what all of you look like, so let's leave it to the others.
Investigate Venezia Trucking, it seems their old don is already in town.
Leon... I'm afraid the situation's getting even more troublesome.
Your Honor, the tech department guys just sent over some data from the scene. We need you to review it—
Wasn't that area's monitoring system broken?
It's not from that. A private car that broke down was parked in the area for several days and its dash cam happened to record something.
A suspicious individual was spotted in an alley. Look, this silhouette looks like a Vulpo, wielding a blade...
What's the matter?
She was just here.
Judge Lavinia, the Vulpo was just sighted in this area.
She's a sharp one. Practically stays in a new place every day. We investigated every place she's been and only figured out her approximate location.
Ingrid Venezia.
Back in law school, my jurisprudence professor mentioned the name once, but it didn't even come to my mind last night.
She told us Siracusa's cruel and conflictive atmosphere stems not from the local races here, but originates from the land itself...
Her rationale for how a gentle Vulpo could turn into a 'Vulpo even more ruthless than a wolf' after coming to Siracusa—and our laws are to rein in this sort of inevitable evolution.
Ingrid has more than twenty criminal records spread out between five nomadic city courts, each one corresponding to a spike in the Venezias' influence in the area...
The famiglia has also always tried their best to keep her away from any revenge killings or court trials.
Rumor has it that Venezia senior even favored her more than his actual daughter.
But in 1094, Ingrid suddenly left the famiglia.
Left?
More accurately, she defected.

Venezia Trucking expanded their business to Terni then. Famiglia Tancredi was actively provoking the other side so they could plunder the local alloy deposits. The conflict intensified and eventually escalated into a gang war.
The two Grey Hall famiglie were practically locked in a cycle of violence, and they even tried to put a hit out on Venezia senior...
Ingrid went into a killing spree, taking down more than ten of the Tancredis' made men.
Strange thing is, when Signora Sicilia personally came down and the two famiglie finally managed to start negotiations... Ingrid ignored the don's order to stop and continued her slaughter.
I saw the records. The stuff she did... freaks me out even now.
Motive?
Unclear.
Anyway, Ingrid basically challenged Signora Sicilia's authority with her actions, but vanished without a trace after that...
The entire Famiglia Venezia was blamed for this, and they almost met the same fate as Famiglia Texas.
The young judge imagines the Vulpo taking lives in the city, along with the smile that never left her face while they were at the winery yesterday.
Judge?
A top killer from a famiglia, with many murder cases under her hands, suddenly appearing in Nuova Volsinii... right in time for Carnevale. We can't let her move as she pleases.
You mean...
The famiglie here have all passed their background checks. Every member and weapon is registered and strictly regulated...
Someone like Ingrid is likely to act as another person's blade, regardless of whether she knows it or not.
We need to capture her first at all costs.
Yes. But... this area is huge. We still haven't managed to nail down her exact location yet.
If the information about her and her daughter is accurate, I'm sure she's headed towards a certain place.
Lavinia looks toward an inconspicuous sign not far away from them—'Rhodes Island Branch Office'.
*sigh* The shop I often go to source materials is about to close for Carnevale.
We'll need to update our reserve count on the terminal in advance.
How's Vigil's doing? The landship dispatched a doctor to the hospital, but we haven't gotten any news since.
Dunno. The support mission this time has a high confidentiality level, and I don't have enough clearance to check his latest data.
I looked for Penance earlier, and she seems hesitant to share too much to us.
Let's trust our colleagues. We just need to complete our support logistical work.
Erm... There's a new notice on PRTS: 'Approved: Application to increase security level of Nuova Volsinii branch office', requested by...
Penance?
There's a Rhodes Island safehouse here too... I see these Rhodes Islanders are like tuskbeasts. Dig many hideouts for themselves.
*huff*... Don't attract any attention... Focus on recovering my strength—
*stomach groans*
Lunacub huddles between the ceiling and a ventilation duct, rubbing her empty belly with some frustration.
...I smell something sweet. Like honey.
No, I need to focus. Got to sharpen my arrows while time is on my side—
I can eat first? But Agnese, didn't you say I have to be patient while I'm out hunting—
My stomach can wait. I don't want you to lose.
Yup, I'll do everything else as you say, except for this. I don't want to let you down.
Lunacub hides in a corner, grabbing her bow and taking aim, almost instinctively.
(Not a wolf. It's the kind of smell that Agnese hates.)
My nose isn't sharp enough to tell which one you are.
But I know one of you is hiding in here.
You have ten seconds to leave this place. I'll wait for you outside.
Of course, it won't be hard for me to get around Rhodes Island employees to deal with you, but I'd rather not take the risk.
So do me a favor. I'm trying my best to avoid leaving a bad impression on Lisa's coworkers.
I guess there's no more room for discussion—
A timely response. Interesting.
I suggest everyone put their weapons down.
We've sealed the exits and activated all defense systems, so let's not make it any worse.
You set a trap for me?
Um... trap?
It's just our regular security system at work.
Rhodes Island truly does not disappoint.
I assure you we're just protecting our own assets, miss.
If the two of you could just wait a moment. The government will soon take things over from here.
(Agnese, any ideas to get out of here? Agnese? Agnese... where are you?)
We meet again, Ingrid.
An unregistered visitor using a deadly weapon inside the city and vandalizing assets belonging to a legitimate company.
This time, you will have to come with me for an investigation.
And her?
Ingrid points at the girl who is still warily observing the situation developing around her.
*sigh* We'll bring her along too.
Agnese needs me.
My hunt hasn't ended yet—
*stomach groans*
Everyone present turns to look at the source of the sound in unison.
We'll get some food for you.
Please, Signora Venezia. I don't wish to have the police act.
You want to protect her?
That is something for us to consider.
Alright, I'll come along. Only because you have my respect, Lavinia.
Judge Lavinia, we're done with their pre-registrations.
The rest of the process will be a breeze.
I said I'd cooperate, didn't I?
Leon told me about the game. You're in it too? Like her?
Lavinia looks at Lunacub.
Um, grazie mille for the honey biscuits.
Lunacub gobbles down her food as she keeps her eyes on everyone around her.
So can I leave already? I have important things to do.
I'm no Fang, Lavinia. I told you, I'm just a mother in search of justice for her daughter.
Hands off my bow!
Judge, shouldn't we take her in? What if—
Don't worry, I know what I'm doing.
Remember me, Lunacub? Don't be rash, the police have already locked down this area.
Stay by my side. It'll be good for all of us. You can trust me.
I only trust Agnese.
Bernardo trusted me too.
...He's dead.
But his heir still lives.
I-I want to ask Agnese about this. But she hasn't been replying me for a while.
Stay with me, and you won't have to worry about getting into trouble. You'll have more time to contact Agnese too.
Ingrid quietly watches Lavinia as she persuades Lunacub.
The girl covered with a wild scent is hesitating. Ingrid knows an opportunity approaches.
If Lavinia can get Agnese's Fang under control, that will save Ingrid a lot of trouble.
The other Fang in hiding will surely come to her.
If you notified me earlier, this misunderstanding we're having here could have been settled in a much more decent manner.
Long time no see, sorella.
Signor Antonio.
Salve, Judge Lavinia. I made my way here just to submit the required documents and company testimonials, to help Ingrid Venezia complete her identification registration.
You—
Leave this to me, sorella. We Venezias have always been happily cooperative with the government.
Your Honor, I know Ingrid's recent actions have violated a couple of rules of Nuova Volsinii, but I believe none of them are serious?
Let's see: not registering on entry, using personal weapons in public spaces... oh, and vandalizing legitimate company assets, yes?
...Yes.
Regarding the last item, Venezia Trucking will discuss compensation with the involved company as soon as we are able.
Signor Venezia, you can just tell me what you want.
Bail. I'd like to bring my sister back home, everyone misses her so much.
Wait, Antonio. I didn't want to drag the family in. There's no need for you to fight with city hall for me.
It's not just for you, sorella. It's for the famiglia too. You may have your own opinions, but papa and I still regard you as one of ours.
I want every Venezia to have a clean record in Nuova Volsinii.
Besides, there's no need to censor the word 'famiglia' here. Everyone knows it's the foundation of our very lives. Nuova Volsinii just wants a declaration from us.
Forgive me for my candidness, Judge Lavinia.
I understand, your company's enduring cooperative nature with us relies on both sides being honest with each other.
However, are you sure you want to stake the entire company's reputation to vouch for Ingrid, Signor Venezia?
Why not? It would have been the same with the old don.
Venezia Trucking will ensure the government that Ingrid Venezia will not step afoul of the law during her post-bail probation period.
If you find any clear evidence of her doing so, I will personally accompany her at court.
Lavinia's head hurts. The other party's request does comply perfectly with standard procedures.
Ingrid does have a right to be released on bail, but... it shouldn't happen now.
I can't refuse you, can I? You really excel at dealing with the government, Signore.
Because I know you are not the inflexible type.
After all, I can clearly tell you know our little friend here with the huge bow, and you have no problems with her actions either.
Andiamo, sorella.
Someone else will handle the formalities for you. I know why you're back in Siracusa, we can help you with Lisa's matter.
Venezia... are you involved as well?
—Aaaaahh!
What's wrong?
I-I just got bitten by a tuskbeast that came out of nowhere!
A tuskbeast?
Wait—where's Lunacub?
Isn't she right next to—
She suddenly freezes—there is only air.
Where did she go?
Your Honor, we just engaged with a very agile individual.
She was mumbling something about wilderness survival skills and an Agnese, we couldn't stop her... we sent some men to chase after her.
I got it.
To be honest, I thought we'd never get a chance to see each other again until the famiglia started considering an expansion in Higashi, sister.
Stop calling me that, Antonio. We were never that close, and I'm no longer part of the family.
Alright.
Judging from their attitude towards you, I see you'll be the next don.
You're looking more and more like the Don—not just your attitude, but your appearance as well.
Your ears and tail... Did you become more like Bella after your marriage, or have you gotten some work done?
Ingrid, why are you still like this...
You don't like it when people make fun of you, but you don't hold back when you're on the offensive.
Really now?
It's been a while, Ingrid. Papa and I missed you very much.
Mhm.
He took you in and raised you as his own. When I joined the famiglia you were the one he trusted the most. He loved you as much as his own daughter.
But the chap who fled here from Bolívar and became a hired thug to avoid starving to death? He didn't know a soul here, and could only tuck his tail and try his best.
Combat wise? Within the wolves, he wasn't any much stronger. So what could he possibly offer such a big famiglia in exchange for a place of his own?
Be it a Vulpo's compassion for a Perro, or the most reliable member of the famiglia doing it to appease her subordinates...
Basically, I wouldn't be standing here today if you hadn't helped me out.
You're over-thinking it.
The Don cares about many things, but decency will always top the list no matter what. He'll never let family members exclude or fight each other.
That wasn't my point, Ingrid.
Oh?
I have to become Venezia Junior. This is my way of repaying what you did.
Six years ago, the day we fought the Tancredis and it felt like it would never end—you were the one who pulled me out from the pile of corpses.
I didn't do it for you.
Lisa was only nine when she was hurt during the Tancredis' plot to assassinate the Don.
I don't care who orchestrated it. They should all perish.
...I know. I'm just admitting the fact that you saved my life.
Enough with the niceties. Both you and him have more important things to deal with.
When I had just arrived here, I was collecting information when I overheard some wise guys at the bar bragging about your achievements.

'Antonio went with Venezia Senior and asked to meet with Signora Sicilia, and then sat at the negotiation table alone. Even though the other party gave him a hard time, he still managed to save a factory in Terni for the Venezias.
Look at you now. Got what you wanted.
It should've been you instead.
But you insisted on going your own way and escaping Siracusa.
You may be a Vulpo, but I personally think you're more of a Siracusan than anyone else.
I've never forgotten the first time I followed you on a mission. We were ambushed in an alley, and a blade scratched your face. There was so much blood I wanted to send you to the hospital...
But you said, our opponents think we're dead, so now is the best time to get them while their guard is lowered.
'You need to be fiercer, Perro.'
'Living in a Siracusan famiglia means living life on the edge. You may lose everything at any moment.'
'If you aren't prepared for this, then you better run back to Bolívar.'
You always did everything better than the rest of us. You were my role model.
So I don't care that you went on a trip and got married to a Higashinese man whom we've never met, or how you suddenly came back with Lisa in tow...
Don't get me wrong, I was fond of her, as well as papa and Bella... We gave her the best room and best teachers, we loved her as much as we loved you.
Papa often said the ragazzina would only make you stronger, instead of becoming your weak spot—
Enough, Anton. Too many words. Sei fastidioso.
I didn't come back to wax nostalgic with you. Just tell me who hurt Lisa.
...You won't even wait until you see our father again?
Come back and help me out, Ingrid.
What happened with Lisa is something the famiglia owes you. I'll definitely help you.
I don't want anything to do with the family anymore. It's the best way forward for both sides.
Listen to me, we took great pains to establish a firm foundation in Nuova Volsinii. A brand new city, a new set of laws. There are many things to attend to.
Papa's old, and there hasn't been any youths worth pinning his hopes on. You know what he's like. He's not willing to change, but he's much less willing to see the famiglia regress.
And then Bella's death dealt such a big blow to both him and I—
Bella's dead?
Happened last year. You know she was never in the best of health.
I really need you with us, Ingrid... I think papa wants you home even more than I do.
Ingrid is silent. She merely casts a cold stare at the Perro before her.
Come back to us, and I'll tell you the truth about Lisa's injury.
I see there's nothing else to discuss.
Grazie mille for separating me from Lavinia on your own accord, but it was not necessary.
I had my own plans in mind.
Ingrid waves her hand, and turns to leave.
Ingrid, I knew you were back on your first day here.
The famiglia has never forgotten you.
Also... I saw the famiglia crest stashed away in your pocket. You're not as sure as you make yourself look.
The Venezias' doors are always open to you.
Well... You're already a true-blue Siracusan yourself.
You honor me.
Wait, Ingrid. Got news you might be interested in.
I know you two. Saw you both next to Antonio the other day.
I believe I made myself clear with him earlier.
We know where Luchino de Montano is. We bagged him and brought him back to the Venezia residence in the city.
We can't take you to him, but we can tell you where exactly he is inside.
Why sell out your own employer?
And how do you know what Umberto asked me to do?
Uh... I don't know how to explain this properly!
Wait, he ain't lying to you.
Minchia. Someone made us do this, okay? We don't know how you're linked to the tailor boy, we're just trying to survive!
Survive?
Explain everything clearly or you can kiss your lives goodbye.
Ingrid knows how to use her blade well. She knows when to take someone's life with it, and when to make people believe so with it.
Unfortunately, Capone and Gambino have no idea she's using it for the latter right now.
Che noia. I thought you were serious at first.
You goddamn pazza!
He's got a sturdy neck, so you might have to put your strength into it.
...Lappland Saluzzo?
Oh. You know me?
We met once.
You've grown up so much, but I remember that smile of yours.
We've met?
I don't remember that.
Ten years ago, in Brunello. I was mediating a dispute between your Famiglia Saluzzo and the Morettis.
Your family was obviously on the losing side, and you were just a teenager then—the successor bearing Alberto's highest expectations. But what you really wanted to do...
Was to seize an opportunity to bury the famiglia six feet under.
Haha. I was young and frivolous then.
People mature. Now all of Siracusa is my enemy.
You really are insane.
Must be sarcasm with that smile on your face.
Why tell me this? Is there a conflict of interest between the Saluzzos and Venezias?
Why would I care about the Saluzzos' fate?
The little tailor owes me two suits, is all. When you see him, do pass on the reminder.
It's rude to keep your clients waiting.
...I'll ask him about it.
And just so you know: You're such a boring Siracusan, but quite amusing as a mother.
I see even messenger work is an arduous task for you two.
If you want us to do stuff for you, let us tell the boss first at least. I don't want the Venezias after me.
What else do you want from us, Lappland?
Wow. I didn't think the both of you would be so willing to work for me.
Relax, I'm not interested in poaching men from the Perro.
Just be good and stay by his side. Help him out when he needs it.
Of course, I'll reward you by divulging where the missing truck is, the one you have been looking for.
...Just what are you up to?
I'm making you both look good to Venezia Junior, of course.
Don't you deserve it?
Gambino. I got a feeling we're gonna end up fed to the fins for real this time.
Yup.
Signora Venezia, did you just say my child was taken away by Venezia wise guys?
My informant has no reason to lie to me.
It's true Luchino went to the port area that night. I heard a car accident happened.
Yes, I was nearby as well.
I'm guessing the Venezias are involved, and if I'm not wrong, Luchino should have discovered something.
*sigh*
Please leave, Signora. I have to close the shop early today.
You're going to find Venezia Junior yourself?
Umberto, I don't know what you used to be like. But I'm sure your age has affected your abilities, even if you were chosen by Vargo back then.
A sickly old tailor might not even make it past Venezia Junior's doorstep.
If Luchino really found out about something he shouldn't have, then he might be stuck at the Venezia estate for good.
I'm old and feeble, Signora Venezia. Even if this hadn't happened, my candle's burning out anyway.
If there's anything I can't let go right now, it's the boy.
He's my only relative, and I am his only relative too. He's still so young. Children always leave us with no other choice.
Besides, I'm no stranger to the Venezias.
Hm?
I didn't tell you this before—but I used to work for them before you did.
It's probably why Vargo chose me.
*sigh* Life goes in a circle, and now I'm going back to where I used to be.
I'll go with you.
Signora... this has nothing to do with you at all.
I'm already very grateful you found a clue to his whereabouts, and came here just to let me know.
I know you had an unpleasant split with the Venezias because of what happened with Lisa, so if you go back just like that, you'll run into all sorts of trouble.
You need not worry. I'm doing this because of my agreement with Vargo. Before I find out what truly happened back then, I won't allow anything to happen to you.
Besides, I understand what sacrifices one would willingly make for the sake of a child.
Is someone there? Can I leave now?
A child?
Did you say something, ragazzino?
Afraid of me?
May I go home, per favore?
I'm afraid I just happen to be here as a guest, so I can't interfere in family matters. Why are they keeping you in here?
I-I don't know, but if you can't let me go... could you at least contact Umberto de Montano of De Montano Tailor Shop for me?
Umberto...
He's my nonno. He'll get worried if I'm gone for too long... I have some medicine for him I got from the shop which he really needs, if I c-can't...
You're worried about your nonno?
Mhm.
Follow me. The Venezias always treat their guests right.
I didn't think I'd meet a friend on this trip.
Lucky for me, I like it when it's lively.
Do I just follow you...? But those people outside...
They're also busy catering to me, their guest.
Luchino follows the old man close behind, and they don't run into anyone on their way.
Wh-Who exactly are you, if you don't mind me asking?
Stay close, boy. It's a big place, but we're almost there.
Earlier you said you wanted me to tell your nonno about a bunch of things, right?
Sorry, I'm old and my memory's spotty.
But I think you can tell him yourself.
Umberto, I envy you for having such a loving grandson.
N-Nonno?
Luchino's head is full of questions—Has he interrupted De Montano's business? Was his nonno worried sick about him? How did he know to find him here?
Umberto only casts a glance at Luchino. The old tailor doesn't say a word.
Have a seat.
I'll decline. My legs aren't as good as they used to. Sitting down is easy, but getting up is quite difficult.
Alright then.
You said Ingrid came with you... so where is she?
She left after learning you were the one who found Luchino. She really trusts you.
...Alright.
When did you move out of Montelupe? You left without even a word.
I just thought there wasn't a need to do so.
Time hasn't been kind to you. The ragazzino told me you're ill?
It's not serious, though it's bothersome. My eyes and ears don't work so well anymore, my waist, legs, and shoulder joints are always hurting... it's thanks to my work.
Just elderly things. Even carefully maintained quality fabric will inevitably turn stiff and brittle.
Take care of your nonno, boy. Don't leave any regrets.
I didn't expect to meet you here, and what's happened with the boy... has caused you trouble.
Hardly. I came here for Carnevale, and happened to bump into your grandson here.
Nonno, I—
I know, Luchino. Let me handle this.
Antonio should have acted in a more decent manner, and I've sent someone to find him so he can explain himself to you.
Antonio... A terribly common name in Siracusa. But right now, Luchino could only match it to a single face—
Antonio Venezia, the head of Venezia Trucking Co.
...Signor Antonio?
I thought this matter would be more troublesome than this, but since you've already stepped in—
Umberto, you lack the patience of your youth.
Antonio has to explain himself to you, and once that's done, I can also rest easy.
Do you want to meet the owner of this place, boy?
...Nonno?
There's no way we can possibly refuse, is there?
Just treat it as a favor to one of your regulars, vecchio amico.
I was asked before to make a suit for you.
Why don't we make use of our time together here and measure you right now?
I hear it's a grand affair, with civilians wearing masks and costumes escorting giant parade cars around the city. I'm sure you'll be there, and need an outfit too.
I used to remember every regular client's measurements, but as you can see, time shows no mercy to us all. Everyone's changed a lot.
I've been relying on Luchino more and more to help me write down things I often forget about.
Alright. We have some time to spare before Antonio arrives.
Luchino, I'm afraid we won't be going back so soon. Can you wait a while longer?
It's fine, Nonno! Can I help out somehow?
Get me my toolbox.
Prepare a file with this gentleman's name on it.
I'm sorry, signore, I never got your name...
Fabrizio Venezia.
The old man writes down his own name, and gives it to Luchino.
Y-you're... But why did you say you were a guest...?
Hush, Luchino. Just do as I say without a word from you, and do what we have to do.
Signor Fabrizio is but a client to us right now.
Would you allow this boy to serve you as my assistant, signore? He's been wanting to work on a major client.
Luchino was never allowed to assist his grandfather with important customers in any step of the process before.
But now, Umberto guides him every step of the way, and even encourages the boy to do it himself.
'You must show your clients respect and gratitude', he is told...
Everything goes smoothly and naturally, as if the kidnapping never happened and his nonno hadn't come here to rescue him.
Just working a regular job.
His hands are as stable as yours. Will he become a 'tagliatore' like you?
A 'tagliatore'?
I see you've never told this child about your past, Umberto.
Ingrid, aren't you going in to see Papa?
So long as the Don is with them, Umberto and Lucchino won't be in any danger.
I just have to make sure they walk out of here safe and sound.
You know that's not what I meant.
You're the one who he needs to see.
Involve an innocent child in famiglia affairs? I don't remember the old man ever doing something like this.
He wasn't brought back here on my orders. But I won't deny it—I would have done this anyway if his presence truly poses a threat to the famiglia's interests.
The famiglia's interests? Or yours?
Is there a difference between the two?
You've changed quite a lot, Antonio.
No, you've been away from home for too long, Ingrid.
This is Siracusa's twenty-third nomadic city. A piece of uncultivated fertile land.
I've sown seeds here, so I'm obliged to ensure they mature for a ripe harvest. No matter the methods used.
It's also why I've been urging you to come back to us. I know what you're capable of, and we need you here.
Anton, the Don won't like this.
He'll be mad, of course.
He just summoned me inside, and I can guess he has a lecture in store.
But do you think he's angry because I got an innocent boy involved? No, he doesn't care at all.
He's just angry I wasn't stealthy enough, wasn't proper and 'decent' enough.
I will prove to him with actual results that the Venezias can establish two sets of order at the same time—one on the table and the other underneath.
But what we should prioritize is definitely not this so-called 'decency'.
Even if it hurts him, and those who trust you?
I love him, I do. He gave me everything I have. I think this is the way I can repay him for that, no matter what it takes.
And you got one thing wrong, Ingrid. I've never changed.
...Never changed—
Is that so...?
A 'tagliatore'...
De Montano Tailor Shop no longer offers that service today. This child doesn't need to know about it.
Pity. I still remember how elegantly you would solve my many problems.
Montelupe's famiglie were all terrified of the elusive 'tagliatore'. One only had to write the target's name down on the order sheet, and he would be reported dead in the newspapers the next day.
...It was all to repay your kindness.
You saved me when I was a young tailor about Luchino's age, and gave me a chance to live in Montelupe.
You deserve a decent finale, no matter what.
You've always been so good to me.
Luchino is having a severe headache.
They are indeed, talking about his very own grandfather, but it doesn't seem to match up with the old man right before him.
Right, what happened to his parents? Your son—Luca, right?
Yes. Luca de Montano.
Parents? Luchino seems to have asked Umberto about them before, but the old man always dodged the question.
He had tried to read his grandfather's eyes, but the old man only focused on his work.
Luca was a good boy. A wonderful, sensible child with a talent for tailoring and willingness to bear any hardship.
He was already a pro in his twenties, and the regulars were all fond of him. I had planned to hand over the shop to him.
Until one day, his body was discovered in a vehicle leaving the city early in the morning.
Next to him was a musical actress, with a little bit of fame in Montelupe, highly coveted by many mafiosi—
Luchino, your hands are shaking.
...Perhaps we should slow down?
Go fetch our fabric samples. I'll handle it from here.
...A-Alright.
...*sigh*
You were a little bit cruel to the boy.
My days are numbered, signore. I can't protect him forever.
Luca's death made me realize something.
Hm?
The blood on my hands can never be wiped clean no matter how many times or ways I try. The stench of it will only bring misfortune to me and my family.
But that boy still fantasized about serving the so-called 'somebodys'. It's my fault.
Be it Signor Antonio, you, or even other famiglie wise guys, Luchino will eventually run into mafiosi.
He has to know about it.
You see Luca's death as punishment for your crimes?
As a price I paid...
Raise your head, signore. To measure your neck circumference.
Nonno would sometimes stay at the workbench longer than usual, and his hands would tremble nonstop.
He teased Luchino on multiple occasions the shop would have to close down if Luchino couldn't learn the trade in time.
But now, his grandfather's hands are steadier than he has ever seen.
Luchino watches him pull out the measuring tape. He starts from Venezia Sr.'s shoulders, and then his arms, chest, waist, and legs.
But Luchino knows his nonno's attention has been on him the whole time.
Unfortunately, he is not yet ready to accept the truth.
After losing Luca, I learned about Luchino's existence—Luca had him fostered at a farm out in the countryside.
And then I found out how Luca had met the actress through a costume order from the theater...
...How he hid a forbidden way of life he gradually built for themselves away from everyone, just to save me from worrying and avoid inciting more trouble…
...How he managed to leave a little speck of hope for themselves, and for me...
Luchino is all I have left from Luca, and my final wish is for him to live.
No matter what Signor Antonio or you have planned, I beg you—please don't involve him ever again.
(Nonno...)
Luchino does not know how to react.
The truth of his parents' demise, his nonno's past, his own future... he drowns within his web of emotions.
Please raise your arm.
Sure.
Signore, I do not ask anything of you.
I only have a selfish wish to give this child some time, to let him carefully observe how a tailor works.
How to measure, how to cut cloth, how to respectfully receive clients...
And how to stay away from a life he doesn't belong in.
*sigh* Very well. I see you're set on parting from all your history.
No need to deliver the suit yourself once you're done. I'll get someone to take it from your sartoria.
Umberto, if you ever feel nostalgic and want to return to Montelupe, make sure to remember il vecchio here too.
Of course.
Grazie mille, Signore.
So, you have no intentions of letting that ragazzino go...
Even if the Don's already agreed to do so.
Ingrid, even if the kid swears he won't shoot his mouth off, there's no guarantee city hall won't hear about some ridiculous rumors from him.
Many famiglie have their eyes on us. I can't afford the risk.
Papa will understand.
Think about your family too, Ingrid.
A wolf asked me to fulfill an agreement we made, and I came back to this country for my daughter.
My anger has long ceased to burn for the famiglia.
I act only for my own sake now, Anton.
...What a shame. Then I'm afraid there's no more room for any discussion.
You think they can hold me off?
No, I just need to make sure that kid keeps his mouth shut.
Anton, answer this question.
When you bailed me out and said you knew who was the culprit behind Lisa's injury, were you telling the truth?
...Absolutely. I've never lied to you, Ingrid.
After I sent Lisa away from Siracusa, I investigated the matter myself in secret, and although I found nothing...
I always had some suspicions I didn't want to admit was possible. Though, I don't think you'll speak any more on the subject, for now at least.
...Forgive me, but I can't tell you anything right now.
No need. I'll find out eventually.
The blade falls just above Antonio's nose, but he retains his smile.
Ingrid, you know there are people watching every move we make in this household.
Such indecent behavior from you.
Ingrid, please lower your blade, for my sake.
...Don Venezia.
Long time no see, child.
Signor Antonio, I'm done taking Signor Fabrizio's measurements.
We have the fabric he wants in stock, so if we hurry, the suit can be finished in time. We won't interfere in your affairs.
...Papa?
(Nods)
I'll be taking Luchino home now.
Nonno, they're definitely not planning to let us go...
Doesn't matter.
Just go. I'll take care of it.
Signora Venezia, what about you...?
I'm afraid I have some family affairs to settle.
Anton, I think you know what the Don's thinking. Send your men away.
You can't settle it like this, Papa.
Umberto made me a promise. We can't keep our guest in here forever, Anton.
But Ingrid—Your actions have indeed embarrassed us.
Mi scusi.
It's important to me that they live.
(Go.)
*sigh* Ingrid, I've been hoping to meet you from the moment I stepped into this city.
But not like this.
We Venezias never point our blades inward.
*cough* Come, Luchino.
Oh no!
What's the matter?
The medicine!
I left them in the room inside, and the goods I was supposed to deliver... they frisked me when I was brought here—Nonno, I-I'll go retrieve them.
Forget it, boy.
The Venezia residence is several blocks away from our shop. It's a long way back.
Luchino turns around, and does not see any mafioso come out from the residence.
He feels the old tailor's hand on his shoulder. He can feel the wizened hand trembling, as if he had exhausted his strength during the meeting with the old Venezia earlier.
To make matters worse, he realizes his own arms are still so scrawny. If his nonno falls, he might not even manage to hold him up.
Nonno...
Hm?
N-Nothing.
Luchino has questions in his head that he can't voice. But suddenly, he recalls a vague image in his mind resembling their current walk back home on an equally strange road.
Was he three, or four?
He remembers a vehicle driving by the farm. An old man jumps down, fails his landing, and falls clumsily.

The old man then walks up to him. He stares at the mud on his face, a pair of scissors, and a ruler on his waist, and bursts into tears from fear. The old man panics, frantically trying to soothe him—
There were far fewer wrinkles on his face then.

The old man takes him by the hand and leads him away from the farm. Uncertain of where they're going, he cries even louder in fear. The noise startles nearby fowlbeasts, sending them flying into the distance.
'Hush, Luchino. Don't cry.'
'Umberto, say something, anything... Ah, Nonno will tell you a tale...'
'Every hunter runs into a wolf before they go into the wastes.'

'The wolf says: Mulberry wood makes sturdy bows, and stones can be sharpened into deadly blades; but you must shed the clothes on you to survive, for it is the only thing that does not belong in the barrens...'
'Haha, you really stopped crying. Did you like the story, bambino? I know many, many more...'
Nonno.
Hm?
Can you teach me how to sew buttons on again?
I'm glad you're willing to listen to this vecchio's suggestion to stay here and catch up on old times, Ingrid.
I thought you would have left like you did the last time, with your blade in hand without looking back.
I also thought traveling to Higashi would bring me calm. But as you can see, I could not escape.
From the moment I set foot in here, I knew it would not be easy to leave.
So you are set on leaving, Ingrid?
You can try to stop me.
The Don is right, we Venezias never point our blades inward.
The Vulpo takes something out from her coat.
Antonio knows too well what it is—the family crest. He received one just like it when he decided to follow Fabrizio.
Are you sure?
Forgive me. I have a feeling you won't approve of what I want to do next.
If this crest is stopping me from untangling the knot in my heart, then I have no other option.
...You're being selfish, ragazza.
I know.
Why did you throw a stone at me, ragazzina?
Accident, I swear. I was trying to hit those stronzi that ran away and then you, you got in my way...
The wound on your face... did they do that to you?
They say my ears are big and weird. They say I'm not a Lupo and don't belong here in Siracusa.
Did you see their injuries? They ended up worse than I did. Why run their mouths if they can't defeat me? I'll pull out their teeth the next time they try me again!
Haha, you're more of a Lupo than they are.
Where's your family?
Dead. We came here as refugees, but papa and mama encountered a gang brawl when they went to ask the government for help.
Do you have anywhere else to go?
No.
Do you want to come with me then?
You need me to fight for you?
We can talk about that when you're older.
My daughter, Bella... she's sick and doesn't really go out, so she could use a playmate...
If she's fine playing with a Vulpo, I don't mind.
That's it?
I'm weak. I've been staying here in the park, but I can't beat everyone up.
Take this.
What is it?
Famiglia Venezia's crest. Take it, and you become one of us.
But I'm not a Lupo.
Many in the famiglia aren't either, but we're even closer to each other than the other famiglie. You'll understand in time.
Don't hide behind your mother like that. She can block your body, but I can already see your tails.
So this is Lisa?
She's a complete contrast to you when you were a girl, Ingrid.
Sorry. For not telling you before I brought her back here.
The vecchi at the shrine discovered this child, and I couldn't let her stay in Higashi any longer.
Because having the blood of the so-called nine-tails means spending your entire life inside the shrine, living a life like her father. Worshiped like a mascot that grants every request, è ridicolo.
We've tidied up a room for her. Once she gets used to life here, she can go to school with the other children in the famiglia.
I hope Lisa will stay far away from the farce in Higashi, but I don't want her to learn the so-called Siracusan way to survive either.
Arrange this with Anton and hire professional teachers for her. Music, literature, art, astrology, Originium Arts... Let her study whatever she likes.
You're not mad at me?
You're still here, Ingrid.
Has your blade slowed? Have you completed fewer missions?
No.
Then you remain the most reliable person to take over my responsibilities.
Si.
Besides, I find myself fond of this bambina.
So the most important thing to do right now is show her I'm not scary. Haha.
Still nothing?
I snapped the tendons in that stronzo's limbs and tail, but he still refused to confess who planned the assassination. He just kept saying he didn't know anything...
Same as the other seventeen before him.
Lisa just fell asleep a moment ago.
Her Oripathy is quite severe. The crystals have spread to her shoulder, there's no need for you to look after her yourself.
I've taken my precautions. Besides, she got infected because of me. Ingrid, you've been silent on this.
Lisa's an innocent girl. She doesn't know how bad things are outside, but she can instinctively pick up the tension in the house.
When you went to rescue Antonio, she wanted to stay with me so you could worry less.
The people from Rhodes Island are here.
So you already found a place better for her.
The famiglia can find the best Oripathy doctor for Lisa. You didn't have to contact that Rhodes Island company behind my back.
Ingrid, there's no need for you two to leave.
Signora Sicilia will visit Terni herself tomorrow. The Tancredis and the Venezias will have no choice but to sit at the negotiation table.
You need to wipe my name off the famiglia records by then. Insist I was expelled, like how the Tancredis refused even at the brink of death to acknowledge they planned your assassination.
I remember discussing this before.
Indeed.
I'm sorry, Don Venezia. The famiglia crest, take it back.
No, Ingrid. Keep it.
I have approved of you taking revenge for Lisa. I am fond of her. Why else do you think no one from the famiglia has stopped you these past two weeks?
We are family, Ingrid.
'We are family.'
This emblem has been a blessing from you, but also a shackle for me.
How is she doing on Rhodes Island?
She's considered an employee now. A few years ago, she even went to Leithanien and helped them investigate an incident about missing Infected.
She even kept it a secret from me and never mentioned it in her letters. I talked to her friends when I went to see her, and they were all saying how well she did on that mission.
I miss Lisa so much.
If she's willing to come back, I won't stop her... You know I'm not here to reminisce about old times.
Perhaps you had many plans for me when you insisted I keep the famiglia emblem—
Ingrid glances at Antonio standing silently on the side.
Keep it, Ingrid. Papa was telling me how we could all visit Montelupe together after Carnevale is over.
Hm?
Anton, it's too early for that.
Didn't you say Signora Sicilia might relent?
She used to loathe Carnevale, and now it's back in Siracusa with a brand new look. Compared to that, Ingrid's violations are not even worth mentioning.
I mean, it's been six years. Famiglia Tancredi today is no longer worthy of Signora Sicilia's concern.
In these six years, I used the single vehicle factory I held onto and ate up all of their businesses in Terni.
Signora Sicilia values results. So I showed her.
Is that how you see it, Anton?
Yes, papa.
Antonio's done very well by your side. The family no longer needs me here.
Anton... After Bella's passing, I could feel him becoming wary of me.
How much value did he have as a son-in-law after Bellas's death? That's probably what he was thinking at the time, and how he got himself so busy after.
Papa, I—
I don't want to give you too much pressure. We already have enough territory, even in this newly-built Nuova Volsinii.
You've done very well, Anton.
Seeing Ingrid leave and Bella die... what I truly care about is the few 'family' I have left... After all, I am an old man.
I don't want to lose any of you anymore.
Ingrid looks at the old man whose hair has turned entirely gray, and does not say anything else.
She puts the crest on the table.
Ingrid, you heard what he said. At least for now—
Ingrid turns and leaves before Antonio can even finish.
Mi dispiace, Don Venezia...
I know it's hard for you, but... I can't do it.
Ingrid?
There's never been much I could do for you, besides killing.
But even the best killer is still just a killer, not 'Venezia Junior'. Antonio fits that role better than I can, so my departure isn't such a big loss.
You must have realized it in the past six years. Deep inside you're disappointed with me.
But wasn't I disappointed too, when I failed to find out the truth about her injury?
Oripathy is incurable. You know better than anyone else why I came back to Siracusa.
I intend to find the culprit I couldn't track down six years ago... and end his life. That's the only thing I want to do.
But you know what it means to abandon the emblem and leave.
Yes. We might end up as oppositions.
I can't guarantee that I won't harm any of the Venezias.
Even so, I'll make things decent, for your sake. This is the only thing I can promise you.
Papa, I can try and talk to her more...
No.
The old man runs his fingers over the famiglia crest, and puts it in his chest pocket.
We all know what kind of person she is.
She's not easily swayed when she's set on something. I'll keep this emblem for now. When she comes to her senses, I'll return it to her.
Keep a low profile for now; consider it keeping me company at home.
But the company...
Do you not trust the subordinates you hired yourself?
I understand.
I may not interfere with your work here in the city, but it doesn't mean I'm in the dark.
Umberto de Montano is an old friend of mine, and he has always acted with decency. So leave them be.
Yes, papa.
I heard that Ingrid asked you about how Lisa got hurt. Did you find out the truth?
Just some clues. So much time has passed, after all.
Is that so? Anton, there are some matters you can judge on your own to be right or wrong...
But when it involves the famiglia's reputation, think carefully before you act.
Yes, papa.
Antonio! We got news!
According to intel from Capone and Gambino, we found the lost shipment at the port.
B-But... Someone stuffed it in a parade float and set it on fire.
Who did it?
Not sure.
The cops took the Truckers' Union's boss away for an investigation. The one called 'Eirene', she...

lines.st: Old Vasily was a valiant emperor.
Some say that even after the age of eighty, he would don his crown and head to battle.
Ursus had suffered a crushing defeat deep in the snowfields. The bodies were churned into the snow, the corpses piled up into walls.
The emperor screamed bloody vengeance, his anger louder than the howling wind.
The surviving officers surrounded his tent, but the crown shone bright, so bright that the people could only shout their reverence for him.
A sudden fierce gust from the north blew the tent open. Only then did everyone dare to raise their eyes and look ahead.
The crown had fallen on the ground. No longer was there an emperor on the snowfields. It is said that in the end, Old Vasily was trampled to death by the crowd.
Of the countless nameless soldiers kneeling outside his tent, none knew the name of the Slayer of Crowns, or from where they came.

lines.x: Oh, this is the first time I've heard you tell this story in Siracusan.
So you got the name 'Crownslayer' out of a book. What's the book called again?
'Truth and Justice'.
Sounds complicated... I thought books like that were all chunky doorstoppers about big ideas, but you're saying it's a storybook?
No, it's not. The stories inside aren't for entertainment, but to get people thinking.
What is 'truth'? What is 'justice'?
Should they be considered the same? Should they be set against each other? Who gets to be the one that defines them? Which side of the scale do we believe we're on?
Or maybe they don't exist in any real sense? Just like... Just like how Reunion is over.
In the end, the only time they're of any use are when putting together a lovely little list of justifications.
Err... Y'know, before you told me this story, all I wanted to ask was where we should head next.
I was just about to say. This is where we part, friend.
If you keep going straight this way, you'll come out of the woods and spot some old nomadic city tracks.
There's likely to be Leithanian border patrol teams nearby, or possibly Siracusan cargo landships passing through. Watch out for them.
You made it through Chernobog and Lungmen. Avoiding them shouldn't be too difficult for you.
*sigh* Out of a team of thirty, I'm the last man standing...
Why can't we just stay here, Crownslayer?
If we stay together here, we'll just become targets for those in power. That or be forced to become cannon fodder again.
Since you guys didn't want to follow Nine, I'll be the one seeing you off.
This is the last thing I can do as Reunio—the last thing I can do for you.
But where will you go? You'd be on your own too, wouldn't you?
I'll wander. I'll check on a few things. I'll think a bit.
Before I left Siracusa, my master told me not to go back to Ursus to seek revenge. I was too weak, and knew too little... But I didn't listen to her.
It might be time to return now. I'd like to see her again. Maybe she can give me some other suggestions... Ways for me to become stronger.
What's your master like?
She's from one of the Siracusan famiglie. She was strong in battle, yet also one of the wisest people I knew. She taught me a lot.
Siracusa, eh? That's a long ways away.
Look at this bonfire out here among the snow, friend. Once, it could ignite the anger of the Infected. It could spark the dream of Reunion. It could set all of Ursus ablaze.
But now, all it does is keep us warm.
Here's the last thing I have to say to you: Forget Talulah. Forget Chernobog. Forget Lungmen. Forget Reunion. Forget us. If you choose to leave, then leave it all behind you.
Hell, forget the crystals growing on your body. Maybe if you forget everything, you can go home.
*sigh*...
Though we both chose to leave, I always felt you and I weren't alike...
You look like you're the type of person who knows a lot, and you've read lots of books. At times, I feel like you, the Captain and Talulah are all pretty similar.
*sigh*... I meant more like... When I was little, my grandpapa always said people who read too much were easily lead astray.
Anyway... Never mind. See you later, Crownslayer. Best of luck to you. Looks like you're going to need it.
We won't be seeing each other again, friend.
Ugh, my tail's all wet.
Nothing's changed at all. Neither these filthy streets, nor this damned rain.
Dunno what it is, but even though the snow in Ursus can kill people, the rain here's somehow more unpleasant.
Hope there's a hairdryer at my master's place...
Maestro!
The thick, inescapable stench of blood.
Her teacher lies face-up on the ground, fresh blood still flowing outward and spreading.
A small knife has been dropped nearby.
Lyudmila tries to approach and stifle her teacher's still-bleeding wound, but she finds herself unable to move a single step, unable to even tear away her gaze.
From the shadows wafts an aura that is both familiar and terrifying to her.
A wolf with gray hair, wearing a red hoodie.
The true wolf is dead. I did as Grandmother asked.
It's you.
What... have you done?!
I am a wolf hunter. I do as Grandmother asks.
The true wolf is dead. The true wolf did not fight.
Why...
Why?
Why has this girl wearing a red hoodie chased after her, haunted her from Yan to Siracusa?
You...
Who the hell...
Who the hell are you?!
Lyudmila silently looks at the fire in front of her. It is a burning duffel bag, her master interred within.

She did not sleep at all that night. She thinks about how she used salt and water to scrub away the bloodstains in the room, how she dragged her master's corpse out into the wilds under the cover of the incessant rain.
She was stick-thin before she died. Lyudmilla barely had to exert herself.
Lyudmila never forgot how she met her master.
There was blood everywhere. Her uncle, her only living relative. His family. His servants. Their corpses strewn across the entire house.

The one who sent her to Siracusa told her that her uncle had control of the businesses on a couple of streets. Not a powerful man, per se, but in a small Siracusan town, it would be enough to let her live comfortably.
But before she could even get used to her new life... A simple Originium bomb smashed through the windows. In the chaos, her arm was gashed. She trembled in fear as she cowered under the bed.
There was no difference between the gangsters rushing into that house and the Ursus Guard charging into the lab. She felt like she was back in Chernobog, back in that night she lost her father.
Only when everything finally went quiet did Lyudmila dare to leave her room.
The gangsters, too, were lying down in pools of blood. The only ones alive in the house were her and the lowliest of her uncle's servants.

If memory served correctly, the servant was always living out by the gate, in charge of sending and receiving letters. The only time she would limp into the house was to deliver the mail to her masters.
She would always tiptoe across the carpet, so as to not make too loud a noise.

lines.st: 'Another gang vendetta. Your uncle upset them. I'm about to head off... Do you have anywhere to go?'
'D-Did you kill all these bad guys?'
'If I didn't, you and I would both be dead.'
'Why didn't you save them when it all started?'
'I thought they were here for me... With this body of mine, I need to wait for them to get close before I can do anything.'
'You're scary... but also amazing. C-Can I go with you?'
'Do you want revenge? For your uncle? You weren't that close to him. And I remember that the one who sent you here wanted you to live a peaceful life... Well, I suppose that's not happening anymore.'
'Lyudmila, do you know what it means to be my student?'
'I've become decrepit and weak now. Are you still willing to be my student, and my tool?'
'We will both regret it, ragazza.'
'I won't. All you need to do is teach me. Teach me how to hold a knife, how to get my revenge. I won't regret it!'
'A knife? No, you're not ready for a knife just yet.'
'First, learn how to light a fire. Burn down this house and everything in it, so that nobody can find us.'
'Fire hides everything, Lyudmila.'

lines.x: She chose to deal with her master's corpse using the very first trick she taught her.
But now what?
After leaving Reunion, she spent two years making her way back to Siracusa, all to lose the last home she could return to in barely a day...
The fire is extinguished, and the morning breeze blows away the last of the ashes. There is no trace of it left in the wilderness.
Where should you go next, Lyudmila?
I want... revenge.
Found you.
Hmm? Were you looking for me?
It's not hard finding a killer from the outside in this city.
You stand out. A lot.
Don't block my way.
Don't you dare think of running!
Hmph.
Before we start, I've got a question for you. Why?
My master never mentioned an enemy like you. Why would someone hanging out with Kal'tsit know her?
The true wolf... must die.
These are the rules of the game.
What game?! What the hell are you talking about?!
I don't care who you are... You killed the last of my family, so I'll make you pay.
You're too weak. You can't beat me.
Don't underestimate me!
You just managed to get a sneak attack off last time! Don't think you managed to scare me!
...You're in my way.
Stop following me.
Get... Get back here!
I'm not letting you go...
Cazzo...!
An unfamiliar city... Unfamiliar food...
It's strange, but it's fine.
You've got quick reflexes, I'll give you that.
...It's you again.
Like I said, I'll make you pay.
Don't even think of escaping. Either kill me, or be killed by me. There's only two ways out of this.
(Bares teeth in irritation)
Angry?
Good, show me what you've really got—
Hey! HEY!
I don't care what famiglie you ragazze are with, you wanna fight, do it outside!
There's a bad smell, and strange sounds.
...Danger.
That strange person...
You... How much longer are you going to follow me?
I told you already. Until I kill you, or you kill me.
You've tried lots of times. You can't kill me.
And now you're tired.
Then... kill me... already.
The exhausted Reproba collapses onto the ground. Before she falls unconscious, she grabs on tight onto the red-clad Lupo's pants.
You... look really pathetic.
Hey, hey! What happened? You alright?
Get back here... Don't go...
*sigh* My truck broke down, couldn't go even if I wanted to.
Why're you lying down here? Are you hurt? Got anyone I can call?
No... None...
What did you say? Can't understand a word of that mumbling... Lemme get that mask off you, let you breathe a bit easier—
Oh, wow... There's crystals in your mouth. That's a pretty serious infection...
A homeless Infected, huh. *sigh* What am I to do with you?
Man, the first time I decide to run a long-haul job for the association and this happens...
Guess we're both out of luck.

lines.st: (The Unlucky)

lines.x: What's going on here, Sommer?
I dunno either! I was just driving my truck when the engine blew a gasket, then I noticed this kid lying down by the road. Scared the hell out of me! Thought I hit her.
She fainted out in the wilds on her lonesome, kept saying she was looking for someone in a red hoodie.
I couldn't just leave her out there, so I brought her back here...
Looking for me? I've got no idea who she is, though.
Seems like an unlucky loner to me. Think we can let her stay in our camp? Considering how busy we've been lately, having another pair of hands would be nice.
You know every member of the association has to go through a strict background check, Sommer.
An Infecte—person of unknown origin like her... What if she has something to do with the famiglie?
*sigh*...
The doctor said her condition's stable, so once she's up, send her on her way.
A-Alright then.
This ragazza sfortunata sure is holding that knife of hers real tight. Someone might get hurt. Let's get that off her—
Don't touch me!
You're awake.
You—!
Lyudmila looks at the woman standing in front of her, and gives her bleary eyes a good knead or two.
Sorry... Where am I?
Nuova Volsinii's Truckers' Union.
Oh, so you saved me... Thanks.
Don't mention it. Our members aren't the type to leave an unconscious girl to fend for herself in the wilds. But now that you're awake, we can't help you much further than that.
We'll give you something to eat, but you'll have to go after you're done. See her fed, Sommer.
Thanks for the help. I'll be going, then.
No need to rush, youngster.
I wanted to ask you something, just to be safe... You're not with a famiglia, are you?
...It's been many, many years since I last heard that word.
Figured. You don't look like the sort who would have anything to do with them.
