Don't worry about it too much, Eirene's just real worried about wise guys getting their claws into the association. She's got a lot on her mind, so it's not like she has it out for you specifically.
She was the one who paid for the doctor to give you a checkup when I brought you back here, even.
Thanks. I get it, I'm not going to cause you guys trouble.
I just need some time to think... about what I should do next.
Know how to drive?
I've driven a truck in Ursus. Does that count?

lines.st: Red.

lines.x: A quiet whisper, and at the same time, an equally-gentle caress.
Like leaves gently falling on grass, the wind that reaches the leeward slopes of the wilds is free of danger.
Once, surrounded by whispers and caresses like this, she slept. She grew.
On Rhodes Island, none dared treat her this way. None could treat her this way, not even Kal'tsit.
...Grandma?!
Can't you see clearly anymore, Grandma?
Like I told you before, my eyes were devoured by wolves. These glasses are just for show.
Only once their eyes are gouged out and placed back into Grandma's sockets can I see you again, Red.
Can't you give me a hug, Grandma?
My arms were swallowed whole by wolves. You must chop off their limbs and bring them to Grandma so I can hug you again.
Can't you teach me how to sing again, Grandma?
I do wish to continue teaching you, but the wolves are always howling, and I dare not startle them.
Why did you take so long to find me, Grandma?
There are simply too many wolves, Red. They are hiding themselves along the side of the road. You must cut off their hides and make Grandma some clothes with them.
Are you really going to disappear, Grandma?
That depends on if you can help Grandma, my sweet child.
I... I found a wolf in Lungmen, a fake one. But she had the smell of a true wolf on her.
I haven't found anything new since then...
Well, actually, I haven't been looking, Grandma.
Oh, I don't blame you, Red. You're the most well-behaved, kindest child in all the land.
I'm not. I passed out on my first wolf hunt. I was too weak. Kal'tsit found me in the grass and saved me.
When I was with her, she told me about a lot of things I didn't know about.
What is 'protection'? What is 'murder'? What is 'suffering'? What is 'happiness'? What can I learn? What can I do?
There's a lot of questions, and they're all really hard. I don't get them.
'Suffering'? 'Happiness'?
It's all because I didn't take good care of you. On the contrary, it's me who needs your help...
If you are unhappy, child, you can stay on Rhodes Island.
I don't have much time left. You can forget Grandma, forget your life as a wolf hunter...
Red opens her eyes.
The windows and door to her room are shut tight. Not even a wisp of wind can find its way inside.
This is the third time this night she has awoken to the same dream.
Grandma needs me...
(Wiggles ears forcefully)
I promised Kal'tsit. If I want to leave Rhodes Island, I need to tell her.
But Kal'tsit's not here. She went to a place called Tremons or something. A city far, far away.
I should wait—No, I can't wait. Grandma can't wait.
(Sniffs in alarm)
I smell a wolf.
Not many true wolves left. They're here.
Hurting Grandma is just a 'game' to them.
And the rules of my game says I should kill them.
...Grandma is still waiting for me.
'For this extraordinary night, I sharpen my claws!'
'For a swift hunt, I bare my fangs!'
'For unrestrained festivities, I fight to the very last!'
'Only when the last one of us stands can the game reach its end!'
'Why are we this way?'
'Because we are wolves!'
The acting is pretty meh, but it's always nice to support street art! Check out these lights, n' the decor! And those tunes—
Emperor? What are you doing here?
Hey, you here too, ain't you, wolf pup?
A party like this needs a genius artist like me! No shit I'm here!
Take a good look, Agnese, these kids know a party way better than you.
Did you come here for the party or for Agnese?
Agnese might've taught you a lot, kid, but did she never tell you a party's a good place to see old friends?
Emperor here with that hyper-killer stare♪ Friends list full at one hundred no matter where♪ ...Grab a gun, shoot you dead, he don't care♪ Whad'dya think?
You're just singing random words...
Improvisation! It's called improv, girl! Agnese, don't tell me you didn't teach your kid what kinda music the people crave!
Don't bad-mouth her! Agnese's amazing, she taught me way nicer songs than yours!
Oh yeah? What else does she teach you?
Lots of things! How to light a fire, how to find places to sleep, how to hold a bow, how to use a knife, how to hunt and how to bleed my prey afterwards!
Yeesh, what's with all the fightin' and killin'...?
She also taught me about which way the wind blows, what flowers smell like, how to talk to tuskbeasts.
She taught me how to talk to humans, and how to live with them.
Y'know, you might just have the gift of the gab there, unlike that red one. She says half a word n' folks is already on edge.
Agnese also told me that the humans' carnival is lots of fun, and how I can be happy and free in the city, just like out in the wilds...
Sounds like she taught you a lot. You lucky. The other Fangs don't got a chance to learn all that.
The other Fangs...
I met the rest of the pack n' their Fangs. You listen to one word coming out they mouths n' you lose it.
*sigh* Tell you the truth, Agnese, if it wasn't for all this urgent business, I wouldn't wanna have nothin' to do with you 'Signori dei Lupi' or whatever you call yourselves.
I know, I know, that wolf pack's a bunch of stubborn, savage losers, but there ain't no gettin' out of this family reunion for the two of you.
Agnese's family...
'My will is not enough to avert the entire pack's gaze...' The hell you talkin' about, Agnese? What happened to all that 'breaking free from this road' n' 'starting a new life'?
'The game is the collective decision of the pack. Before it ends, we cannot be distracted by other matters. This is our nature...' Why're we back at that boring-ass game of yours?!

'To go against our nature is an endless climb...' Talk about a pain in the ass! Seriously, I don't wanna talk to y'all ever again. I try n' have a normal conversation, but you always gotta bring it back to 'nature' or whatever.
Agnese said 'this game must have a winner'.
You dumbass mangy mutt, I take back everything good I ever said about you!

Forget it. You taught this kid so much about the outside world. Don't tell me you still thinkin' about sendin' her into a deathmatch with the other Fangs? You didn't ask me to get her to Rhodes Island just so she could die today, did you?
If you so worried about keepin' her safe, then get her ass back to Rhodes already. Sheesh, y'all keep runnin' around for no good reason!
Rhodes Island...
Whoa! When the hell did these tuskbeasts come? Who're you tryna scare, flashin' your grills like that?!
They're scared. They can smell the Fangs.
I smell them too.
The wolves are here.
Hey, what the—! What's the big idea, shoving that freezing can of salt soda in my face?! Scared the crap outta me!
You get way too into Lyudmila's stories.
So, what happened next?
This Talulah lady, did Crownslayer manage to kill her in the end?
No. Crownslayer didn't kill her.
Shame. Imagine staying by Talulah's side the entire time but never realizing your so-called leader was an evil dragon spewing lies... A lot of lives could've been saved.
Say, if this Crownslayer didn't kill Talulah or the Emperor of Ursus, why take that name?
Easy for you to say. We're talking about an emperor and an evil dragon here. You ever killed anyone, Romi?
Wait, you couldn't have, could you? I mean, when you worked for the Borgias...
I've never done any of the sort.
I was just their night driver. They're not gonna ask a guy like me to kill someone.
Considering how crappy your driving is, would the dons really have picked you to drive them around at night?
Night driving... This some sort of famiglia lingo I don't know?
It means exactly what it sounds like, driving at night! You're talking like a police officer! I'm tellin' ya, it's all in the past now.
Now that you mention it, Romi, you've been getting more tickets than usual lately.
It's Carnevale. The whole city's packed full of both people and cars. You know that, man.
And just so you know, I used to drive around a nomadic city full of ruins while trying to hide from a Catastrophe!
Still stuck in one of Lyudmila's stories, huh?
Speaking of, those stories are just ones you heard while in Ursus, right? You go into so much detail, it's almost like they were actually about you...
Look at you guys, drinking the soda she bought you, listening to her stories, and now bothering her with questions. Didn't your mom ever teach you manners?
Got me there, Sommer. Sorry, Lyudmila.
It's fine.
You know you don't need to treat us every week, right?
How long has it been since you came to our camp? Three months? Four?
Not too sure myself, but it's been a while now.
We pay you a wage, but you always end up giving it all back to us. Why not listen to my advice, save some for the future so you can start a business?

You've all got families to feed. Me? I'm flying solo. Don't have anything else to spend that money on anyway. Besides, I'm one ragazza sfortunata. Even if I save it all, it's likely to sprout wings and fly away.
Oh, don't be like that...
Look, it's just two crates of salt soda.
I actually wanted to treat you guys to some beer for the weekend.
But since you guys will be busy with that film shoot at Court Square tonight, you'll just have to make do with soda.
If you're still thirsty, feel free to take more.
I'll have a couple more, then!
Here. Not gonna try one yourself, considering you're the one that bought them?
Oh, didn't realize you haven't had one yet...
Just how many bottles are you trying to have, man?!
Alright, I'm sorry, I'm sorry!
...I'm good.
Look, everyone knows, Lyudmila.
Though I wouldn't say we don't completely mind. To tell you the truth, when I took off your mask... It scared the hell out of me.
We just understand that... as an Infected, you're probably more worried than us about it spreading around.
Don't blame Romi and the others for pestering you with questions. They don't mean anything bad about it. They just want to get to know you a little better.
Don't be a stranger, Lyudmila.

The setting sun dyes the grass of the camp and the drivers' faces a deep red. Sommer leans against the back of his truck, stretching his hand out to her. The other drivers look at her, nary a hint of wariness on their smiling faces.
Can you get drunk off salt soda? The Reproba wonders as she furrows her brow.
After a short moment of silence, she lowers her mask and takes the proffered bottled of soda from him.
Alright, it's nearly showtime. Time to pack things up and drive over to Court Square.
Eirene was real clear about how strict the filming schedule is, even stricter than our dispatch timetables. We can't be late, or we'll be causing City Hall a whole lot of trouble.
What about Eirene herself?
Said she wanted to go talk with Judge Lavinia about qualification audits for new drivers.
Oh...
Oh yeah, Sommer, you're really not coming along?
It's easy, you just need to drive your truck and follow along, make a big scene. City Hall's even paying us more than a day's wage to do it.
I-I've been driving a little too much lately. Now that I've finally got some spare time, I wanted to catch up on some Zs.
How 'bout you, Lyudmila?
I've got something to take care of, so I'll be hanging back too.
Suit yourself. We'll be off then.
*phew* Finally...
Once the convoy of trucks has left and night slowly washes over the camp, Sommer, all on his lonesome, goes to his own truck.
He starts the engine, but before he even closed the door, a figure was already sat in the passenger seat, seat belt fastened and all.
L-Lyudmila...
Thought you were going to catch some Zs?
Not much of a night drive with an empty passenger seat, yeah?
Lyudmila, I really...
What was it you said again? Don't be a stranger, Sommer.
...That's the gist of it, Lyudmila. Told you everything I know.
What're you delivering for the Venezias?
It's a batch of tires tonight.
It's usually stuff for making vehicles. Originium engines, steel, alloys, things like that. At times there's also frozen fins, frozen meat, booze, meds...
It's pretty common in other Siracusan cities. Goods the famiglie buy don't have to go through normal customs, so they save a lot on taxes. But here in Nuova Volsinii—
I get it. No need to go into detail.
Alright, alright.
This isn't your first time, is it?
Err... Lyudmila, did you hang back from joining tonight's film shoot because of me?
What do you think?
When did you figure me out?
Why the hell d'you keep following us?
Look, sorry, but Ruggiero and I are really busy here! Would you mind leaving?
So busy you can't even stop for a cup of coffee?
C'mon, Sommer, Ruggiero, lighten up!
This is Nuova Volsinii! We're all citizens here, and we're going to be rubbing shoulders for quite a while longer. Gotta talk to one another, y'know?
How about we set up a caffè across from your camp? With that many drivers, business would be booming, and it'd be easy for us to meet up—
The hell's going on *cough* *cough* Where's all this smoke coming from? My eyes, gah—
Merda! Which of you stronzi hit me?!
Ouch—
Sommer! Ruggiero!
W-We don't know who did it either! We're all the way over here, and we haven't moved an inch...
And you know we can't do Arts...
I heard the reason why this plate was put out on the border of the city, and why City Hall didn't do anything with it until they let us set up our camp, is 'cause it used to—
S-Stop talking crap already. I'll let you two off this time.
...Now I remember.

We never had a chance to know you better until you helped us out of that bind that time. Everyone started to talk to you, asking you about what the hell your mysterious Arts are, and your experiences in Ursus...
A famiglia associate's not going to come looking for you just because they 'don't like the look of your face'.
They kept pestering you after that. Why didn't you tell me, Sommer?
Does Eirene know?
(Shakes head)
I need to get Guri her treatment and bring her over to Nuova Volsinii. I need this money, Lyudmila.
And what use would telling you have been? They're a pack of rabid wolves who won't give up until they catch their prey. I'm not going to bring that sort of trouble onto the association.
We're a band of brothers and sisters living together. But we're just ordinary people, nothing more.
The faces of the drivers flash through Lyudmila's mind one after another. She doesn't say anything.
Hey, you didn't tell Lyudmila, did you?
Don't worry, she's in her tent right now!
She's never taken off that mask since she arrived here, and she sometimes frowns when... Breaks my heart, it does.
You're telling me. I've been holding back from smoking these past few days she's been around. It's killing me.
Quick, light up already—
L-Lyudmila...
Scusa, scusa! We're about to do a night delivery, so we were just gonna have a pre-drive smoke to pick ourselves up—or not! No smoking is fine! We'll put them out right away!
Err... If you want to smoke, go ahead. No need to hide it from me.
Huh?
Well, everyone thought you wore the mask because you've got a weak throat or something... Your crystals are in a pretty peculiar place, you know. Us drivers smoke a lot, so we thought...
You're overthinking things.
Well, we just hope you like it here, Lyudmila.
You'd better get off and wait at that intersection coming up. We're about to arrive, and there'll be trouble with the Venezias if I show up with a rando in tow.
Lyudmila...
Enough. I got on your truck, so I'll stick with you all the way.
Thanks, Lyudmila.
No matter what happens, I'm lucky to have you with me.
Hmmhmm♪ Hmmhmmhmm♪ Hmm♪
You've been listening to this song on loop for a while now. Not tired of it?
Of course not!
'A Chance Encounter' is an amazing song, and Sora's got one hell of a singing voice!
It's the hottest new single this week. There was an interview saying she recorded it just for Carnevale.
'A chance encounter'... Now I'm not a cultured man, but these words... Don't you think they're about people like us?
You're going to leave, aren't you, Lyudmila?
Eh? What?
You said you didn't have any family or friends left in Siracusa, and no place to go. Everyone at camp will be happy to have someone as interesting as you stick around.
Eirene and I have talked it over before, but I feel like you're not going to stay with us at the Union forever...
There's doubt in your eyes, and hate, but I don't know who they're for.
Do you still remember what you were like when you first saw Eirene?
I don't remember anything worth mentioning.
I woke up in the camp, and you and Eirene were standing in front of me...
The moment you saw her, your expression changed in an instant... Though it went back real quick.
You thought she was someone else, didn't you?
I might have been a bit light-headed when I woke up.
A Lupo woman wearing a red hoodie, protective gear around her neck... Eirene really does remind me of someone I hate.
Doesn't matter if it's in Yan or Siracusa, every time I run into that girl, I get struck by a bout of bad luck.
So that's how it is.
I'm not going to ask you if those stories of Ursus are ones you heard from elsewhere or ones you experienced yourself, Lyudmila. No need for us to argue over who's more unlucky in life, either...
This really is a chance encounter between two sfortunati, haha.
Eirene always says life will show how much it cares for people with bad luck, it's just running a bit late is all.
Lyudmila says nothing in response. As the truck turns past the intersection, she turns her head to look out the window on a whim—
That's—
Cazzo! The steering wheel's not working all of a sudden—
Sommer, look out! There's a car over there—
True wolf. Finally found you.
You will not get away.
Hey! Can't you see there's people in there? Your knife and my arrow blew out their tires. Shouldn't we save them?
Oh.
You're right, Agnese. We shouldn't get distracted during a hunt.
A splitting headache pounds against the inside of Lyudmila's skull.
She yanks off her seat belt and pushes open the truck door, falling from her seat as she does. As her face plants into the ground, she sees a familiar red silhouette fade down the intersection.
*cough* *cough*...
Before the dizziness disperses, before she can take stock of the situation, before she can check on Sommer... Rage. An unending rage washes over her like the tide.
What the hell's with this bad luck?! How come it's you again?!
You goddamn lupetta!
I've handled tougher fights in the Siracusa of old with many more people on the other end.
But I'm always the last one standing. No exceptions.
Don't treat humans like beasts. Your hunting skills won't work on me.
You can certainly continue to try. Same goes for you, 'Projekt Red'.
I'll find you.
My blade... not sharp enough...
Come.
You can't hide from me forever. I won't wait any longer for Vargo's answers.
I lost. To the Vulpo out of nowhere.
Damned pup!
Think you can just walk away after I finally found you?
You again?!
Lyudmila brandishes her knife once again, but Projekt Red nimbly dodges it. A heavy kick immediately slams into her back. Her arm is pinned down, and her knife clatters onto the ground.
By the time she can react, she is already being pressed down into the dirt.
Ugh—Agh—
The rough scraping of her face against the ground bring a memory to the forefront of her mind—
In the alleys of Lungmen, she was held down by the same damnable wolf pup, unable to resist then either.
The same sorry figure pressed against the ground, the same pain shooting through her body.
Gah! My arm! Let go of me! Pizdets! Vaffanculo!
You stronza! I nearly had my revenge in Lungmen. Why'd you have to get between me and Kal'tsit?!
Maestro couldn't fight any more, so why'd you kill her?!
Why the hell do you keep showing up over and over again?!
Why can't I kill you?! Why?!
(Bares teeth in irritation)
...I remember you.
I hunt wolves. You're not a true wolf. No need to kill you.
Kal'tsit said we shouldn't kill a homeless hyena. You're in pain. Kal'tsit doesn't want me to kill you.
Grandma's waiting for me.
I don't have time to waste. I need to find the last true wolf.
Don't get in my way again. I've left Rhodes Island. I'm not listening to Kal'tsit any more...
The Lupo clad in a red hoodie stays for not a moment longer, disappearing down the depths of the alley.
Lyudmila doesn't get up. She lies against the ground, cradling her near-dislocated arm, waiting for the pain and humiliation to seep in and drown her.
She suddenly remembers Sommer's words.
This really is a chance encounter between two sfortunati, haha.
Eirene always says life will show how much it cares for people with bad luck, it's just running a bit late is all.
You sure about that, Sommer?
Sommer... Oh right, Sommer...
Dammit, I got so caught up in chasing that stupid wolf pup... I forgot Sommer's still in the truck...
I'm terribly sorry that you came all the way here for nothing, Signore.
Did she not tell you where she was going?
No, b-but please let Judge Lavinia know that Eirene is looking into her request in all earnestness.
When she came back to camp, she asked us all to inspect all the association trucks. She should be investigating the accident right now—
Alright, calm down. I'm not here about that.
Eh? Then—Oh, Lyudmila, do you know where Eirene went?
Why're you being so sneaky? Nearly didn't notice you...
How'd your clothes get so torn up? Did you get in a fight?
...I tripped.
Are you Lyudmila?
I have something for you.
It should really be sent to Eirene so she can notify you, but since she's not here, giving it directly to you should work too.
What is it?
A citizenship certificate and identity documentation.
You passed City Hall's and the court's background checks, and you've accumulated enough citizen points...

From this day onwards, you are a legal citizen of Nuova Volsinii and formally registered in the Truckers' Union registrar of drivers, and are entitled to all rights stipulated in the New Urban Management Act.
Haha, finally!
You treated us to salt soda a day too early, Lyudmila!
Surprised? We are aware of your *ahem* special circumstances.
You still need to undergo regular Oripathy checkups. If your infection worsens, we will re-evaluate your treatment plan.
The Truckers' Union holds a special position in this city. At first, it would have been quite difficult for you to pass the background checks...

Eirene actually came over to City Hall and courthouse many times over this matter. She even made herself your guarantor, and many of your fellow drivers followed her and signed off your materials, too.
Oh, don't look at me like that, Lyudmila. We couldn't just let you treat us to food and drink forever.
Lyudmila?
Look, no need to push her too hard, she's not the talkative sort. Why, when I got my cert and documents, I was probably way more shocked than she is.
I understand. According to our data, the uncle who took you in as a child was involved in a turf war, and his entire family was murdered. To be honest, everyone's got a story like that...
After the founding of Nuova Volsinii, many Siracusans once under the yoke of the famiglie rushed over, obtaining new identities and breaking free of their influence.
Many mafiosi have also broke free of their famiglie in this manner.
In this city, we all have the right to escape our pasts and embrace new lives.
Congratulations, Lyudmila.
Lyudmila silently stares at the certificate and documents in her hand.
'A chance encounter'... Now I'm not a cultured man, but these words... Don't you think they're about people like us?
This really is a chance encounter between two sfortunati, haha.
Eirene always says life will show how much it cares for people with bad luck, it's just running a bit late is all.
So this is how life decides to repay the unfortunate...
What's gotten into you, Lyudmila?
Has Sommer shown up?
No. Hasn't he been on break from yesterday evening up 'til now? Maybe he's picking out Carnevale presents for Guri.
Don't make excuses for him, Ruggiero.
I know what's going on with the two of you. I was riding alongside him last night.
You talking about the car crash last night?
Mhm. That was our truck.
My God...
Based on the look on your face, he hasn't come back here. Know anywhere he might go? Think quick.
There was supposed to be a delivery to the service station near Dock #3 last night. At times, we'll also head over to some abandoned warehouses under Venezia Trucking...
I've already checked them all.
The scene of the crash was already cordoned off by the time I went back, and Sommer and the truck were both gone. After that, I searched the places you mentioned. Nothing.
How could I have left him there? Dammit. If I wasn't distracted by that wolf pup, maybe...
Lyudmila...
I'll keep looking for him!
This is the only place left.
Lyudmila stands between the shipping containers, looking out at the bustling port in front of her.
Over the course of the entire day, she searched everywhere she could, but found nothing.

She was once able to make her way through Catastrophe-torn Chernobog with ease, and lead her team on precision strikes in the middle of a chaotic battlefield, yet now, she could not locate even an ordinary truck driver.
She feels like the target of some sick joke. An inkling of helplessness suddenly worms its way into her mind.
Are you saying we're sending the floats off now?
Yep, they're all assembled anyway. Send them all to the expansion plate. That's where the Carnevale parade is starting.
I knew that. What I meant was, weren't we meant to send them off at night?
That rainstorm pouring down all of a sudden earlier, remember? Not often we see such bad weather come by Nuova Volsinii. Who the hell knows if it'll rain again later today.
That's a dozen giant floats out on the move we're talking about. If they ran into that kind of weather, we'd have to do traffic control, and I'd rather not have to deal with that headache.
I suppose that makes sense.
C'mon, sbrigati, while the rain's stopped.
Oh, right, you're going to be passing by a lot of places on the way. Remember to cover those floats with tarps. City Hall wants to keep their designs a mystery until the parade.
Got it. I'll go call the boys over.
If Sommer could only go back the way he came, and he wanted to hide a truck in a port this busy... Don't tell me he...
In a corner of Nuova Volsinii's port, a dozen or so mobile cranes are hoisting up giant tarpaulins, creating a labyrinth out of thin air, sealed away from the outside.
Under the tarps, a dozen oddly-shaped giant beasts sleep silently. Ere their resurrection at Carvenale, this is their cemetery.
Lyudmila passes by them. She is not in the mood to investigate these strange constructs. She walks directly to the end of their procession and stops.
There lies a great wolf. Vast amounts of imitation fur cover the truck underneath its frame. Its tail oh-so-conveniently droops down over the eye-catching dents and gouges of the truck's carriage.
It bares its fangs in soundless snarl, guarding the secrets hidden below its body.
She pulls open the door to the truck's container. She is greeted by a pile of black tires, alongside...
This is...
What're you delivering for the Venezias?
It's a batch of tires tonight.
It's usually stuff for making vehicles. Originium engines, steel, alloys, things like that. At times there's also frozen fins, frozen meat, booze, meds...
It's pretty common in other Siracusan cities. Goods the famiglie buy don't have to go through normal customs, so they save a lot on taxes. But here in Nuova Volsinii—
Lyudmila pulls out her dagger, running it against the seam in the tire she spotted just now. She slashes at the thick rubber, revealing what lies hidden within.
Guns, staves, crossbows, even Originium explosives...
Sommer, you idiot. Did you know you were transporting this kind of 'merchandise'?
Pizdets! This is suddenly a lot more complicated than smuggling tires and crashing into the Acting Mayor! Wait, someone's coming.
It should be here somewhere.
I'll take that side, Gambino. You look here. Veloce, veloce.
Capone, did you ever think about how we're going to explain to Venezia junior where we picked up this trail, assuming we do?
I mean, surely we can't just say Lappland told us.
Actually, I'm more worried about how that crazy pazza even knew Sommer stashed his truck in the port...
What the hell is she cooking up here?
Fuhgeddaboutit! No point thinking about that crap, just get back to work—Wait, I think the polizia's outside! Hide!
Sir. It's me, Noemi.
Have you found anything on your end?
I don't drink coffee. It's too bitter.
C'mon, let's stay focused on the task at hand. I know the truck we're looking for is hidden around here somewhere! Get over here, quickly!
Right, the west side of the port, where the floats are being stored!
Wait, the dockhands are starting to...
Lyudmila hears the sound of cranes pulling away at the tarps, and the engine of the vehicle up ahead starting up.
Both the mafiosi and the police are close by. This float, its secret, Sommer, and the truck drivers' 'new lives' are about to be exposed to the light of day.
Blyat!
Lyudmila's hand brushes past her side, and she feels a damp spot in her pocket. It's the documents she folded up and stuffed in there, proof that she is now one of those idiots.
They must have gotten soaked in the rain as she ran to the port. She didn't have time to stash them away in her tent when she left camp.
Caught in the autumn rain again, she snaps back to reality. It's getting cold, she thinks.
It would be great if she could warm herself up by a fire right now.
Look at this bonfire out here among the snow, friend. Once, it could ignite the anger of the Infected. It could spark the dream of Reunion. It could set all of Ursus ablaze.
But now, all it does is keep us warm.

lines.st: 'First, learn how to light a fire. Burn down this house and everything in it, so that nobody can find us.'
'Fire hides everything, Lyudmila.'

lines.x: Lyudmila stands in a shadow not too far away, silently watching the blaze lick away at the carriage. It rises, engulfing the entirety of the great wolf.
Fire can ignite anger. It can spark a dream. It can also bury everything in ash.
And her own smoke can help conceal the fire. Before anyone else catches on, she can let it build into a towering inferno, inextinguishable.
*sigh* I'm one ragazza sfortunata.
Tsk, tsk.
We got here too late. They finished their fight and now there's no one alive to talk to.
You stopped our duel just to watch a boring shootout?
Woman and wolf stand on the barrenlands as the sun rises, with a yellow cab parked not far from them.
The cab's hood bears a deep depression from a prior intimate meeting with a boulder. Tire tracks twist and turn in its wake peppered with strewn up dirt, narrating the car's journey here...
Several bodies lie around the car. The wind carries the stench of blood far away, inciting low growls from wild beasts in the distance.
Zaaro, there are two things you ought to understand.
One: I am not a toy for your entertainment. Two: As my toy for my entertainment, I've realized an immortal Signore dei Lupi is just so boring...
Compared to you, isn't a famiglia shoot out happening in the middle of the wastes much more interesting?
Haha, the car can still move even after all that?
Where was its original destination?
Let's see... a spot circled on the map—'Nuova Volsinii', and some entry documents in the glove box to boot... Looks like we're all set!
A familiar name.
Hey, Zaaro. Help me drag these corpses aside so I can reverse the car.
You want to...
Siracusa has graced us with an invitation.
Don't tell me you'd refuse? I didn't think a Signore dei Lupi would be so rude.
I told you, I will walk with you until the victor is decided.
Then help me out!

lines.st: (The Taxi Driver)

lines.x: Salve.
Um, y-you don't have to be so formal, Signor Capone.
Relax. I just didn't wanna spook you with this scary mug of mine. After all, it wasn't easy to secure this meet-up with you.
I thought a lot about your proposal. If city hall staff discover I privately transferred my citizen points to you, will they...
Just pick a few documents from your pile of certs, write a little explanation, and change the name on 'em. Simple.
I'll earn fifty points, while you get... a lotta dough.
We ain't violatin' no laws here. If city hall got a problem, it just means the New Urban Management Act needs more revisions. I bet you know this better than me.
When you put it like that...
Identity registration's awful unfair, ain't it?

I turned over a new leaf, paid so much guarantee money the past six months and did so much community service all thanks to my criminal record. All that work, and I still haven't earned even half of the 120 points I need...

But you? A 'clean resume', and 'experience in law-adjacent tech industries'... You're pretty new here, but the points you converted from all that's already enough to become a citizen three times over!
It'd be a waste to keep 'em for yourself.
A-Alright.
I applied for an appointment next week to get my documents authenticated. I'll get these revisions done before that—
Easy, pal.
Huh? Aren't you the one wanting to become a citizen ASAP?
What I mean is, have you ever thought of puttin' off your citizenship application?
Wh-What do you mean?
Lots of us guys in the city could use a lot more points, but there ain't many guys like you who can get 'em so easy. So...
I'm happy to play middleman.
Nuova Volsinii introduced the New Urban Management Act to deter people like you—S-Sorry...
I mean, aren't we contradicting the city's founding principles by doing this?
C'mon, pal. Relax, relaaax. I was just thinkin' out loud, is all. Don't mind me.
But noodle on it a bit. Why'd you come here? A stable income? A better life?
You think you'll get that after you become a legal citizen of Nuova Volsinii and work an entry-level job at a law firm?
(Claps)
Are you going to stay here and watch them all night, Lappland?
Why the rush? How can we start our night shift before getting some food? Want a slice of this freshly baked polenta?
On second thought, let's not waste it.
You're in a good mood... Just because you witnessed that strange deal happen?
Zaaro, how long have we been here in Nuova Volsinii?
More than two months.
And how many places have we been, in that span of time? How many customers have we ferried?
Isn't it uncomfortable, seeing everyone in the city act so prim and proper?
The city hall staff and court judges are itching to slap their laws on their foreheads, ready to fight against Siracusa's old customs and rules at any moment...
While the famiglie are trying their best to prove how much they like and have adapted to this new set of game rules.
I've never seen Siracusans this restrained, this uncomfortable, like everyone has a mask sewn onto their faces.
It'll be Carnevale soon. Are they even in the mood to enjoy the so-called 'carnival'?
I'm thankful for our two friends here worrying over their citizen points for showing me Siracusa remains the same, and it's in every Siracusan's nature to turn every new territory into a swamp.
Haha, now I'm looking forward to Carnevale.
Hey, are you even listening?
I smelled their scent.
Your compatriots, the other Signori dei Lupi?
No plans to catch up with them?
A Signore dei Lupi who has left the game should no longer interfere.
Don't look so defeated, Zaaro. You did lose, but it doesn't mean you can't derive any more fun from it.
Think about it, what else can you do?
Free yourself. We returned to this swamp for a leisurely swim.
Hah...
And you? When are you planning to say hello to your past?
Carissimo padre mio, you mean?
I already said addio... though I did prepare a gift for him this time.
The cab driver stops talking, and lifts her legs onto the dashboard to rest in a more comfortable position.

Through the car window ahead is a wall sporting a huge billboard advertising a winery. A sullen-looking man with his tightly-knit brows brandishes a wine glass towards the camera, with the following invitation—
'Saluzzo Vino is looking for a senior legal advisor! Several other positions also open, apply to join us today!'
Don Alberto, we got all the information here. Please have a look.
Mm.
We have a total of thirteen wineries in this city. Six of them are undergoing a qualification audit and cannot operate for the time being.
Two of them on the city's outer plates are about to welcome their first harvest, and the warehouse, lab, and bottling line are ready...
But there's no way we can handle this many ripened grapes in this short period of time with half our wineries not operating.
The root of our problem lies with city hall's attitude towards us, those stronzi are obviously targeting us on purpose—
These files are meant for our friend here. He has the means of solving such problems.
Alberto looks across the table, where a red-headed Lupo has been sitting for a while.
The wine is finally decanted, and he takes a sip, calmly returning Alberto's gaze.
Merge and restructure all Saluzzo properties in this city into new entities, including both the operating ones and those undergoing an audit...
Then, hire non-stakeholder employees to manage on-site operations. This is feasible under the New Urban Management Act.
Simply put, I will transfer these properties to you.
Famiglia Bellone does not have large-scale businesses, so the procedure should be relatively simple. Yet you've been rejected more than ten times now.
Whenever it involves a 'Bellone' or 'Saluzzo', the government and court—or should I say, Leontuzzo and Lavinia—always tend to react in a more defensive manner.
It sounds like I have no reason to refuse this arrangement.
Demetri, what I value more is that you understand Leontuzzo better than anyone else, as well as the rules he has set—
You are the best fit for this position.
I want to see if city hall will still brazenly clamp down on us even after we tie our companies together in total compliance with the New Urban Management Act.
Don't you agree, our new Don Bellone?
Don't treat me like your puppet, Alberto.
I admit Bellone's company registration has met many bumps on the road, and your invitation was timely.
You get rid of some troubles, while I gain the rights to legally enter the city. We're just business partners getting what we want.
Even though the Bellones are not what they used to be in Siracusa anymore; even though this new city doesn't have famiglie anymore, you need to know this—
My choice to work for Saluzzo Vino does not in any way mean the Bellones will be at the Saluzzos' beck and call.
So you'd best put that title to rest. I'm not sitting here as the don of Famiglia Bellone.
There's no point in acting tough, ragazzo.
Whether or not this collaboration will mean 'The Bellones have thrown themselves at the Saluzzos'—I'm curious how Leontuzzo will see it.
Oh, almost forgot. Maybe he doesn't even care at all anymore.
He already abandoned you.
If you insist on this topic... Aren't you a lone wolf too, Alberto?
The White Wolf of Saluzzo—your daughter—who always disobeyed your orders, has left her famiglia forever.
According to my sources, she arrived in Nuova Volsinii a few months ago... as a cab driver.
I'm curious, too. Did she come here to add insult to your injury, or will she return home and help the Saluzzos?
...Lappland?
I banished her.
From what I know about her, she's probably thinking that's not how it went down.
I won't take offense from you, Demetri, you are different from Bernardo, you understand what's going on.
These harmless differences will not affect our shared interests, so why not take this as a happy start to our collaboration?
Fine, I won't waste my breath then.
Why not you sign this equity document first, Signor Alberto.
I'll use the dividends from this month to get Leon to reminisce about the good old days.
Went looking for your fellow floating heads?
I did. A hypocritical and sly wolf.
Caesar has turned her Fang into an idiot who only knows how to bite people and suck blood. She won't be able to find Vargo's Fang even if she sniffed every single corner of this land.
That Fang has never showed himself. Hidden very well.
But if we had Bernardo here, locating a Siracusan in Siracusa would be like going through a pile of hay for an animal. Simple.
If I don't interfere and help them out, god knows how long they're going to drag this round for!
Zaaro, it's rare to see so many words spill out from your mouth.
You're upsetting the table, and relishing it.
Like you are.
Esatto. Stop worrying about winning or losing, learn to do it for the love of the game, and derive even more enjoyment from it. That's what games are really for.
How do you wolves fail to even understand this...?
Nuova Volsinii nights are dark.
Woman and wolf stand atop a building, sandwiched by the night sky above and the noisy city below—
Pre-Carnevale activities happen in all sorts of shades and forms, vehicles speed through the streets, pedestrians shuttle to and fro. They enjoy a panoramic view of everything happening below.
They cover their fangs with masks, and use a festival to divulge their thirst to have it all. How sad.
On the contrary, you seem to be enjoying this farce. When did you become so interested in such boring activities?
Zitto, Zaaro. You're noisier than the people who used to hang around me before.
I'm just enjoying the breeze.
We practically have a VIP seat here. So keep quiet and wait for the show to begin.
The actors are already on the stage...
You're late, Leon.
I'm sorry, it's been a really busy day.
Still working on your inaugural speech?
Well, that too... We're planning a birthday party for an entire city. It's not quite the same as planning a party at home, is it?
I'm surprised... you still remember.
Of course I do. Did you think I was going to forget how you bought ginger ale instead of beer for the banquet, and pocketed the difference to buy a car?
Well, I guess that DID happen.
You sound... distracted, Demetri.
Do I? It's nothing. I guess it's been too long since we last saw each other.
Take care on your way, Leon.
Might have taken that as a threat, if we weren't in Nuova Volsinii.
Enough joking and hurry up. I'm waiting at the seat by the window.
The car drives by the apprentice tailor jogging on the street and heads to the end of the main street.
I should pick up the pace. I can't miss the delivery window to hand over the clothes to Signor Alberto.
The Don of Famiglia Saluzzo...
What kind of man is he?
Luchino gasps for breath with the suit in his arms as he runs through a deserted alley, illuminated only by the light coming from a cafe.
That's the fifth slice of pizza and third glass of juice that you just eliminated. Are you that hungry?
Shut up and drink this espresso.
It's so damn sour!
If we screw up this mission because this swill makes me throw up, whose head am I gonna give the boss, yours?
Live with it. I just picked it up off the shelf.
Nobody uses this frontage road anyway. Besides, it's just transporting a bunch of 'tires', what could go wrong?
No need to get all worked up about an unimportant job like this.
I'm tired of listening to your complaints for the past two months, Gambino.
Haven't you figured out our new boss yet? Trying too hard to show off in front of him will only have the opposite effect.
Fresh meat like us, we gotta be patient and wait for our chance.
Well, ain't you the model employee, Capone.
I thought you would at least show a little bit of, I don't know, shame? There's hardly any place for a Sicilian in Siracusa these days.
I'm not Sicilian anymore. It's your shame, not mine.
I just want to get my Nuova Volsinii citizenship sooner rather than later. All the work I put in over the past six months, all the guarantee money I paid, and I'm only halfway to the required points—
Halfway?! You've got fifty more points than I do? Have you been doing—*gasp*—community service while I wasn't looking?
Shut your crap, Gambino. We're on our last chance and you know it.

Keep your eyes sharp on the street. It should be coming any time now—
You got any pizza left? Give me one.
Cazzo, it's cold, how do you even manage to eat this...
As the two grumble to the other, a dark shadow quietly slips through the alley.
*sniff*
This is the smell. True wolf.
A truck drives past Red and attracts her attention.
Are you hiding inside?
*sniff*
No. Hm, over there?
She looks toward the opposite end of the street.
Grandma, I am a wolf hunter. I remember.
The girls?
Vargo, I hope you left me accurate instructions on how to identify them.
*sigh*... It'll be a headache if I hurt the wrong people.
With blade in hand, the Vulpo leans on a wall hidden within its shadow, waiting in silence for the prime opportunity to strike.
She will eliminate the two targets who threaten Umberto's life in one go, and then demand her reward from the wolf.
Tsk. A kid loitering around the streets outside this late?
(That figure... was I just seeing things?)
Lyudmila, Lyudmila... why did you space out so suddenly?
It's nothing... Just a little tired.
You'd better get off and wait at that intersection coming up. We're about to arrive, and there'll be trouble with the Venezias if I show up with a rando in tow.
Lyudmila...
Enough. I got on your truck, so I'll stick with you all the way.
Thanks, Lyudmila.
No matter what happens, I'm lucky to have you with me.
Hmmhmm♪ Hmmhmmhmm♪ Hmm♪
You've been listening to this song on loop for a while now. Not tired of it?
Of course!
'A Chance Encounter' is an amazing song, and Sora's got one hell of a singing voice!
It's the hottest new single this week. There was an interview saying she recorded it just for Carnevale.
Sommer hums to the song looping from the speakers.
Soon, the truck will reach the turning point ahead and bump into the other car.
Carnevale's curtains are slowly pulled open.

In just one city block, we have our young and promising Acting Mayor, Fangs, people coming home for the festivities, and Sicilians too—I don't even know who they're seeking protection from...what a crowd.
I could make it even noisier.
Just wait, Zaaro. You don't have to do anything.
Pretend you're a cab driver who turns on the radio at the right time to tune into a story.
The games humanity plays are much more interesting than your kind's preference to select just a few people to fight each other.
Caesar's pup has discovered Agnese's cub.
Look! Isn't there another car coming from the port?
—'Bang!'
The truck crashes into the car, and flees in a panic. Eye-watering flames, hysterical crowds.
Those who share the same stage, obviously perform the same play.
Just a chaotic show. I don't see what's so fun about this, Lappland.
What's with the rush?
Before the play truly begins, allow me to perform my duty as an upstanding citizen of the city.
Pronto. Buonasera, is this the police?
Me? Just a driver-for-hire. I'd like to make a report.
Si... the port district. That's right, an accident just happened.
Buongiorno, dear residents.

After several months of preparation, Carnevale will be held as scheduled on the evening of November 9th, where hundreds of thousands of citizens will be on the streets celebrating the one-year anniversary of Nuova Volsinii.
According to the Carnevale organizing committee, all the beast floats have been assembled at the port, and will be moved to the expansion plate today in preparation for the night parade.
The street theater competition has also concluded. The most popular team will gain the privilege of illuminating the floats on the behalf of every citizen here on Carnevale night...
Other shortlisted plays will also be performed on both sides of the parade path alongside the floats.

The committee hopes that everyone will cooperate and maintain the floats' mystery before their forms are unveiled on Carnevale night! Please rein in your curiosity, and do not sneak a look, photograph or spread any float-related information!
In other news: On November 5th, a traffic accident occurred on Quinta Strada in the port district late at night with one injury reported. The vehicle responsible escaped after crashing.
The police quickly got the scene under control, and restored order in the area. The incident did not have any serious consequences, so there is no need for our dear citizens to worry.

Presently, the police have established a special task force to investigate and identify the vehicle responsible, and the case should be closed within the next few days. Citizens who discover any relevant clues should contact the police as soon as possible...
Texas.
You're back.
Just a brief visit, I'll go back to court later.
How's Leon?
The doctor came here three times... only for routine check-ups though—at least his condition isn't getting worse, I suppose?
I'm guessing you had trouble dealing with that case yesterday?
The case was closed... in a way I didn't want, with a way I was powerless to stop.
Texas, I don't recall you having a habit of tuning into the afternoon news.
The situation isn't as optimistic as the news make it out to be, right?
We found some clues and locked onto some suspects, but we haven't made any other progress.
Who called the police that night?
The info we got was all fake. Hard to investigate.
Maybe I could help? I know quite a few people in the city, those from the famiglia...
Grazie, Texas... I know you've been keeping an eye on the whole thing.
Your mere presence in Volsinii was enough to strike a nerve in every influence in the city. Some were angry, while others got a wake-up call, and we managed to turn things around.
But a year has passed, and we're in Nuova Volsinii now. In a new city without the famiglie, the 'Last Texas' can't really do much.
In other words, the 'Last Texas' shouldn't be allowed to do much here.
...Yes.
Signorina Texas, there's a phone call at reception for you.
Me?
Who is this?
Haha. Need a ride, Texas?
I won't wait too long.
The sun was so strong the last two days. What's with the sudden heavy downpour?
You wouldn't be carrying an umbrella if you were really surprised.
Here, over here.
On the hood of a cab, and under a huge black umbrella, the driver casually plays with the car keys in her hand.
Haha, you got here quickly.
Da quanto tempo, Texas.
The driver slowly lifts her umbrella, revealing a sharp crescent—an unrestrained smile. She then nonchalantly tosses the umbrella aside and salutes a greeting to Texas.
Lappland.
The weather got me thinking about our old home. The nights and rain in Volsinii.
Yeah. The weather here has always been great until today.
But you seem to have seen this rain coming, Texas.
I just prepare for the worst. We're still in Siracusa after all. Having just one rainy day after so many days of fine weather is pretty rare.
What exactly happened with that accident?
You mean the one with the Bellone wolf pup? Hahahaha. Surely you're not suspecting that I did it with my car right here?
I wouldn't be surprised if it was you.
You wouldn't be here right now if you had nothing to do with it.
...I do know a few things.
Willing to have a ride with me?
Hmph. Why not? I wouldn't be standing here otherwise.
You know, I thought you'd be nice and shade me from the rain with your umbrella.
You seem like you're enjoying the rain. Didn't want to spoil your fun.
Hahaha, that's the Texas I know.
Are you going to just stay on your car like that? In such a big rain with no spectators? Not tired?
Oh? Haven't you been watching me the whole time?
Get in the car, Texas. Our destination is quite far away, so it'll be quite a long drive.
Right, I prepared a gift for you in the passenger seat.
I saw the snacks... I don't have the appetite right now.
It's alright, it's not millefoglie this time.
I've long changed my tastes.
Though I know you're still obsessed with those old habits of yours.
Texas? Back so soon?
It's me.
...Demetri?
You shouldn't have clearance to come in here.
There's always a way. You can't prevent me from seeing Leon forever.
I brought some flowers. Hmm, is there a vase in here that might fit?
We used to have roses like this at the Bellone residence, remember?
Don't look at me like that, Lavinia.
The police have thoroughly investigated my travel itinerary on your orders for the past two days, every single detail.
But they found no clues linking me to that accident.
I'm not so stupid to think you'd commit a crime yourself.
My city hall colleagues told me how they recently received an entity ownership change application from Saluzzo Vino... Why are you collaborating with Alberto, Demetri?
Since this city—or I should say, you—have been so unwelcome, I had to figure out my own way to stay here.
Doesn't it comply with the city rules?
...Perfectly.
Then, wouldn't it be better if I stayed within your sights?
You've already deployed enough officers here to watch every step I take, any contact I make, every order I give.
I should be allowed a visit.
I was the one nursing Leon back to health whenever he was sick.
The Bellones won't give up trying to enter the city, Lavinia.
But in any case, there is no need or reason for me to hurt Leon.
I'll accept your flowers in his place.
But I'm sorry. As an officer of the law, I will not allow a potential suspect to meet the victim in private.
Leon's safety is closely tied to the city's.
No one can say where the chaos will come from next.
—This is what you wanted me to see?
I see the rain earlier wasn't heavy enough.
Fire.
The speeding cab suddenly brakes when it nears the fire, leaving clear skid marks on the still-wet ground.
Would you believe me if I said I brought you here to extinguish the fire?
The monstrous wolf breathes flames and thick smoke into the air.
The giant beast-shaped float burns, the bright fire light standing out against the dull dark sky.
Lappland... how much do you know about this?
Suspecting me?
No. You would have chosen a more classical and conspicuous method.
You'd be standing in the fire right now at least, not next to me.
Haha, you know me so well.
Too bad the workers and police are pretty quick to act. I think the fire'll be put out quite soon.
They should be thanking that burst of heavy rain earlier, otherwise the fire might have already burned down Nuova Volsinii Port to cinders.
No matter how big the fire is, it doesn't change the fact that it's here in the city.
Leontuzzo and the others wanted to give the new city a brand new festival to help build a collective identity.
A car accident that left the Mayor in critical condition and a giant blaze breaking out at the logistics port just before Carnevale. That's enough to keep the civilians up awake at night.
Doesn't it feel like some kind of omen, a proclamation, or even a provocation?!
How much do you actually know, Lappland? What's your objective?
Tsk, tsk. You sound anxious—Why?
Wasn't Nuova Volsinii the proving grounds Signora Sicilia promised to the youths?
An experiment is bound to produce mixed results.
As the first witness on the scene, I can tell you for certain the Bellone pup's car crash was purely an accident.
You've been watching over him at the hospital, and his condition has already stabilized, right?
So what's causing your anxiety?
I look forward to your answer, Texas... at Carnevale.
No matter what you're planning...
I'll be keeping an eye on you for Lavinia.
Hahaha, then sit tight.
I have no interest in wasting my time with those officers.
H-How can that be?!
The parade float... got lit up on fire along with the goods—
Ugh—?!
Calm down, it's me. The police are on their way here, so stay out of their sight.
L-Lyudmila?
Where did you go the night before yesterday? No, wait. Why are you here?
I have more questions than you, Sommer.
Where did you go after the accident?
I-I went to find Eirene. She knocked me out cold and then locked me inside the warehouse—Well, a lot happened, and it was a hell of an escape.
*sigh* Come with me. Everyone's attention is focused on the float now. My smoke will cover our tracks, and no one'll spot us.
Why's there a burn spot on your jacket?
The fire... was y-your...
My doing.
The police would have discovered the truck and its contents if I hadn't done it there and then. It was the only way.
Enough, we'll talk when we're safe. Those officers—
W-Wait, Lyudmila. Someone just went by over there... Eirene?!
Who's the guy next to her?!
Eirene Lavazza, and Alberto Saluzzo. Could you both please kindly explain what brings you here just two hundred meters away from the fire?
I don't see a need to explain myself.
If you want my answer, have the judge bring the evidence and arrest warrant to me first.
I'll be waiting.
We'll get to it, Signor Saluzzo.
It's true we don't have any evidence to pin you as the culprit, but this doesn't mean you're completely unrelated to the case.
As for you, Signorina Eirene Lavazza.
The float burned nearly to nothing, but we managed to collect some fragments.
We'll send them for analysis later, but we're basically certain that the vehicle used for the float was a truck from your group.
As the head of the Truckers' Union, you'll have to come with us for our investigation.
...Signor Saluzzo.
Signorina Lavazza, our previous agreement still stands.
I believe you have no link to the matter, but do be careful about what you say once you leave this place.
Otherwise, even I won't be able to help you.
Don't you find this place familiar, Texas?
But this time, the one standing in the defendant's dock is neither you or me...
What a shame.
Even if we take the judge's word that this case is suspected of endangering the city's public safety, there's no reason to let all of these people in here.
A trial for a truck driver, and the audience gallery seats are filled with made men from two famiglie with conflicting interests.
Hahaha. I'm liking Nuova Volsinii more and more, Texas.
But I have to say, this trial was put together more quickly than I thought.
Probably a special procedure. Carnevale is tomorrow, so it's understandable they'd want to settle the case ASAP.
Do you know this Eirene woman?
You don't have to probe me, Texas.
I only met this poor little driver once, but I can guess how she ended up here.
You mean, she's innocent?
I didn't say that.
Isn't this scene a regular occurrence in the swamp of Siracusa?
It swallows everyone up eventually. Not everyone can escape it like you did...
Either you get killed and silenced, or you hide within the mud and drag out a meaningless existence.
Texas, do you believe that Nuova Volsinii offers a third way of life?
Silence.
In accordance with the New Urban Management Act, the Nuova Volsinii criminal court will commence with the Nuova Volsinii Port Arson Public Prosecution Case, number 00278. Court is in session.
Defendant, please state your identity for the record.
Eirene Lavazza. Born in Sicilia, currently a member of the Truckers' Union and living in the Trucker Camp on Viale Alito, Zona Torino.
What did they mean our testimony would be invalid in court?
Does that mean we can only sit here and wait for the results of her trial?
I wanted to go even more than you do, Lyudmila. After all, it's because of me that Eirene...
But you heard about what happened at the police station, right? She didn't say anything... so we can't just barge in there ourselves.
She believes in the government and the courts, and thinks she can prove her innocence...
Do you believe that'll happen?
I believe in her.
Wait for me here, Sommer. I'll call the other guys to go with us. We should be standing by her side right now no matter what.
Sommer slumps over on the ground cradling his head, watching Lyudmila leave.

If he hadn't helped the Venezias transport their goods without telling Eirene, if that car accident hadn't happened, if he hadn't asked Eirene for help, if he had managed to break free from his chains earlier and make it to the port in time...
He's the reason why this incident happened, but now the one standing in court awaiting trial is his best friend, and his... daughter.
He gropes around in a pocket for a business card. He doesn't know who stuck it in the gaps of his campervan door.
The card only has an address on it—'Terza Zona Porto'.
Eirene, I know you outsmart me in most cases...
But this time, I know I can help you out for sure...
He stands up and leaves the campground alone.
Eirene Lavazza. Identity matches registered information.
The blaze at Zona Porto yesterday caused serious property damage, put the lives of dozens of workers at the port at risk, and gravely threatened the public safety of Nuova Volsinii.
Carnevale is right around the corner, and this incident will immeasurably affect public opinion.
The court has examined the evidence submitted by the police, and found that the great wolf parade float, the source of the fire, has obvious traces of arson.
The vehicle was from the Truckers' Union. The police reviewed the trucks' shift schedules and found out it was not deployed at that time, and should not have been in Porto Nuova Volsinii.
You, head of the Truckers' Union, also just happened to be at the scene of the crime—
Signorina Lavazza, you are our prime suspect. You have every right to defend yourself.
Your Honor, I'm happy to tell you everything I know.
Eirene calmly explains how yesterday's fire had no links to any one from the Truckers' Union.
But the judge's expression becomes even gloomier.
The judge can't help her, even if he wants to.
Of course. As long as the real culprit doesn't show up, her attempt to defend herself will be for naught.
*sigh* Woe, the poor lady! Don't you want to help her out, Texas?
You could stand up and vouch for her, etcetera. The court might be lenient on her if it's you.
I know what you're thinking, Lappland.
Nuova Volsinii's laws absolutely cannot be trampled upon or scorned like that. They strove to make these rules a reality.
Then I fear you can only sit back and watch everything unfold, or...
Lappland sends Texas a provocative look, eager to try something.
I could care less for your boring rules. Want me to help you out?
*ahem* Please hold on. The court has just received a supplementary list of new witnesses who would like to provide testimony.
Uh... A-Alberto Saluzzo?
What's wrong, Your Honor? As a legal citizen who has completed his identity registration in total compliance with the New Urban Management Act, surely my testimony will be valid in court?
O-Of course. Please proceed to state your testimony for the record.
Signor Saluzzo, are you intending to provide evidence to the court to prove that Eirene was indeed related to the fire?
Alberto does not answer. His gaze falls on the opposite site of the witness box. Venezia Junior and the other Venezias are sitting on the left side of the audience.
Alberto's eyes turn to the seat behind Venezia Junior—there sits his own daughter, the banished Saluzzo.
She crosses her legs, grins, and waves to him as if no one else is in the room.
Next to her is Texas.
(Ciao.)
Texas, my dear father is watching us. Stop being so reserved and wave along.
Signore? Signor Saluzzo? Please continue with your testimony.
Hmph.
I was there as well, and I can attest that Signorina Lavazza did not start the fire.
Terza Zona... should be here...
Sommer?
The boss made us wait here for you.
Come. He wants to see you.
Are you from the Venezias? Or the Saluzzos? Or the Medicis...?
Tch.
This'll help Eirene, right?
Right up ahead.
The container in that corner. Just open the door and go in.
Alright.

lines.st: Eirene. I'm sorry for not telling you, but I already made arrangements for Guri.
You know how I kept asking for more shifts and saved quite a bit of cash. It should at least be enough to pay for two years of Oripathy treatment and her school fees.
The school isn't too far from our camp, so it'll be easier to look after her this way too.

I did take you in at the start, but compared with all the help you've given us all these years it's hard to say who has helped the other more. Eirene, it's not fair they've got a spotlight on you now.
It's time you think about yourself too, haha.
I wanna say I've never been much of an idealist. After all, I've seen enough in this life, I know very well what I can do or can't...
I'm just a tiny supermarket owner and driver who hopes we can all live a peaceful life... this is also why I was willing to follow you here.

The shop's no more, but we can always move to a new place and figure out a new way of life. There's no need to waste our time on those guys who ruined our lives. Everyone gets their share of bad luck, so there's no need to blame anyone.
We just have to stay far away from the troubles.
Fortunately, you helped us all find a good spot.
I bet Guri will like it here.

lines.x: I guarantee you my testimony is absolutely true. As I said earlier, she was with me the whole time from yesterday before noon until the fire happened at the port.
Two Saluzzo Vino vineyards will soon welcome our first harvest. I hope the Truckers' Union will allocate a fleet to help transport our fresh grapes.
This is why we were at the port to check things out. My employees can vouch for us as well.
(Sounds of agreement)
In other words, Signorina Lavazza has a strong alibi.
(Claps) Magnifico.
My astute padre has tied the Truckers' Union to the Saluzzos.
That poor driver will have to do his bidding in future.
I don't think it'll end just like that.
The truck came from your union. We checked the shipping schedule and it wasn't slated with any arrangements for the last two days. So why did it show up at the port and become one of the floats?
Signorina Lavazza, even if you truly did not start the fire, as the head of your union it is your responsibility to explain the illegal usage of this car.
I can testify for Signorina Lavazza on that point.
Signor Venezia?
Your Honor, please take a look at the customs declaration form in the supplementary evidence you received earlier. The truck was transporting tires for Venezia Trucking.
This is it. Go in.
He's waiting.
The shipping container door lies ajar, and all Sommer can see is a faint light flickering inside the dark interior.
So, who on Terra is helping me... and Eirene?
Enough questions, vecchio.
Go in and you'll find out at once.
You want to help that driver, and so do we. That's all you need to know.
Sommer puts his hand on the door, and hesitates.
Can you guarantee—
There is no one available to answer him. The mafiosi already left, leaving him on his own.

lines.st: Eirene, I don't have brains or guts like yours.
Which is why I was so surprised but also worried when you promised Judge Lavinia and Mayor Leontuzzo you'd be the head of the union.
It meant we were going to get into tons of trouble... and sure enough we did.
But back then you told me what we were starting wasn't actually a 'union' to help city hall handle the famiglie, but an 'association' to provide mutual aid to anyone in a similar situation as us.
You always say I was the one who took you in, but we all know it was you who gave me another chance at life.
I'm not saying we're even now, I owe you so much.
Eirene, if a day comes where I can help you out, I won't hesitate at all.
I don't want Guri to think her papa as an ungrateful person.
I tried to be a good role model for her.

lines.x: Signor Venezia, I'm sure you're clear what your statement means, right?
Signor Saluzzo claims he submitted testimony out of his responsibility as a citizen, but this incident actually involves Venezia Trucking.
The demand for our vehicles here has spiked in the last two months because of Carnevale, and we had to order an impromptu shipment of automotive consumables from the other cities.

This newly-arrived batch of goods has already cleared custom, and the Truckers' Union was helping us do a last-mile delivery. It was an impromptu request, which is why they haven't updated this job to their schedule.
Signorina Lavazza, do correct me if I got anything wrong.
You're right, we do have the paper trail, if needed.
As for why that truck was found at the port amongst the parade floats, we don't know who did it yet, so I implore the police to continue their investigation.
Though at least, the Truckers' Union did not use their truck illegally, and Signorina Lavazza was merely a bit negligent in this whole affair.
We'll verify the information.
Oh, the poor driver's pretty lucky. We're so close to the end of the tunnel.
The pivotal testimonies from my father and Venezia Junior are enough to clear Eirene of suspicion. Nothing will happen to her.
But the suspect has now become an outsider.
Eirene's the actual person involved, but she can't even get a word in. The courtroom's turned into an exclusive stage for the Venezias and Saluzzos.
These two factions are clearly fighting under the table, but they seem to have a tacit understanding once they're in court.
Everyone knows that's how the mafia works.
They want to claim complete innocence, and seize the Truckers' Union for themselves at the same time.
Of course. After all, we haven't found the actual person who started the fire.
Unless... they'll never find the person ever again.
Hahaha, the famiglie never consider what the persons involved are willing to offer when they want something in return.
...I have to contact Lavinia.
He takes in two deep breaths, pushes the shipping container door open, and goes in.
...Nobody's here?
Inside is a sole lamp and chair with no one else.
Sommer is stunned.
He decides to wait. As long as he can help Eirene this way...
He's willing to wait.

lines.st: Eirene, I like this new city.
I hope Guri likes it too. At the very least, I've prepared a better environment for her here.
Grazie, Eirene.
Look, I better get going. I've never gotten a late delivery on my record.

lines.x: Terza Zona... He should be here...
Blyat! How am I supposed to find him in such a huge place?
Lyudmila anxiously pops in and out of alleys created by the containers, trying to locate any trace of Sommer.
Until she hears voices nearby.
So? Where are you going on vacation after Carnevale?
Volsinii? The old-school musical theaters are all there. I'm planning to go to the more sophisticated establishments.
I rather go to Terni. I hear the clubs there have male Perro striptease dancers from Bolívar.
Tsk. You better tuck your tail in if you do, Saluzzo.
Hah. Want me to recommend you some ticket vendors in Volsinii?
You're a pretty good guy, and get work done quick. If you can't get along with the Venezias, come find me and I'll introduce you to some guys.
I'll pass. Want a smoke?
Heh, this lighter's pretty easy to use.
Lyudmila sees the two yapping openly, but she could not care any less what they are talking about.
Her sole target is the golden lighter one of them is playing with in his hand.
She recognizes that lighter, and knows the two are not its owners. It belongs to a driver who doesn't smoke.
What Lyudmila doesn't know is that it originally belonged to a famiglia associate.
After a roller-coaster ride, it ends up back in the hands of the mob once more.
Texas, you're back.

With me...
What? Am I not welcome here?
...Lappland?
Let's not overreact here. What if we disturb the patient's beauty rest?
Texas, quick. Tell our dear judge here I'm just here to deliver some documents.
(Nods)
Here, Your Honor.
We met a judge at the door who wanted to pass you this. The mayor's ward can't have too many people in here, so Texas and I took it for you right before we walked in.
Arson of Nuova Volsinii Port, Emergency Court Hearing, Defendant... Eirene?
The driver lady's your friend, right? Relax, just flip to the back. She was acquitted on the spot.
Texas and I happened to sit in that court hearing. So exciting. The Venezias—
Haha, sorry, sorry. I mean the Venezia Trucking and Saluzzo Vino bosses both testified and vouched for her innocence, and even fought over who got to send her home after the trial...
Tsk, tsk. A popular girl to be sure!
The way she walked out of the courthouse reminded me of how the famiglie would take a courthouse trip as proof of maturity.
Sign to confirm, receive the verdict, leave the court. The process went smoothly, but Eirene is still a little dizzy. The sunlight stings her eyes, and she lowers her head—
On the huge court square are two cars parked on her inevitable path. Both car hoods sport a special emblem—patterns one can see everywhere in Siracusa.
Even if she didn't recognize the Saluzzo Vino logo or the Venezia family crest, there is no way she could possibly fail to recognize the two men before their respective cars.
Eirene knows they're waiting for her—Famiglia Saluzzo and Famiglia Venezia.
Congratulations, Signorina Lavazza.
...There's nothing worth congratulating.
Signor Saluzzo, the fire yesterday was an accident, but my worries have indeed been solved, albeit in a confusing way.
I had been thinking I shouldn't cause you any further trouble, since I ended up asking you to meet with me for nothing.
But you showed up today, which means you just declared to everyone—
The Saluzzos want to help the Truckers' Union, of course.
Our present situation does mean some deals are no longer on the table, but I'm sure we both know how valuable a friendship can be in Siracusa.
Our agreement remains, Signorina Lavazza.
*ahem*—Zio Alberto, I hope I'm not disturbing you?
Signorina Lavazza helped us transport our tires, and since something has happened to them, I have to work out compensation with her.
Signor Venezia...
Don't worry. Today we've properly met for the first time, but we already have a good understanding between us.
You said nothing at all in court, which was wonderful.
I think we have a lot to talk about, such as the Truckers' Union and many other matters.
Get in the car, I'll drive you back.
Venezia Junior—
A loud horn interrupts the conversation between the three, and a truck drives between the two cars, coming to a steady stop before Eirene.
The driver's door opens, and Eirene sees Lyudmila get off the truck.
Lyudmila!
What's wrong?
Let's go. I came to take you back home. There's a lot back at camp waiting for you to make the call on.
Alright, let's go.
Once we're home, we have to quickly figure out a way to send Sommer away for a time. He's not safe in the city right now.
Something on your mind, Lyudmila?
Lyudmila says nothing and hands the lighter she's holding to Eirene.
Huh...? Isn't this Sommer's?!
He did everything he could.
I should have understood long ago the famiglie will never allow anyone who might pose a threat to them to live.
Eirene looks through the rear view mirror and meets the two mob bosses' eyes.
She suddenly understands why they had confidently testified for her, and why they had both waited for her in the square.
Venezia Junior was right. Her silence and their voices means the three parties had reached a tacit understanding in court.
Where's Sommer?
I'm sorry, I couldn't find him...
Eirene's grip tightens on the lighter.
So things did exceed everyone's expectations, and they had no choice but to come down here and protect me.
But the price paid...
They want the guys at city hall and the courts to know that the Truckers' Union and the famiglie are on the same boat.
Those two earlier... they were basically fighting over a territory.
The court hearing does not mean the matter has come to a close. It is only just beginning.
For a long time to come, the famiglie will become a thorn in the Truckers' Union's side, and she will have to struggle against these people to maintain their tacit, disgraceful understanding.
The truck gets farther and farther away from the court square. She looks at the lighter in her hand, and her fear suddenly dissipates somewhat.
It's not over yet, Alberto, Antonio...
When Sommer and I had just moved here from Volsinii, we really thought we left the famiglie.
...Heh. What a joke.
I'm sorry, Sommer. I'll never trust any of them ever again.
Such an interesting scene, like it came straight out of a film. Saluzzo and Venezia fellas, truck drivers, so many people surrounding that driver lady, they took up half the courthouse square...
They called it my coming-of-age ceremony, the first time I went into a courthouse. When I came out, there weren't many people greeting me.
Texas had it even worse, haha. She came to Siracusa as a guest, and accidentally got herself involved in a lawsuit. I was the only one who greeted her after.
Stop bringing up your boring pranks, Lappland.
Signorina Lappland, thank you for coming here and for the reminder. If there's nothing else, please leave the ward.
You're welcome, Just the least I could do.
Carnevale is tomorrow, dear judge. You still have a lot to do.
I was with Lappland the whole time since yesterday noon.
By the time we reached the port, the fire was already dying. She's not directly involved with it.
She spent the entire night after that just being a taxi driver. We went to the courthouse today, and then came here to the hospital...
Right now, I'm not sure what she's planning.
Are you still looking through the file, Lavinia?
Judging from its contents, there was no problem whatsoever with the hearing. But the feeling I'm getting is all too familiar for my liking...
They covered everything in the trial, and the testimony and evidence are stated in detail, but other than getting someone acquitted, it didn't find out any truths or solve any problems.
In Volsinii, and other places in Siracusa, most cases can only end up with a case file like this one.
I had thought such a trial would no longer exist here in Nuova Volsinii.
Lavinia...
You don't have to console me, Texas.
What concerns me more, is when did these people actually start to care about whether a civilian outside the famiglie is innocent or not?
What's more, before this hearing, Famiglia Venezia had a near perfect record in the city you couldn't easily find a reason for criticism, but this time Venezia Junior actually showed up.
From Demetri to Ingrid—I may spent the last two days chasing in the wrong direction.
What is certain, before this trial—or because of this trial, they are now on the same side.
Leon's car accident, the hit-and-run vehicle, and the port fire. These three obviously have a deeper connection...
The Saluzzos, the Venezias, and the Truckers' Union. What are these three forces hiding from us together?
Lavinia closes the file, running her hand over the court emblem, then the 'New Urban Management Act' on the cover.
After a moment, she raises her head.
Texas looks at her friend. She seemed somewhat gloomy and confused earlier, but she is definitely back to her old self now.
Carnevale is tomorrow, but Leon is still in a coma... I'm running out of time to figure out what kind of plans they have in store.
We can't let the situation worsen, let alone stay sitting fowls.
You mean...?
These people still think the New Urban Management Act is something they can use, but I shall show them it is the foundation of order in this city.
Hey, you! You can't go in there!
Texas?
Bursting into my winery without reason does not seem like something you would do.
Of course.
Told you we'd come find you, Signor Alberto Saluzzo.
These are Saluzzo Vino's qualification documents submitted in the past months.

A group of employees have in the short span of two months, changed their positions in your company's registered profiles several times. First they were security personnel, then winemakers, then technical staff...
We have reason to suspect Saluzzo Vino is employing illegal methods to circumvent the standard background check procedure.
Please come with us to assist our investigation.
Didn't the constant changes happen because city hall keeps changing the requirements?
What's the big idea, digging up these old files so suddenly?
