Don't waste your breath, these cops are obviously targeting us Saluzzos on purpose! Vaffanculo, we even paid so much in certification fees!
Anyway, don't let them take away the Don on our turf—
Stop. Don't get in the police's way.
If they're only suspecting Saluzzo Vino of circumventing background checks illegally, then it won't be difficult to resolve... we just need some time.
Listen to him.
Got it, Don Alberto.
This must have been the judge's idea.
What?
Of course, she's aware she can't do much with such a trivial offense, but she can at least keep me in jail for the next few days.
She must have gone to Venezia Junior or Eirene, otherwise you wouldn't be the one standing here right now.
I just happened to be free today.
Making such a swift decision with only just the little information she has. Decisive.
I still remember her looking so weak when she rushed to my place looking for Bernardo last year—she was a completely different person.
Telling me all this won't help. There's nothing to fear if you're truly innocent.
Of course, I'll go with you. I'll do my best to aid your investigation.
I said in court today I'm simply doing my duty as a citizen of the city.
Besides, Saluzzo Vino will not pause operations because of my temporary departure.
Demetri, I'm sure you must be familiar with our work here in Nuova Volsinii by now, yes?
Certainly, Don Alberto.
Take care of my wineries for now, manager of Saluzzo Vino.
Of course.
You sent for me, Don Venezia?
Anton has a question for you.
Over here, Carle.
Antonio...
Any cars headed towards Sicilia recently?
Passenger routes are operating as usual. Though, most people are coming in here, and not out.
Good.
Go pack up your belongings. You should be able to make the last ride over.

Gotcha—
Uh, Huh? Antonio, what's the meaning of this?
Don't you have a fratello in Sicilia? You can't stay in Nuova Volsinii any longer, and can't go back to Montelupe either, so you should go to him.
Your negligence has caused us to lose a whole carload of 'tires'.
I gave you a chance. Just greet the don at the port, couldn't even manage that. That old Saluzzo stronzo even got to meet papa before you did.
Carle, I've already given you the most decent ending possible by letting you leave, alive. Take this—
A check from a bank, with a huge sum written on it.
The famiglia associate takes it, stunned. He sees his own name written on the upper left, already crossed out by Antonio himself.
Call it a settlement fee. Without the famiglia's protection, you'll need a lot of money.
I've been with the Venezias for more than a decade. I was following the Don before you even came in here, can't you just, for his sake—
D-Don Venezia—!
Employees who make mistakes must naturally be punished, yes?
Of course. You're the head of Venezia Trucking. You call the shots.
Anton... you didn't inform me about this in advance.
Please don't blame me for this.

The police were just here earlier. They wanted to inspect the vehicle factories and service stations under the Venezia name. They said it was just a routine inspection before Carnevale, but the official warrant said it was an impromptu check.
I also just received news that Alberto was suddenly temporarily detained for corporate qualification issues.
I have no doubt the trial this morning has upset our dear judge Lavinia. She's hoping to control a situation she knows nothing of this way.
The situation spiraled out of my control, so I had to deal with Carle like that. It'll be hard to convince the masses otherwise.
This year, Venezia Trucking has carefully complied with the New Urban Management Act, and the only clue we had proving otherwise was burned to cinders yesterday at the port.
The only person who can affect our business has been dealt with.
But when Carnevale is over and Lavinia has her hands free, I'm afraid she'll try her best to target us.
How do you plan on handling it?
My original idea was to slowly transform this city—Let it have a set of laws absent in other cities, while establishing an underground order the Siracusans are accustomed to.
Venezia Trucking's business has been smooth sailing. The trick is to follow these two tenets: Comply with the New Urban Management Act, but also not.
Of course, the other famiglie are doing the same, but adapting to the rules of the new game is quite difficult.
The Venezias can fit perfectly into both sets of laws, and we've been excelling at it.
So much so that today we can discard one and switch to the other immediately.
So you want to completely control this city?
You've lost yourself, Anton. Don't forget Nuova Volsinii has Signora Sicilia's endorsement. She supports the youths' reforms.
You're openly defying her authority and turning us against all of Siracusa this way. You're using the entire Famiglia Venezia as your betting chip!
It sounds like that, yes.
The last one to do that was Bernardo... he only died last year!
Are you afraid of Signora Sicilia, afraid of the youngsters at City Hall yelling their ideals across the room?
You've really gotten old, papa.
We Venezias hold a most unusual seat in the Grey Hall, a 'hybrid' famiglia. Lupos with no blood ties who left their previous pack, Vulpos like Ingrid, and Perros like you...
The other famiglie don't think too kindly of us, but we stand here today nonetheless. Our presence here embodies every Venezia's sweat and blood.
Our ties run closer than bloodlines, and I want everyone in the famiglia to have a decent end.
But you, you want to put us in great danger... Anton, I think you've forgotten my teachings.
Papa, have I ever mentioned how a Perro like me ended up in Siracusa?
I was a soldier, serving the True Bolívarians.
I have but a superficial understanding of the situation in Bolívar...

It actually doesn't matter which flag you fight under in Bolívar. You don't have to understand why the three parties are fighting one another. I mean, everyone's just trying to survive by giving it their all.
I was in a guerrilla force raiding a village under the Singas dynasty, but we were discovered, and the entire squad became captives.
The twenty of us emptied every pocket and piled our possessions together to bribe the executioner to spare us, change our status from 'death penalty' to 'hard labor'.

The officer responsible for driving us was a paunchy aristocrat. He was drunk that day, made a mistake during the review process, and only came to us with a truck... big enough to sit just ten people.
The executioner asked him what to do next. Can you guess how that stronzo replied?
He belched from his drink as he whipped out a pen and drew a circle on our list, saying: There's clearly only ten people on the list, right?
We killed him, cut off his head, escaped Bolívar, and what happened next, I'm sure you know.
Anton, this is the first time I've heard of—
Haha. It's been too long I almost forgot about it.
Do you know why it suddenly came back to me?
I was discussing with our young and promising mayor about building a logistical network in the city. He refused me, and then shared how the city came about, and its mission...
A so-called 'experimental ground', with a new order governing people from the old, vastly different from the Siracusan tradition...

He knew people would have trouble adapting, irreconcilable conflicts would occur, and the system might even swallow groups of people in the process... but it couldn't be helped, there was a potential future.
And all of that happened because four young people sat at the Grey Hall's round table and lobbied for their idea so passionately...
Signora Sicilia allocated them one of Siracusa's twenty-three cities, without hesitation...
A move so cavalier, like that aristocrat who circled half our names away.
Papa, Leontuzzo was indeed quite sincere and inspiring at the time, but I almost lost control, I wanted to burst into laughter... That boy knows nothing at all.
You said I lost myself?
No, not at all. What I want to do has always been the same.
I hate that aristocrat and Signora Sicilia. I hate their power to wantonly draw their circles as they please. I want to take it all away from them!
Anton...
Phew—Alright, Papa. I've said a bit too much today. I'm not here to let you lecture me on decency or dignity, nor am I here to analyze what's going on in Siracusa.
My activities this year alone are not enough to land a seat at the gambling table...
Papa, don't we have a landship en-route nearby? I'd like the rights to make an emergency dispatch.
This is something only Don Venezia can do.
If I refuse, will the men guarding the door deal with me like how you handled Carle?
Of course not, you're a truly decent man worthy of a dignified end.
You're old, Papa. Haven't you always suggested you retire?
Scusi, is Eirene not here right now?
...Y-Your Honor?
U-Uh, Eirene's not here. Everyone went to celebrate her acquittal, that's why.
So why aren't you with them?
I-I'm just servicing these trucks. See?
Carnevale's tomorrow, and they'll need a lot of our cars...
Someone has to do it.
You're lying, Signore.
When you see her, please let Eirene know I need to meet her, even outside of office hours.
...Got it.
Eirene, here. The schedule table for this year.
As you said, I marked all the Venezia Trucking related records. Trip times, loading instructions, driver names... they're all in there.
Yep, matches with Sommer's notebook.
Sommer wanted to bring Guri here; Carmelo was secretly nursing his injuries with alcohol and couldn't get it from us; Gianni and Ruggiero had their own troubles too...
Every member of ours they targeted was carefully selected.
There aren't many records, but the shipment frequency is very consistent. That night wasn't the first time they smuggled weapons into the city for sure...
You were right. That judge did come looking for you at the camp. After she left, some famiglie associates were tailing her in secret.
So...
I drove behind her car and came back here only after I saw her enter the courthouse.
Good.
There's also one more thing.
What is it?
I happened to see Saluzzo Vino's legal team hurrying into the courthouse.
I asked around, and apparently not long ago the police suddenly showed up at one of the Saluzzo wineries and took Alberto away.
Venezia Trucking also got the cops at their door at the same time. The police and judges should be inspecting their factories right now.
Lavinia probably doesn't know about the weapons yet, but the car accident and port fire has already sent out a dangerous signal...
Leontuzzo's injured and still hospitalized even now. As Nuova Volsinii's Supreme Justice, she tries her best to get the situation under control.
She didn't use a direct method with us like she did with the other two... she went to camp to warn us.
In other words, an ultimatum.
Wait, about what Lyudmila just said...
Neither Alberto or Venezia Junior will willingly meet their demise.
Lavinia's in danger right now.
Those wise guys only followed her with no intentions to harm her.
We're in Nuova Volsinii after all, we got cops patrolling the streets. Besides, they have an even better time slot available...
You mean Carnevale...?
We could let the judge know.
Eirene stays still for a long time before shaking her head.
Are you still hesitant to let her know about the Truckers' Union? But can you really just stand here and watch something happen to her?
She's not a bad person at least.
You got it wrong, Lyudmila.
It's too late for us now to stay out of it.
If the famiglie dare make a direct attempt on Lavinia's life during Carnevale, there's no chance they'll let the Truckers' Union go. If that happens, we can only wait for the slaughter to happen.
I'm just thinking what I should do. I don't want something like what happened with Sommer to happen again...
Giving Lavinia a direct warning isn't the best choice here.
Lyudmila, did you see the weapons? How many were there?
Based on the amount I saw and total number of shifts on the schedule—
I estimate there's enough Venezia weapons in the city to arm several tactical squads with dozens of people in each.
We have more than two hundred registered members, but if we include ad-hoc staff and contract workers, we'll have several times the number of people.
I just realized the Truckers' Union is actually the largest organization in Nuova Volsinii.
Maybe we can manage it. Protecting a single person in the dark.
Do you really just want to protect that judge?
Sommer taught me one thing at least: no one can be trusted in this city but ourselves.
So we can only protect ourselves... even if that means resorting to violence.
When I was in Ursus, I knew someone with similar ideas. She lost herself in the end.
You want to stop me?
No... What happened with Sommer needs a proper ending.
But of course, he wouldn't want to see you lose yourself either.
Eirene, I guarantee you won't cross that final line...
I promise you I won't, Lyudmila.
I'm perfectly aware what I'm doing.
Pizza delivery from 'One More Slice'!
Pumpkin Pepper, Cheese, Fries & Sausage, Supreme, Devil's Pepper, and classic Mozzarella.
Eat up! Can't work on an empty stomach. This one's yours, Signor Emperor... er, should I call you Consultant instead?
Mm. Not bad. Soft and chewy crust, nice meaty flavor. You got a card for this pizzeria?
I made it myself at the dock workers' kitchen.
I can write down the recipe for you, if you want.
Mm—
No need to be shy, it's the least we can do.
If not for your help, we couldn't possibly have fixed all the damaged floats before the parade tonight.
I'm just thinkin' how authentic Siracusan pizza might go over with the Lungmenites. Maybe Exusiai could add it to the menu at The Ends of the Earth.
What about that signore who's singing while working? Should I call him over?
Hey, Big Mouth, over here! This'll shut you up.
I don't know what's worse, the way you break into song anytime anywhere, that obnoxious singin' voice of yours, or the song itself...
I mean, what's that crap? You belt that out while clappin' together your building blocks in the jungle?
Can't find the energy to work without song.
Unlike you, who's just sitting there giving 'advice', I'm the one who has to put in the work and sweat!
You got a reason to do that, I don't.
It was your float that got its ass burned off, wasn't it?
It was bad enough that it didn't look cool at all, but how could they put me so close to that wolf?!
Well, all the other floats have their arms and legs stuck back in place. The only one left is the wolf that's just a pile of ash now.
If you ask me, it makes more sense to spend the time making the other floats look cooler than rebuilding one float.
There are so many floats, who would miss one wolf?
Fair 'nuff.
N-No! You can't do that!
There are many legends of beasts in Siracusa, but the wolf is special.
City Hall had already announced the number of floats and what they're based on. People will get anxious if one is missing.

You know—
Hey fatso, stop stuffing that pie in my mouth—
Pineapple? On pizza? Now THAT is what I call radical.
Do all Siracusan cops have to learn to make pizza?
Oh no, my papa has a pizzeria in another city, and I used to help out there.
We're pretty well-known. When I was little, I dreamed about having branches all over Siracusa, even our own dedicated headquarters plate.
Maybe you've heard of our ad? 'Noemi Pizza—one more slice even when you're full!'
Nope.
We never had the money to start a second one.
Business was good, and no one dares to not pay for their food, but paying our protection fee takes up most of our profit.
I came when I heard that the Nuova Volsinii Police Academy was recruiting. Maybe I'll be able to protect the shop once I become a cop.
Uh...
I know what you want to say. It sounds naive, doesn't it? Being a cop is much harder, and then there's all the trouble that's popped up these last couple of days.
Think of this another way.
Becomin' a cop means you can change your slogan—
'Noemi Pizza—if it isn't good, you can lock ME up!'
Oh...
You remind me of my Zumama. The young always have such simple goals and such strong motivation.
This is all YOUR fault, fatso. I didn't even have time to tell Zumama I was coming here.
Ain't many parties worth bringing us together, Big Mouth.
Besides, we got serious business to talk about after the party.
Anyway, I really appreciate it! We were at our wit's end when you guys offered to help.
As for your compensation, I'll put in an application with City Hall as soon as the Carnevale is over.
We got our compensation already.
Emperor puts another pizza slice in his mouth.
We'll see this through to the end. This IS good pizza, after all.
High Priest puts another pizza in his mouth.
Gotta admit, it gives me a warm n' fuzzy feelin' to see that wolf burn to ashes.
So you...
But it don't compare to the anger I feel when a good party gets ruined.
Oh, cruel fate!
All the other stalls are busy preparing to make a killing tonight. We're the only ones who have been given the death sentence.
I don't care, you're eating all the losses.
That's just not fair. I did what I did with good intentions! I was trying to minimize the losses when the rain hit the other day.
By getting the beer cart instead of the food drying in the courtyard out in the open?
Mistakes happen...

The beer cart is fully enclosed and equipped with a functioning thermostat. Who cares if it gets a little rain on it? But you had to push it back, and now you cost me the spot that took me so much effort to get!
No stall, no food. This ain't a Carnevale, it's my funeral!

I should have stuck to movies. Yes, I had to deal with the mafiosi who all fancy themselves filmmakers, but at least I got to make a living. I shouldn't have listened to your tall tales and come here—
Y-You again!
Zzz... zzz...

You're still here? Did you fall asleep on the spot after that ice cream?
Father? Father!
—I really should take a photo of you right now!
So what's stopping you?
I'll pay if it looks good.
Huh?
You said photo, right?
Yeah.
Today is the biggest day of Nuova Volsinii. Everyone will show up fully dressed.
Masks, costumes, and massive, mysterious floats. Definitely something I would want to take a few photos of, for the memories.
But I can't stop for something like that, not when the parade keeps rolling. Wouldn't it be great if there was a professional photographer who could do it in my stead?
Besides, it's better business than competing with everyone else selling beer and ice cream—and to tell you the truth, your ice cream isn't that competitive. Not sweet enough.
You heard the Father, what are you still standing around for?
H-huh, what's happening now?
Go and get the camera! And don't forget to buy a few more rolls of film!
Thank you. Can't have two furs in one tail. Looks like this guy and I just don't match.
Problems always float to the surface, and there are always ways to deal with them.
I don't think there's a church in this town... where did you come from, Father?
Montelupe.
The capital... did you come all this way for Carnevale?
An old friend asked me to see what it's all about.
It's been a long time since Siracusa has had something this big, after all.
Like Leithanien's Empresses' Celebration, which takes place every year and involves the entire country, and it just gets bigger every year...
Yes, it's been a long time since Siracusa has had something this big.
I hope it doesn't rain. It would make it so much harder to keep the peace.
What are you doing, Signora Venezia?
You're not using this cloth anymore, right? Picked it out of the discard pile. Just right for wiping my blade.
I could get a piece of softer cotton cloth for you.
No need for that.
There will be festivities on every street tonight. There's no need for you to stay at Tailor de Montano.
I promised Vargo to solve your problem.
You found Luchino and accompanied me to the Villa Venezia to bring him back. You've already helped so much. Thank you.
There's no need to thank me, Umberto.
You saved Luchino yourself, by convincing Venezia Senior to not pursue the matter any further.
Is he still shutting himself in the room? I haven't seen him. Don't tell me he's ran away again.
If he runs away, just let him. So much has happened over the past couple of days, and he's still young. He needs time to process things.
Perhaps I've been too strict.
Maybe he's just struggling with how to view you, his own grandfather, an old tailor, and once the terror of Siracusan dons.
Well, that's not important. What's important is how he views himself after he stood at death's door and learned about all these things in the past.
And he needs to figure it out. I don't have that much time left.
Well, Signora Venezia, I bid you addio.
You already know, don't you?
What?
The culprit who hurt your daughter.
And why would you say that?
Did you forget I was once a killer too, Signora Venezia?
Your weapon is sharp. It needs no honing, and you are not in the habit of doing so.
When a killer starts polishing their blade, they're either planning to put it away forever, or planning to put it in someone.
Fair point.
More conjecture than proof at this point... but at least I have something. That's why I said you don't have to thank me.
You came to Nuova Volsinii and stayed here at de Montano to learn about the truth—who planned the assassination, and hurt Signorina Lisa.
This was your agreement with Vargo, but if you have learned the answer, there's no reason to continue it.
Besides, I don't have any troubles left to deal with.
The game is not over yet. The Fangs are still in the city. I've crossed blades with them. They may be young, but they fight well.
If I can find them, they can find you.
You're old and frail, Umberto. There can only be one end.
It's an end that already looms large. There's nothing you can do.
Tonight, at least, I wish to be alone, and have a moment of quiet to myself.

Alright, we'll be going—
Hey, new face. Where's the old dispatcher?
...Shift changed half an hour ago. He's probably in rehearsal right now.
Huh?
Didn't you know? He used to be a saxist at the Teatro di Milano. They recruited him for the parade.
I see. He's keeping you in the dark so he can show off tomorrow when his face is in the newspapers!
Stronzo!
So you're on duty tonight?
Yup, I'm the new guy. On the bright side, the harbor's gonna be rather quiet because of all the festivities.
I'll turn the radio up and feel the festive spirit from here.
I would go now if I were you... if you want to get a good place in the procession.
Alright, be seeing you.
Phew...
You almost let it slip.
How about you do it, then?
Hey, I don't got the acting experience. You're the one who's an extra in the mayor's promotional video.
It ain't the same thing. I was just being myself in the promo, and we could always do another take. Screw up just now, and all of Antonio's plans would've been blown.
This is an important night for you, for me, and for all of Venezia.
Once this city is firmly under our control, Antonio will be as good as his word. He'll support you in... what's the wording? Restoring the glory of Sicilia?

I just hope I don't end up like Carle...
What about our musicista?
Playing his saxophone in hell. In that container over there. We'll deal with the body once the dust settles.
You got the dispatch station, then.
Don't worry.
Every vehicle that goes in and out of the port has to go through special checks. Venezia Trucking's trucks have been added to the list.
The nomadic city's positioning system has been turned off, with the exception of a single encrypted channel. Other than the famiglia's landship, no vehicles will approach the city tonight.
The rest of the boys are outside. Once the landship approaches the city and starts docking, everybody in Nuova Volsinii Port who ain't part of this is gonna disappear.
What about Antonio?
You think the boss needs us to worry about him?
A few hours left... enjoy the night, Nuova Volsinii. Enjoy the true Carnevale.
Bit trite for an opening speech, don't you think?
The voice came from above.
The White Wolf looks down at them from atop a crane, amusement on her face, surprise and shock on theirs. She frowns, like a listener who can no longer tolerate the performance.
Who are you? Have you been here all this time?
Do her in, Gambino!
Gotcha.
I thought you said it was trite. But you're clappin'.
You just gave me the first twist, didn't you?
You know, I was kind of hoping you would try to—how did he put it? 'Do me in.'
I wanted to, but I ain't about to do somethin' doomed to fail.
Fair enough. Where's your little friend?
Capone? Helpin' Venezia Junior with some other stuff.
Sicilians been busy, eh?
Whaddya want, Lappland?
We've done enough for you. I can't—
Hm?
Uh, I mean...
Venezia Junior has put everything the famiglia's built over the past year into this. At this point, even if you wanna sabotage it, there's not much I can do to help.
You still think you know me, Gambino. Why would I make things hard for an old, er, friend?
Just do the job that your boss told you to.
What he's trying to do doesn't conflict with what I'm trying to do. Maybe I'm even trying to help him?
It's Carnevale, after all. The night when all the wonderful dreams we have about this city come true.
That's why you didn't even need to kill this guy on the ground—oops.
You really shouldn't be so eager to prove that you're a villain. No wonder Capone's racking up citizen points so much faster than you.

...
*Sigh*
What else do you want me to do?
I dropped something at the Villa Venezia. Bring it to me.
What is it?
A gift box.
You mean, the one that the little tailor was—
Uh-huh.
And get me a bunch of ribbons, balloons, spotlights, that kind of stuff. Hurry!

lines.st: (The Long Carnevale)
Once upon a time, there was a strange pink Nightmare that roamed the wild west.
Its hide was black charcoal, its hair charred clouds, its horns twisted briar.
Many were deceived by its beautiful mask, mesmerized by its sweet scent, falling into deep dreams, becoming its nourishment.
The Nightmare targeted Lupo children, once luring thousands of children away from a city in a single night.
But the wise Siracusans came up with a way to fight the terrible pink Nightmare—
The Nightmare was afraid of wolves. When you saw it, you'd simply cry 'Wolf!' three times, and the Nightmare would disappear without a trace.

lines.x: That is QUITE enough! I can't listen to any more of this!
No need to get excited, Dolly. The musician isn't wrong; that's what Siracusan legends say about sheep.

If you're not happy about it, you could always jump on top of his head from your sheep float. After all, no one would be surprised about anything that happens tonight, no matter how out of this world it is.
Look, I just like getting chased by wolves. Playing hide-and-seek with them was one of the few hobbies I had during my time in Siracusa.
How could they twist the story like that? Blasphemy, I say, blasphemy!
That ain't the word I'd pick for it.
Stop throwing cold water on our fun.
How do the Siracusans see YOU, Emperor?
Tell 'im about the black-robed singer, Big Mouth!
Well... once upon a time, there was a mafia Don who loathed music and banned it in his city, until a penguin singer dressed in a black robe arrived many years later.
When he started singing at the town square, the citizens, having no concept of music, found it too noisy and tried to catch the singer and hand him over to the Don.
But the singer was too quick and slippery, even for the most athletic young man.
He continued to sing as more and more people joined the chase, which eventually turned into a great dance that engulfed the entire city.

The singer sang for three days and three nights until the furious Don had enough and came to catch him personally. But the Don was caught in the revelry as soon as he walked out of the door, and was never seen again.
Why does Emperor get to sound like a brave, clever hero when he's not half as wise as I am?
It ain't about wisdom, it's about Graf Pangwen's attitude toward music! Freedom n' struggle! You never did listen to the albums I signed for you, did you?
Let's go, we're getting in the way.
(Plays excitedly)
(Juggles excitedly)
(Plays even harder)
(Shows off skills)
Achille? Where's my Achille?
Did you just realize you had been walking with a stranger for half-an-hour, Signora?
Oh, I recognize your voice! You're that judge from Terni... I'm so sorry, the Leica famiglia made me lie in court—
(Taps mask) All water under the bridge, Signora. Remember the rules of Carnevale?
Look around at the sea of smiles around you! Fool and king, queen and priest, all are the same in their masks. Forget the scores of the past, and let us toast to the future of Nuova Volsinii!
The crowd passes by three Aristocrats of Beasts, as Dolly's clones meander through the sea of people. All can see them; no one shows any surprise.
In this ocean of revelry, not a single sea spray feels out of place.
A dozen massive floats form an orderly row, carrying the visages of wolf, sheep, penguin, cat, dog... legendary beasts that have come together in this city to lead the procession.

The streets are brimming—nay, overflowing—with plays and improvised performances. Musicians and acrobats seem locked in a competition to outdo one another, forcing food cart owners to raise their voices even higher to peddle beer, pizza and ice cream.
This is even more exciting than an active volcano.
Better than the old Carnevale. Even fatso didn't enjoy that kind of party.
Please don't dirty the word 'party' by usin' it on a mafia-masked murder-fest.
One tree don't bear two fruits. The Siracusans are just like those wolves.
Thousands of years ago, we left the wolves out of our parties because those dumbass pups didn't know nothin' but fighting and killing. So they came up with their own game.
Lucky the Siracusans are smarter, and they decided there's things worth celebrating besides than killing and fighting for territory.
As for the wolves that don't know how to change—
They sense the same thing that we do. They know something is not right.
We're 'kindred', after all. There are certain things that we need to talk about.
I've met the ones that are less stupid, but they won't listen to anyone until their stupid game is done.
Well, they got a sayin' in Yan, and it means basically 'When in Londinium, do as the Londiniers do.'
Now, if this is a party, the Siracusans better not let me down!
Wind brushes against Demetri's cheeks.

The windows of the ward were closed when he left yesterday, and Lavinia had instructed the nurse to keep a close eye on the temperature in this autumn season. But he sees only an empty bed when he looks up.
It's been a while, Demetri.
I'm sorry, Signor Bellone, this man insisted on coming in. I tried calling City Hall and the courts to reach Judge Lavinia, but I couldn't get through...
It's okay. You can leave.
When did you wake up?

The bright lights illuminate the skies and shine upon the hospital ward. The noise of the crowd can be clearly heard even through the window. Leontuzzo stands next to the window, gazing down at a city in revelry.
He has changed out of his patient clothes, and there is no hint that he was in a coma not so long ago.
A few hours ago.
You know, I thought we were meeting up at a restaurant, not a hospital.
Good, you still remember what you were doing before the accident. Looks like it didn't damage your brain.
Tsk.
You sure didn't lose any time to get dressed as soon as you woke up. Don't tell me you plan on attending the festivities.
Surely the nurses have told you that you need to lie down and get some rest... you're still wrapped in bandages underneath those clothes, aren't you?
Just a little light-headed. Got some pills for it, and I feel much better now.
Hundreds of thousands of citizens will be on the streets tonight, celebrating the one-year anniversary of Nuova Volsinii.
The original plan calls for the parade to start from the expansion plate, travel through twenty-three streets across nine plates, where it will be joined by various festivities.
At midnight, they will arrive at Court Square, where I will address the crowd and welcome a new day together with them—
City Hall has contingency plans.
Your inauguration can surely wait until you're in better shape.
Do you really think that, Demetri?
Leontuzzo turns around.
Lavinia has yet to announce the cancellation of the speech. I don't think it's because she forgot to.
I have a rough idea of what's been going on these few days.
The car accident at the port district, then the fire at Nuova Volsinii port... what will the citizens think if the mayor misses tonight's scheduled event?
If Carnevale concludes without a hitch, then the worst thing that comes out of my absence is that I lose a few approval points.
But if there is something hidden under the bright lights outside the window, I need to be at the scene.
Let's reschedule our dinner to another day.
Today.
The Carnevale has begun. The whole city is busy. Didn't you hear the doctor? Neither City Hall nor the courts are answering phone calls.
I'm afraid they have their hands full, right now.
I brought some people with me. We can escort you to the scene.
You don't have a choice, Leon.
If you know what's been happening the past few days, then you have at least an inkling of what's going on between me and Saluzzo, but you didn't bring it up.
I have some things I have to tell you. Today is the chance.
Leontuzzo gazes at his old friend.
The threat in Demetri's words are thinly-veiled. After a brief moment of silence, Leontuzzo extends his hand.
I'll be counting on you, then, Demetri.
Just like old times.
How are you feeling, Grandma?
You're lost, my child.
I am looking for the last true wolf. I have wasted too much time.
Easy, my child. It's not your fault.
Grandma has taught you how to tell the scent of a true wolf's pelt.
But the last true wolf is clever. He is a weaver of pelts, hiding his scent perfectly. He is a...
...Tailor.
Grandma guides me.
I will find the true wolf. Wait for me, grandma.
...de Montano.
Oh, xuj!
Wh-What is it, Lyudmila?
...Red hood.
That one again? Where?
The alley up ahead. I could recognize her from a mile away.
Man, we were just taking a minute to change the tires, and you run into her AGAIN?
You want to go after her?
Let's get back on the truck. Eirene's waiting for us.
I left Sommer at the accident scene the last time in order to go after her, which just seemed to lead to all this crazy shit...
You know what's funny? I keep running into bad luck just like I keep running into her. It's like a curse I couldn't shake.
It's a brutal pack of wolves that we're facing today. I've seen what they're capable of.
That's why I need to be with you guys, this time.
Well, nothing for you to be ashamed of.
The bastard taunted you when you tried to take revenge and killed your mentor. You're way calmer than I would be if I was in your shoes.
You know what, once we get all this crap out of the way tonight, we'll come with you to find her, beat her up, and tie her up behind our trucks so that she gets to know what dust tastes like.
Nobody messes with OUR ragazza sfortunata.
Hah. Well, let's get going.
Dammit, boss, this place was hard to find.
Our guys are in position, but are you sure you don't want to be on scene at a time like this?
Come. Look.
Court Square?
Man, this house is so close to Court Square? I took so many turns on my way here that I thought I ended up in another district. Hell, I could see the posters on the courthouse windows...
Well-concealed, but offers a perfect view of the square and the surrounding streets.
I will be giving orders in real-time from right here, until the dust settles.
How'd you get your hand on such prime real estate in such a short time?
It has been under my name for a long time.
I bought it soon after I came to Nuova Volsinii. I come here from time to time, when I wanted some time off.
...To stare at the courthouse and City Hall?
How are things with the old man?
No problems. The estate's crowded, and they got enough whiskey, cigars, and classic records to keep him busy all night.
The port?
Gambino got everything set up. Our landship is the only thing that's coming to the city tonight... it's almost time, come to think of it.
All our vehicles are being deployed around the Nuova Volsinii port. The goods'll be delivered to every associate of the famiglia once the landship has docked.
Then, it's up to us.
Where is the parade procession?
The new art district, where all the theaters and entertainment establishments are. There's lots of performances on the streets, and activities for visitors to interact with the floats.
At least an hour until it gets here.
We have a small number of select famiglia associates nearby, equipped with the weapons that we had smuggled in.
The square is undermanned, with most of Nuova Volsinii's police keeping order along the parade route. Suppress the courthouse and other administrative buildings quickly and efficiently.
With the port and the courthouse in our hands, and the supplies from the landship, Famiglia Venezia will become the de facto rulers of Nuova Volsinii.
It's time for Nuova Volsinii to wake up from revelry.
I just thought of somethin'.
Hm?
City Hall's plan calls for the parade to be welcomed at the square by Leontuzzo's inaugural speech, where he's gonna talk about stuff like the new order and Siracusa's future n' whatever...
You gonna say something too when they're welcomed by fully-armed associates of Famiglia Venezia?
There is no need, Capone.
If you need to think about something, think about whether you still remember how to use a weapon after all the time you spent working at a vehicle company.
All good—
I know your history, Capone. The Sicilians started the first city state war in the history of Siracusa.
You looted through the city, pointing your weapons at all who would resist or speak up, and harvested the tails of the vanquished with sickles.
That was Siracusa's first Carnevale, even though the name came later.
This is your chance to prove that the history of Siracusa began with you, Sicilian.
Well, that sort of talk is better for Gambino. He's the one who could never stop talking about 'Sicilians'.
Lungmen, Volsinii, Nuova Volsinii... we've gone from one place to another over the years. The Sicilians fell on hard times after Signora Sicilia abandoned her familglia.
Which is why I've never forgotten how to pull a trigger.
Good, good.
Do it.
Happy Carnevale, signore! Have a balloon!
Thanks... hey, wait a minute.

Is the parade here already? I thought I would hear from the folks at the earlier stops.
Gugh—
Y-You're...
Hm. Slower on the uptake than I thought.
What did you think was gonna happen? This ain't Signor Mayor's promotional video, where we go to the ground as soon as a cop tags us.
They've been on the job for, what, one year?
There is no famiglia in Nuova Volsinii. Did you think they would have the skill or the guts to put their head on the line, when all they do is issue traffic tickets?
Left side of the square is clear.
Right side, same here.
The roads that connect the courthouse and the administrative building is secure. No survivors.
Caught a few paper pushers as they were coming out.
Damn, one got away and went back into the courthouse—
Alright, no complaining.
It's time. Do it!
What time is it, Texas?
Eleven.
Here's your whiskey with ice.
Thanks.
You've been looking at these route maps over and over again, Lavinia. Take a break.
The area around the square has been cleared of all non-relevant personnel. Traffic control is in effect from the new art district to the courthouse. All that's left is to wait for the procession.
What's your decision?
You're not concerned that it would cause a panic if it's the 'Last Texas' who makes the midnight speech, rather than the Acting Mayor as originally planned?
No, I'm not.
Every float tonight is themed after a legendary beast, however mysterious or cruel, and many citizens have put on masks to play the wolf pack.
Beast or man, they will walk together tonight in Nuova Volsinii.
I can think of nothing more appropriate for the Carnevale than having the Last Texas give a speech to the citizens about a better future.
And I can think of no one else who could take Leontuzzo's place as guest of honor.
Alright, let me see that draft again...
I'm sorry, Texas, but I need to prepare for all possible contingencies.
You've looked on edge since you came back from the truckers' camp.
I've done what I could in the time that I had, but the threat hasn't been eliminated. Tonight—
J-Judge Lavinia!
What's the matter... is that blood on your clothes?
A large group suddenly showed up at the square. They took out the police...
Who were they?
I couldn't tell... they were all dressed up in costumes and wearing carnival masks.
But they looked like they're ready to charge into the courthouse!
Get your wounds treated. I'll check it out.
No, Judge Lavinia! They're all over the square! If you go out now—
If that's the case, hiding here won't do any good.
I'm with you.
Good.
Stay here, Lavinia.
I'll deal with what's going on outside.
Eirene?
It's all wise guys. They want this building, and the Supreme Justice—you.
I'm here to protect you.
Protect me?
How—
What the—
Lavinia rushes to the window.
The pack of beasts that had lay dormant in the darkness begins their hunt.
Elaborately dressed, masked figures emerge from the alleys and approach the courthouse in orderly formation, quickly making their way through the police lines.
And no further.
A truck appears out of nowhere, coming to a screeching stop in their path. The police barricade that it sent flying makes a dull sound as it hits the ground.
Then, a second, and a third, as truck after truck barge into the square.
Demonstrating surprising coordination, they form a tight line between the oncoming crowd and the courthouse, stopping it in its tracks like a dam stops the tide.
They won't get near the courthouse for a while.
I feel like I've seen this before...
Perhaps you've seen City Hall's promotional video for Signor Bellone.
Insurgent mafiosi block off the courthouse with trucks, creating an area in the city where the police cannot reach, allowing them to exact wanton violence...
That was the inspiration. I wouldn't have thought of it if I wasn't part of the shoot.
Eirene closes the window.
Time is short, Lavinia.
There is much that we need to resolve.
What is this?
A list of people who have been coercing drivers to smuggle contraband into the city, compiled from the truckers' testimonies.
It is the same people behind the accident that night, the fire at Nuova Volsinii port, and the insurrection on the square that's happening right now. The mafia.
...I see.
You should have told me about the things that happened to the Truckers' Union. I went to the camp yesterday but didn't find you—
Too late for that now, Your Honor. The important thing right now is to put these people on trial.
Trial?
They want to take over the courthouse and eliminate their greatest threat before Carnevale is done... first Signore Leontuzzo, then you, Judge Lavinia.
They are no longer citizens of Nuova Volsinii. They have violated everything that the New Urban Management Act stands for.
The evidence is right before you. The drivers are the witnesses.
Signorina Texas here belongs neither to the government, the famiglie, nor the Truckers' Union. She is eminently qualified to be the jury.
Er...
And you are the Supreme Justice of Nuova Volsinii. The conditions have been met for emergency judicial procedures... did I get the term right?
Under extreme circumstances, the courts may, when the basic conditions have been fulfilled, try persons who present a significant risk to the public security of Nuova Volsinii in absentia.
The most important matter at hand is to bring the situation under control, to ensure the safety of the citizens and the smooth progress of Carnevale.
That's what I'm here for, Your Honor. Deliver your judgment, and the Truckers' Union will carry it out.
Is that so?

This list of names that you gave me—
It's a little too detailed, Eirene.
Venezia, Saluzzo, Medici, Leoti, Genovese... are all of these famiglie on the square outside?
Nobody on this list is innocent, Your Honor.
If emergency judicial procedures are activated, once I sign this verdict, everyone on this list becomes an outlaw under the New Urban Management Act...
By law, anyone may apprehend, even execute them at their own discretion, without legal consequence.
The present situation is not enough for me to justify such a measure.
But they're here for you, Lavinia, and they're not coming with good intentions.
That may be true, Eirene. But what intentions are YOU coming here with?
Why have you surrounded the courthouse with all these trucks? Is it to protect a friend... or to take the opportunity to solve all the problems you're facing?
When you came to the camp yesterday, I was trying to find Sommer's body...
Thank you for remembering him... yes, he's dead. I mobilized a lot of people, but we never found his body.
I don't know who killed him, but I know he—or she—is out there, in the darkness below.
It's not any one man or woman who killed him. It's a shadow that has always hung above our heads, one that has continued to stalk us, even after coming to this new city.
I want the murderers to get the punishment they deserve. I want to protect the rest of them and prevent the same fate from befalling anyone else.
That's my intention, Your Honor.
I should go. They're not used to this kind of stuff. I got to be by their side...
I know it's a lot to take in all at once, and you need time to process. I'll make sure you're not disturbed.
But don't take too long.
What's going on? An ambush?
Did City Hall know about our plans?
I saw their leader go into the courthouse. She must have gone to see that judge...
What'cha scared of? It's trucks, not tanks.
The government's so scared, they have to hide behind a bunch of truckers.
Forget 'em. We're going in!
E-Eirene! What do we do?
Give her some time.
Signore e signori, I know who you are, and I know what you're planning.
I suggest you go no further.
...It's quite a scene. I never imagined there would a festival in Siracusa like this, where everybody is part of it.
It's because this city belongs to all of them.
Tell me, Leon... in those grand dreams of yours, how many cities like this will there be in Siracusa?
You're quiet. It's not like you.
Just thinking about whether I should give you an actual figure, or just say, all of Siracusa.
If Nuova Volsinii is the only place where this legal system, this new order without the famiglie exists, then it will be nothing but a lonely island in the ocean...
This is something that Lavinia and I have taken to heart.
One lonely island in a swamp matters little. Before that, though, I must demonstrate to Signora Sicilia that this is possible in Siracusa.
How many people, how many things will you leave behind in the process?
You haven't changed one bit, Leon. You're firm, unwavering, and very talkative when you talk about your dreams.
But I'm not interested in engaging in a philosophical debate about the future of Siracusa with you here.
In the past few months, City Hall has rejected all applications by businesses associated with the Bellones. You're behind it, aren't you?
I won't deny it.

I can understand why you did it. You wanted to separate yourself from the past, to demonstrate the purity of City Hall...
Who's the one who hasn't let the famiglia go?
You remember how some associates of the famiglia chose to stay one year ago, even after most left because of what the old man did.
What you don't know is that, as we discussed where we would go from there, there was unanimous agreement from the remaining associates to keep the Bellone name.
Even if the name put a target on our backs for the other famiglie, for all our enemies, and gave us trouble after trouble...
Even if it forced us to seek new opportunities in Nuova Volsinii...
They don't regret it. They had lived together, fought together, and bled together under the Bellone name, carved out our own bit of turf together, earned the respect of everyone together...
Not even the betrayal of the Don and his heir could shake this sense of belonging.
You and the Don wanted to do away with the old and bring in the new, to change this country from the foundation. How admirable. How determined.
But isn't it a little cruel, if there is no room in your great ambitions for the fratelli who have had your back all this time, who had gone through hell and high water with you?
They respect you, Leon. But they're also...
Disappointed.
Yes, disappointed... you knew.
How cold of you, Leontuzzo.
If Nuova Volsinii rejects purely because the mayor was once the heir of Bellone, because of the potential backlash that permitting the entry of Bellone business might bring—
The problem can be solved by taking you out of the equation.
You men are in place nearby. The reveling crowd pays no heed to us. It's not hard for you.
Barely an inconvenience.
I said a year ago that I am responsible to those left behind.
I had intended to tell you this, that night at the restaurant. I've had to push it back for a few days.
I didn't have anything to do with your accident, Signor Mayor. But I will not hesitate to get you out of our way, if you become an obstacle to us.
I believe it. You were never the joking type.
So—
What's that sound?
An even bigger float? Did the City Hall plan a surprise for the parade?
I don't know how many of you are skulking out there tonight, but I can tell you pretty much every driver from the guild is here, be it full members or temporary contractors.
Hah, strength in numbers, she says.
What's next? You'll whip out a spanner from the trunk or something?
I'll tell you what, get out of my sight right this instant, all of you, and I'll pretend I didn't see you tonight.
I never thought we could stop you through strength of numbers alone.
A bunch of haulers who could hardly hold their own in a drunken brawl, much less fight to the death. Life's already hard enough. There's too much in life that we have to give up.
You've proven one thing: The famiglie will never give a second thought about us. Not then, not now, not ever.
Continue to cower, and all we'll continue to see is blood. Our own, our friends', our family's...
It's been a full ten minutes.
Can you reach Gambino?
No, I ain't getting through at all...
Something's not right...
The landship shoulda reached the rendezvous point and made port, and the convoys shoulda been on their way already. But there's no movement at the docks at all...
Are you going to listen to that driver talk until morning, Capone?
They're just a bunch of haulers, Antonio. Are you sure—
They're in the way, Capone.
What's going on? Feels like the whole plate is shaking...
It came from the direction of the port—
Hey, that way—
That's our landship... right?
A balloon escapes the thread that bound it and rises into the air for a dozen seconds, until it comes to an abrupt end when it meets a corner at the top of a massive object.
It is a custom-built cargo landship.

A massive vehicle that travels from city to city, its enormous, resilient hull able to protect massive amounts of cargo from Catastrophes and hazardous terrain. It is not an object that one would expect to see inside a city.

But it has forced its way into Nuova Volsinii by brute force, its tracks crushing the ground underneath it, sending gravel flying. It tears buildings apart like paper as it brushes past them, and the resultant rubble falls from the sky like a rain of stone.
It shows no sign of stopping even as it enters the square, casually brushing aside the trucks that surrounded the courthouse, while mafioso and truck drivers alike scatter, fleeing for their lives.
Finally, slowly, the landship comes to a rumbling halt.
That was when they noticed that there are ribbons and balloons all over this behemoth of steel, meant to roam the wilderness. It is here for the festival.
Ciao!
*cough* *cough* Why so serious, wolf pups? It's Carnevale! A time to celebrate and party!
Be it admonishment or taunt, all could hear the voice coming from the deck of the landship.
The White Wolf removes her mask. No one can take their eyes off her.
Beams from spotlights sweep away the darkness around her. The tactful night breeze brushes open the bright red on her shoulder.
The best tailor in Siracusa has weaved roses and blood into the dress of the White Wolf, equally at home on stage or in the wilds.
Seemingly satisfied at the reaction of the audience of mafioso and truck drivers, Lappland delivers her brightest smile, and takes a graceful bow.
Shall we dance? Fall behind, lose your tail.
Well, why are you standing there like a bunch of statues? The least you could do is blow a kiss or two.
This is the final stop of the night of wonders. What are you doing, if you're not welcoming the parade with the grandest show of them all?
Oh, I see! Most of you are still empty-handed!
My bad, my bad. A game has got to be fair, and a performance has got to be grand. Thankfully, someone has prepared the props ahead of time—
Music begins to play, and Lappland puts her foot forward to the rhythm. Acting like she owns the place, she kicks box after box off the deck; all the while, she never stops talking.
The boxes break apart in mid-air, sending their contents scattered across the plaza—Arts units, guns, staves, crossbows, Originium explosives...
It is a rain of weapons that falls everywhere. All one has to do is stretch out their hand to pick up the raindrops.
Don't be shy, don't be shy! It's what you've been waiting for, what you've been looking for, isn't it?
No rush, no hurry! There's plenty for everybody!
The night is young, Nuova Volsinii!
The White Wolf of Saluzzo has hijacked our landship...

Is this Alberto's doing? No, the use of the landship was a last-minute decision. Alberto hasn't shown any sign of intervening, other than the two of us butting heads on the matter of the Truckers' Union.
What is that Lappland thinking? She's not doing this just for fun, is she?
Still can't get through to Gambino.
You better pray he's still alive.
Uh...
No need to be anxious.
Lappland just spared us the effort of delivering the weapons to the square. Other than the commotion being a little bigger than expected, things are going largely according to plan.
I'm going to the docks. Lappland is just one Lupo. Even if something did happen at the Nuova Volsinii port, there will be enough time to deal with it.
You take care of things here at Court Square. Get the guys from adjacent districts to come over towards Court Square. Take inventory and make sure the goods are delivered properly.
The city's brain and heart is what's really important. Once that's under control, everything else will fall into place.
You got it.
I had the boys clear a path that takes you away from the parade and cops, so that you can get to the Nuova Volsinii port in the shortest amount of time.
The route has been sent to your terminal.
I'm on my way—
Capone?
Wasn't easy to get you out of the shadows, boss.
Is betrayal a proud Sicilian tradition too?
Who are you wagging your tail to? City Hall? Saluzzo? Or one of the other famiglie that's trying to take advantage of the situation in the city?
Oh, just putting you in touch with somebody who wanted to meet you.
Antonio hears footsteps behind him. He turns around and sees the familiar figure at the end of the alley, walking slowly towards him.
As for your question...
You weren't such a bad boss, you know. Temperamental, but sharp. I actually felt pretty guilty when that crazy Lappland came knockin'.
Glad I don't feel that way now. Thanks for that one.
The famiglie got a hundred ways to deal with enemies and traitors. Cut their tongues, slit their throats, bury 'em alive with their families...
We're villains through and through, and there ain't much we wouldn't do... but no famiglia would raise a hand against honest folk who pay their protection money.
What?
Times may change, but there's some rules in Siracusa that never do.
I guess, when all's said and done, you're still a Bolívarian, Antonio.
That was quick.
You need to get your news faster.
I was waiting nearby all this time.
Still, thank you.
If you wanna thank someone, thank Lappland. Not that I got any clue what she's planning.
...If I get a chance.
Need a hand?
You can go.
Ingrid...
Texas stands on the roof of one of the administrative buildings.

She had came here from the courthouse's rooftop. The night wind rustles her clothes. She tries to keep herself pressed against the fence as she moves towards her mark, ignoring the commotion on the ground.
That landship... it's registered with Venezia Trucking.
They secretly brought an entire ship's full of weapons...
It's no longer a secret.
Lappland's actions threaten to cause the situation to spiral out of control.
Maybe that's what she wants... never expect an attention-seeking lone wolf to act rationally.
It's not me that Lappland is after this time. I'm concerned that she might not stop at this.
She once said that all of Siracusa is her enemy.
Go, Texas.
But you'll be by yourself...
Not much we can do here, trapped in the courthouse. The best we can do is to neutralize potential risks as best as we can.
Both Venezia and the Truckers' Union are here for me. That's why I must stay... besides, I can't really move like you do.
I don't think Eirene intends to do anything to me, for the time being at least.
As for Lappland...
Leave her to me.
The landship's tore up the ground, the square is in mayhem, and the courthouse door is blocked...
How will you get to her?
Texas suddenly misses her friends at Penguin Logistics.
Croissant would bash a path through the chaotic crowd with her shield. Exusiai would probably come up with something even more outlandish.
Texas takes one look to estimate a direction.
She takes a deep breath, and takes a leap of faith.
(Claps)
What a dramatic entrance, Texas!
What, not happy that I stole the show?
Why would I be, when you're so eager to come and see me?
Did you break your leg? Do you need a doctor?
Well, it's not like a doctor could get here in this mess. Just find a place and sit down.
Sit where?
You've turned the deck into a... stage?
Did you seriously get this costume custom-made just for this?
It's the Carnevale, after all.
Looks better than what you're wearing, doesn't it?
Hey, Gambino!
(Panting)
Have you given out all the candy?
...There's a little left in the hold.
Go deal with that candy thief in the hold... I think he came up with you.
Leave the front-row seats for me and Texas.
Look, the poor child. The box cracked next to him, and a whole box of weapons spilled into his arms.
See how he stares with mouth agape. Does he not know how it works? The ones in the masks have seen him. He raises the gun in his hand by instinct...
Finally talking, Caesar?
What did you think?
It's not just I who didn't want to speak. Agnese, Vargo... we were ready to rip your throat out, until your little pup showed up.
Is this what that Lappland wanted to show us?
Only a little better than those colorful streets and flippant fireworks.
Does the 'human game' refer to how those humans still refuse to remove their absurd masks and show their true face?
Is this worth all the Signori dei Lupi gathering here tonight?
Are you anxious, Caesar?
Well, Vargo is the one who should be anxious right now.
Thanks to your help, Red should be paying a visit to the Tailor de Montano as we speak.
I am ashamed of you, Zaaro.
Hah, is that an emotion worthy of the Signori dei Lupi, Vargo?
Besides, you were the one who broke the rules first.
You interfered directly in the game. The killer that you had the Higashi dog find attacked the fangs of Caesar and Agnese in public.
Hmph.
There have been too many irregularities in this round. Agnese, Vargo... it is a position shared by more than one kindred. The game itself must be reviewed.
But—
The wolves that laid lazily upon the deck suddenly spring up.
With Caesar, the Signori dei Lupi surround Vargo and Agnese, shifting and crouching while bellowing belligerently.
If not for the night wind that drowned out the sound of the Signori dei Lupi's hunt, those on the ground would have been overwhelmed by terror beyond fathoming.
Well, well. After all these years, we're going back from using our literal fangs.
...Shut up, Zaaro.
The last three fangs have revealed themselves. The Signori dei Lupi shall stay here until this round is decided, and shall not intervene in any manner!
Any in violation shall be exiled from the pack, and be punished in the most cruel way for eternity!
The pack has spoken!
Let us watch with patience.
We will see if the human game is truly so much more interesting, as Lappland said.
Vargo?
Didn't nobody tell you that by the rules of Siracusa, sneaking onto somebody else's turf gets your leg cut off at the knee?
Now get outta those boxes and go sit in a corner.
It's you who should sit down, big guy.
Oh, it's you, the little de Montano tailor.
So, after I spared your life, you tailed me to the Nuova Volsinii port, and sneaked onto this landship?
You got talent on this front, I'll give you that.
I wouldn't have known that you were smuggling so much weapons into the city if I didn't! What's Venezia junior planning?
Whaddya think?
Have you any idea how many people will die?
Didn't you wanna join a famiglia and become somebody? Never thought about what the somebody had to do to get there?

Nonno was right...
A somebody like you keep ordering new clothes because they keep getting soaked in blood...
You're no big shot! Nonno's too good for people like you!
If I was you, I'd listen to my nonno, swallow my tears, and learn how to sew buttons.
I won't let you!
Who's gonna stop me? You?
G-Get back!
Luchino kicks open a plank. Red sewing threads nimbly weave through it, with Luchino holding the end.
...Explosives? You learn that from the movies?
All the Originium explosives are connected by this thread. All I need to do is pull it, and these weapons will burn to ash...
You know what's gonna burn to ash? You.
Or you give up that dream of becomin' a famiglia somebody? Decided to be a hero who teaches the famiglie a lesson?
Sure, the headlines will all be talkin' about the young hero Luchino who saved Nuova Volsinii. Maybe you'll get a medal from the Mayor.
You won't be that little tailor anymore. Everyone'll tip their hats when they see you on the streets...
That's enough, pup. I don't got time to play What I Wanna Be When I Grow Up with you.
I-I don't need that.
What're you talkin' about?
I caused trouble, I'll deal with it myself. Nobody needs to know.
Luchino de Montano won't spend his entire life as a nobody... Maybe I'll never be more than a tailor, but right now, I don't think so!
And I don't like the way you were talking, big guy.
Don't look down on tailors! You don't know what we can do with a needle and a thread!
Hmph.
You know, kid, I'm startin' to like you. You remind me of me, when I was a pup.
Huh?
Two things was common knowledge by the time I got old enough to know my own name—Sicilia is the oldest famiglia in Siracusa, and it ain't around no more because of Signora Sicilia.
All who tried to curry favor with Signora Sicilia came up empty, and all who tried to revive the famiglia were banished. The name 'Sicilian' was a big, fat joke.
But it became my label without my say-so! Just like how everyone calls you 'little tailor'.
That's why I told myself I was gonna be somebody. All Siracusa's gonna know the name of Gambino Ricci, the Sicilian!
Um... and how'd that go?
Didn't go.
If you just want Venezia Junior to suffer, he's sufferin' enough right now.
This ship ain't under his control. You're barking up the wrong tree, kid.
You see that crazy bitch on the deck? Stay clear of her. She's trouble.
You think I'd believe—
I'm just too young, thought Luchino when it happened.
Gambino had been drawing closer and closer. Even worse, Luchino had accidentally snapped the thread in his panic—
But there was no explosion, nothing. Everything was obscured by fog.
The next instant, he saw Gambino floating in mid-air, then he finds himself in mid-air too, falling off the landship and heading straight towards the ground.
He seems to hear a faint sigh.
Cazzo! Cazzo! What is wrong with that white wolf?! What is she doing?
Form up and pick up the weapons. Have the guys nearby join up here.
Get a group to board that landship. It belongs to us!
Gotcha.
S-Stop right there!
E-Eirene's orders, nobody gets through.
Merda, when will these haulers learn to give up?
Well, looks like we won't be disturbed for a while.
What's that look on your face?
I was expecting more gratitude for exposing a famiglia plot to bring violence back to Nuova Volsinii. Maybe even a medal.
Like THIS?
This is not Sauin in Lungmen, Lappland, and those Arts units and explosives aren't candies.
Hundreds of mafiosi are trying to take over the courthouse and city hall. And there are many out there in that revelry who want to see things get worse.
You know what this means as well as I do.
That famiglia haven't changed their ways and incited another civil disorder?
That Nuova Volsinii isn't really any different from the other twenty-two nomadic cities in Siracusa?
Or that Carnevale is finally living up to its name?
There are almost a thousand truck drivers on this square.
The mafiosi are picking up the weapons, and these ordinary folk have to do the same in order to protect themselves—
Why did you decide to come down from the sky, Texas? Who blocked the courthouse doors?
The mafiosi are not the only ones wearing masks on this square.
Famiglia Venezia has been plotting to take over the city with these weapons. You needed to discover their plot. Those drivers needed to protect themselves...
All I did was bring out into the open what everybody wanted.
You know how much I hate chess players. I prefer to put my foot down on the chessboard.
Tonight is Carnevale. It's a time to review the progress that this new city has made in the past year. Everyone has the right to express their views and their demands about it, don't they?
Candy alone isn't enough to make everybody happy.
Look over there...
No rush, Texas.
I know you have a lot of questions. but why don't we make a bet first.
Not interested.
I'll take that as a yes.
You were always more alert, more vigilant than anyone.
You never claimed to have high and lofty ideals, but you have a funny way of ending up on whichever side stood for justice, whether in Yan or Siracusa.
The time now is 11:30. The parade procession will arrive at Court Square in fifteen minutes.
Who do you think will fire the first shot?
Will hundreds of thousands of Nuova Volsinians see two groups lining the streets to welcome them in perfect peace and harmony...
Or will they see the streets run red with blood, and bring this truly Siracusan festival to a climax?
I've seen the weather forecast. Clear skies are expected tonight. There will be no rain to wash away the blood.
Are you awake, pup?
I don't know what's sadder for Umberto: To slowly waste away from age and illness, or to watch how his pups get dumber with each generation.
How sad and pathetic, humans.
Y-You're—
The memory is blurry, but Luchino remembers the voice.
The night that Umberto brought him back to Tailor de Montano...
Sitting on a chair, Luchino looked blankly at the unfamiliar room before him, filled with various fabrics and tools.
Umberto was outside talking to someone. Suddenly, he turns around, and through the window Luchino thought Umberto was showing him to someone else, but does not see who it is.
His grandfather, his only living relation. Luchino feels his stomach churn with nervousness.
Then, he hears a voice mixed with anger and resignation exclaim, 'Shameless human! Up to the same old tricks again!'
Are you nonno's... friend?
Friend?
Friend, teacher, master, employer, client... my relationship with Umberto is not so easily defined.
I am the master of these wilds, pup, and your grandfather is my Fang.
A most incompetent one, I might add...

Guess I'm really getting old.
No matter how long I sat by the worktable, the nights never felt this long...
And I would have noticed you coming in. When?
Ten minutes ago.
Do you mind if I kept working while we talked?
I was delayed by an old friend's unexpected visit. I must hurry and finish the most important work on this suit.
Luchino can take care of the final touch-ups himself.
Why?
Hm?
I smell no scent of wolf from you, even a little bit.
Nor have you masked it in any unusual way. I was watching, I can tell.
...Are you really the last true wolf?
True wolf? Ah, you mean the Fangs.
Yes, you've come to the right place, young one.
I observed Umberto for a long time.
I watched him serve customers of every kind, casually putting his scissors through some of their throats while taking measurement, without getting a single drop of blood on himself.
He was not a warrior, like Ingrid, but he was smart and knew how to make the most of who he was.
He was also good at disguising himself. He spent most of his time in Tailor de Montano, and he was equally in demand as a tailor as he was as an assassin.
That's why I chose him. I told him about the Signori dei Lupi, and the existence of the game.
Nonno's stories were true!
You're the wolf from the stories... no wonder he wanted to leave the famiglia. He was the Signore dei Lupi's chosen, the most special tailor in Siracusa. He sure is very lucid!
Did nonno accept your deal?
No Fang can reject the Signori dei Lupi.
But Umberto set a condition.
C-Condition?
He was not the first Fang to try to bargain with the Signori dei Lupi. Impertinent fools! I know a hundred ways to destroy them and find someone else to take their place.
What did nonno want to do?
Umberto wanted to raise his pup to adulthood, before delivering my victory.
His wife had passed away, he said, and his young pup, Luca, was too young to survive without him.
That was it?
That was it. I agreed.
The society of humans was no more dangerous than the wilderness. I did not imagine it would take up so much of his time and energy.
I visited the tailor's shop from time to time to make sure he was not deceiving me.
That day, Umberto shut down his assassination business, devoting all his time to the sewing machine, fabric, and the young pup as it grew.
But his hands were still steady, and his eyes still sharp. Therefore, I kept my promise.
I needed a Fang that would not break, not easily-distracted prey. I gave him time. I had plenty of it.
But papa...
Died meaninglessly.
I gave Umberto plenty of time to recover from the shock.
It may not have been how Umberto planned it, but at least now he was no longer burdened. And so one night I visited him again, and demanded that he hold up his end of the bargain.
But my Fang once again bargained with me—
The Signore dei Lupi pauses and glares at the young tailor before him.
Do you mean...
He learned that his pup had a pup of its own, the last family he had... He wanted to raise you to adulthood too, before the hunt begins.
'Shameless human! Up to the same old tricks again!'
That was you...
This time, I did not assent. But he seems to have forgotten who he was, and who I was to him!
Every time I pressed him, he babbles something like how life keeps repeating itself, while shamelessly promising that he will hold up his end of the bargain.
But nonno was never in good health, as long as I could remember.
My Fang could hardly defend himself now, much less deliver my victory.
The only result of my patience was that he had grown old.
Nonno actually—
I know.
I know he's only ever cared about his shop, the suits he made, and the people he loved.
That's why he did those things, which he was never made for... Umberto was a fool. I was wrong about him.
Why are you telling me all this, Signore dei Lupi?
All my effort and patience have only earned me an eternity of scorn from the pack for breaking the rules of the game.
You are his pup. Now you must face punishment.
P-Punishment...
All your efforts tonight will be futile, whether you are trying to destroy the weapons, or destroy the ship.
Look down at your chest...
Luchino looks down and sees that the front of his jacket has flown open. He must have dropped his button in the chaos.
Whatever you want to become, pup, learn to sew your own buttons first.
Now, get lost. Go back to Umberto!
You are a terrible actor, Vargo.
That mock anger... were you afraid we would harm that pup?
He was not part of the game—
Have you forgotten the decision of the pack, Vargo?
The Signori dei Lupi shall stay here until this round is decided, and shall not intervene in any manner, or else...
I've already lost, Caesar.
The silvery Signore dei Lupi suddenly looks up.
He casts his gaze towards a corner of the city, a city engulfed in revelry, the night sky bright as day. No one notices that a certain cloud has dispersed.
The wind brings a familiar scent. Red has done her work.
I won't be shameless like Zaaro, Caesar. I have gone against the decision of the pack, and I shall accept my punishment.
Are you mourning your Fang?
Just bitter about losing.
I wouldn't have found you without grandma's help.
Grandma?
That's how you call your wolf? I've never thought of them as being particularly kindly...
But then, Vargo must have figured out a long time ago that he was being deceived. There's a soft heart there, underneath the surface.
You won't resist either?
Either?
The last true wolf that I killed didn't resist.
She just watched me pierce her chest as she cuddled her limp leg.
She sighed and muttered, 'Finally, no need to live in fear anymore...'
Sounds like death was a relief to her.
Well, it's not as if I stood any chance if I tried to resist. Besides, I don't mind this chat going on for a while longer. It would ruin this new suit if we started fighting right now.
Phew—almost there.
Measure, cut, shape, trim... that's how plain cloth becomes stylish clothes.
There are about four hundreds and fifty steps to a standard Siracusa suit, but it doesn't take as much time as one might think, if you go about them step by step, calmly and patiently.
The sound of the sewing machine stops.
The old tailor trims away the protruding threads, gets up from his chair, and walks by the huntress to hang the new suit on the wall.
He sighs, rubs his swollen eyes, and lies down on a sofa in a corner.
Do you mind if I lie down? At this age, I get dizzy whenever I work for an extended period of time.
Grandma was right. True wolves are good at disguising themselves.
I do not understand why you don't smell of wolf, but it doesn't change what you did to grandma.
You ate grandma's eyes, arms and heart...
Answer me, true wolf. Why do you engage in such a cruel game?
I see. That's how you understand the game to be like.
I take it you asked the last Fang you killed the same question.
I don't have an answer either, young one. We were all chosen by fate, by happenstance.
Whether it is lies or the truth that accompanies us, however, all of it will converge upon a single end... What will you do when that time comes?
Find the other true wolf who escaped me.
Her bow is weak. She is no match for me.
No, what I mean is, what happens when you've killed all the true wolves?
The game ends, and grandma is saved.
*Cough* And what about you? Where will you go from there? What will you do?
Have you been seeking out and killing true wolves all this time?
(Waggles ears)
No. I was helping Kal'tsit. There are many things at Rhodes Island that I need to do...
The huntress bows her head.
I have wasted too much time.
The cold glint of a dagger shines out of Red's sleeves. She had been holding it all this time.
You're not afraid, true wolf?
Of course I'm afraid. I always am, whether you're here or not.
My health has been in a steady decline for a long time. You can't help but be afraid when you know that you don't have much time left.
But at least I was not like the one you spoke of, who spent a long time waiting for death's visit... that's much scarier than death.
Come to think of it, I didn't really achieve anything in my life.
A killer who racked up a mountain of blood debts, a father who watched his son die, a grandfather who doesn't know how to teach his grandson, a Fang who never played the game—
But I was a good tailor, a lucky old man.
How many can say that they were doing the work they loved on their deathbed?
I'll leave the finishing touch to you, Luchino...
Red does not raise her dagger. She does not need to.
The old man on the sofa is no longer breathing, as though he had expended his last bit of strength to finish the suit.
Red gazes unblinkingly at the old man's face.
She had seen the deaths of many, all of whom had died with an expression like a mask on their faces.
Even the limp true wolf that was waiting for death had terror on her face when Red came in, which only changed to relief in her final moments.
As for the Reunion members in the Lungmen ruins, and those who invaded Rhodes Island, their faces were twisted from rage or fear.

But the expression on the face of the old man before her never changed, from the moment she walked into the Tailor de Montano, to this very instant. Red was finally certain that he was not disguising himself.
Was the man really who grandma said he was?
The true wolf died before the huntress, but not by the huntress' hand.
Doubt fills Red's heart.
'Where will you go from there? What will you do?'
I don't understand, Grandma...

lines.loc: Fifteen minutes until parade arrives at Court SquareNovember 9th, 1100 11:30 P.M.

lines.x: Seems like you hate Siracusa even more than I do.
Ha, you think I'm doing this for revenge?
No?
I've already said goodbye to my past, Texas.
Maybe there wasn't a formal ceremony, but that doesn't make it any less serious.
I didn't tell you how I became a taxi driver, did I?
I never asked.
Hahahaha.
Zaaro and I came across this pile of scrap metal in the wilderness—
The two of you got together?
Not many people would interrupt me like this, you know—yes, he's my lackey now.
A few Siracusans came to Nuova Volsinii together, but started brawling out in the wilderness for no reason, and the car ended up in front of me.
Siracusa invited, so I had to answer, just like you had to return to Volsinii last year.
Are you absolutely sure you're not just recalling the plot of a radio program?
Hey, I would be happy to sell the story to a radio station or filmmaker. I think it'll make a good road movie.
One year ago, I was sitting at that table in the Grey Hall with you when you won this new nomadic city from that Befana Sicilia.
You could call me one of the startup investors in this youthful dream of a new Siracusa.
Of course I needed to be here at the one-year anniversary of Nuova Volsinii, to celebrate with my friends!
You know, I think I deserve some gratitude for all the problems I dealt with behind the scenes to make sure the festival proceeds without a hitch.
Like scattering weapons across the square?
Haha, much more than just that.
Such as?
Such as my dear father, who is quite, let's say, ambitious about his plans for the new city.
I thought Alberto was in custody.
Oh, come on, haven't you spent enough time in my family to know what a stronzo the Saluzzo don is?
He wouldn't tolerate the thought of anything being outside his control, despite his failure at Volsinii, despite the vigilance of Nuova Volsinii's City Hall and courts against his interference.
What did you do?

Took a few things out of his control, that's all...
You'll find out soon enough.
Are you SURE you're not doing this for revenge?
Well, I guess that's a matter of interpretation.
Oh, and these masked dudes on the square. You remember seeing their boss, that cheerful Perro, in court?
Venezia Junior...
