It's often those who despise the game the most who understand the rules most thoroughly.
He and his Venezia Trucking have never been in violation of the New Urban Management Act. As a matter of fact, he is a two-time winner of the Outstanding Young Entrepreneur award...
He might hate Siracusa more than either of us, Texas, but he know how to read the winds better than most wolves.
But...
But what?
I don't like his hairstyle. Too oily.
I prefer Vulpos who aren't so much like wolves.
A Vulpo mother from a distant land, who returned to Siracusa to seek justice for her Oripathy-afflicted daughter... Touching story, isn't it?
Besides, I happened to know that mother.
What did you do this time?
Helped her find the culprit. Super easy, barely any inconvenience.
I'm sure you would have done the same.
Sounds like the City Hall should give you a medal for outstanding citizenship.
What better time to do that than tonight? You can do the honors.
Nah, it wouldn't sit well with my conscience.
After all, things have gotten to the point where they are, even with all the 'business' that I threw a wrench into.
Now, let's get back to that wager.
Two packs of wolves, increasingly antsy... the end is approaching, Texas.

lines.loc: Ten minutes until parade arrives at Court SquareNovember 9th, 1100 11:35 P.M.

lines.x: ...I'm glad you're here, Ingrid.
The White Wolf of Saluzzo has disrupted our plans. I'm going to the port to deal with matters, lest she ruin everything that Venezia has built in Nuova Volsinii.
I returned my emblem a long time ago. Whatever happens to the famiglia is none of my business.
Drop the act, Antonio. You know why I'm here.
How did you know it was me?
When you told me you knew the truth.
But it was your reaction just now that removed the last vestige of doubt.
Why did you think the Sicilian betrayed you the moment you saw me?
Ah. You tricked me.
Did I?
I had my suspicions, Antonio. If the Tancredis wanted to assassinate the Venezia don, doing it through a child was a highly unreliable method, to say the least...
No matter how intricate their plan was, they could not possibly have prepared a doll that Lisa had only fallen in love with half a month ago because of a movie.
There never was an assassination attempt. You hurt Lisa to agitate me, in order to run me out of the famiglia.
If that was so—
Oh, you spared no expenses to make sure that Lisa wanted for nothing when I brought her back. She called you the 'really nice Perro uncle'.
And the way you beseeched me to come home, after I arrived in Nuova Volsinii...
You wore your mask perfectly, Antonio. You put on such a natural act that it would put the real thing to shame.
No, no, it was all real.
Lisa. Lovely, clever child. I was happy from the bottom of my heart when she called me uncle.
But you were in my way, Ingrid.
I could never have risen in the familglia with you around, much less rise to my present position.
The struggle between Venezia and Tancredi had reached the point where nothing was off-limits. It was a chance I could not afford to miss. Lisa was your only weakness.
As for why I tried to bring you back after chasing you out from the famiglia, the answer is even simpler...
Times have changed, Ingrid. Be it within the famiglia, or in Nuova Volsinii, Venezia Junior needs a strong right hand.
I even made preparations to sponsor Rhodes Island under the name of Venezia Trucking, in order to allow them to devote their full effort to Lisa's treatment.
The Don took us in, and we lived together as Venezia for more than a decade. But somehow, you have it in you to hurt the people closest to you—
Are you going to chastise me for lacking in 'decency', Ingrid?
I'm curious: Do you truly agree with what the old man says, or are you just playing dumb?
We keep emphasizing 'decency,' acting like we're principled, pretending we're filled with love, and claiming we're so different from the other famiglia!
But we're no different, are we? Just a bunch of stronzo with blood all over our hands.
That tailor, Umberto. He's done dirty work after dirty work for the old man, just because he owed the old man one back in the day.
If decency was really about the famiglia, why did he leave the famiglia to focus on his suits?
That scar on your face. Is it a mark of honor, or shame? Behind the scar, how many lives were taken in the name of the famiglia?
Who's really decent in this swamp they call Siracusa?
No one amongst us, at least.
Good. This is the right distance for stabbing.
You really are forthright, Antonio.
I just believe we shouldn't expect something unrealistic from one another.
What the Siracusans should do is be prepared to lose everything, including those they love the most. That's what I used to admire you for.
A bright flash of light.
The alley they are in is separated from Court Square by a high wall. Only a few dim street lamps illuminate the dark street. The sudden flash of light dazzles Ingrid momentarily.
This is your Arts? You hid it well.
Doesn't do much, except cause a strong flash of light... it even looks a little silly, doesn't it?
This is the second time I've ever used it. Without this light, I would not have dodged that bullet while kneeling in the mud of the jungle.
I would not have been able to hold that drunken noble who circled our names away from the list, before cutting his head off.
Anger and distract me with that speech you just gave, then create an opening with the flash—you planned this the moment you saw me, didn't you?

*Cough*— Ugh—
But all my knife found was the omamori you wore on your belt...
You never had a chance, Antonio, the moment I found out what kind of person you are.
You know, it's funny, I was actually trying to think of an excuse to spare your life on my way here.
Lisa's condition can never be cured, but she has found friends at Rhodes Island, even became an operator for them, working to help other Oripathy patients.
I was worried about Lisa's education, but life has thrown her a bone by the strange deliverance of fate—
Sorella! Ingrid Venezia! You will insult me with this 'decency', even now?
You would never have missed my heart if you didn't mean to—*cough*—I shouldn't still be talking.
You're mistaken.
It's just plain Ingrid. A mother whom you've angered.
If I was thinking about decency... it was only so that you wouldn't die with any.
I left you alive so that you could bleed to death.
You deserve the pain, Antonio.
Another firework explodes in the skies not far from here, casting its light on Ingrid's face. The parade is about to arrive at the square. Carnevale comes to a climax.
It will be a long time before they find out about the Perro lying on the other side of the wall. Ingrid snickers at the thought.
She walks over the figure lying on the ground and leaves the alley.

lines.loc: Four minutes until parade arrives at Court SquareNovember 9th, 1100 11:41 P.M.

lines.x: I still can't get in touch with Antonio! What the hell is going on?
And where's Capone at a time like this? I knew you couldn't trust a Sicilian.
What do we do now?
What do you THINK we do? This ain't a movie when you just call 'Cut!' and go to lunch.
Everything has been exposed. City Hall sees us for who we are now. Do you think we can go back to being 'honest citizens of Nuova Volsinii' after this?
Complete Antonio's mission, capture the courthouse, then get in touch with the port.
Do the reinforcements have their weapons?
Yeah.
Good. On my order.
Maybe some of us haven't touched a weapon in a year, but we ain't any more out of practice than these cargo-hauling scemo.
Eirene...
You have the answer?
It's not the one we want to hear, is it?
Tell Eirene that I'm very sorry about Sommer...
But I will not sign this verdict, and this decision is final.
Nuova Volsinii will not tolerate the crimes of the mafia, but that doesn't mean other people can use the opportunity to achieve their own goals.
I've gotten letters from many friends over the past year. Some of them are judges or lawyers in other cities; others have chosen to pursue a different career.
They shared a lot of advice with me, about the jurisprudential basis of the New Urban Management Act, and specific room for improvement.
I read many feelings between the lines. Concern, confusion, hope, even envy...
The New Urban Management Act is a unique thing in Siracusa. It is the basis of this city's existence. Many have their eyes upon it; even more have sacrificed for it.
It's true that it can be powerless at times. But it's especially in times like this that I cannot allow it to become empty words, much less a tool to be exploited.
Still the same old Lavinia.
Hey, Eirene.
What is it?
Listen to me carefully.
These people have gotten their hands on weapons. They have become true wolves. The situation is out of control.
I saw what the mafiosi are capable of when I first came to Siracusa as a kid. They slaughtered my uncle's entire family in just half an hour.
In a few moments I will release smoke to obstruct their view. Get the guys out of here when that happens. Hand signs, signals, whatever, I know you have a way.
You'll need to be quick. I've never released smoke in such a large area before, so I don't know how well it will work, or how long I can keep it up.
The trucks are right behind us. Get in, get away.
I wasn't with Sommer that night. It's partly my fault that he's dead...
There's not much that a luckless loner like me can do, but I can't let my brothers and sisters die here.
That's a gun, not a spanner! Stop pointing it at me—
No, I don't mean point it at yourself!
B-But I don't know how to use it...
Then point it in front of you, Pedro.
But that's—
Don't worry about it going off, or accidentally touching the switch on some Arts unit.
I've talked to Judge Lavinia. These mafiosi have been declared outlaws under the New Urban Management Act...
No one is going to hold you responsible if you accidentally shoot them.
So, take a deep breath, and hold your weapon firm. We're protecting Justice Lavinia, protecting the courthouse, and protecting ourselves.
But, Eirene...
Listen.
What?
The cheering. It's getting nearer and nearer.
The parade will reach Court Square in just a few more minutes.
How will the people interpret what they see? They're the ones who will pass judgment.
The law needs only facts, doesn't it?
Eirene's voice is trembling, but the crossbow in her hand is firm. Suddenly, Lyudmila feels like she is looking at a stranger.
The beast masked-figures on one side, the truck drivers on the other, like they are standing on two sides of a river.
There is no weapon on the ground, only shattered boxes. The weapons are in their hands.
Everyone hears the sounds filling the air.
The rumbling of floats, impromptu band performance, the spirited yelling of acrobats... the parade draws ever closer.
Then, sounds that are fainter, but even more audible. The sound of guns being loaded and locked, Arts units being activated, crossbow safeties pins being unlocked...
There is nowhere to hide. There is nowhere to run.
No one has moved, yet. At the first shot, the dammed river will burst, and the tide of madness will wash over everything.
No one speaks. Nerves are wound to the very tightest. Everyone is listening to the sounds, and their own racing heartbeats.
A new day will dawn upon Nuova Volsinii soon.
But the night is long.
Cazzo! I said, on my order, you idiot!
I-It wasn't me! I only just pulled the safety on my gun—
And it's not them, either. See that shocked look on their faces, they're checking themselves for holes...
Did someone's weapon go off?
No, nobody's hurt... but where did that gunshot come from?
I thought I knew what to do just now, but... w-what do we do now?
I can see the head of the first float already...
...Hey, you over there.
*Cough*—Gah—
Two hundred meters... just two hundred...
Haven't lost your touch, Ingrid... not one bit...
Merda, it hurts... I can feel my blood flow out of me... but that means I still have time...
Get out of this alley, get back to the square, and I can still bring things under control...
We have the courthouse and the port...
Maybe I'll die, but... *cough*... before I die, I'll sit in the judge's chair, and deliver the final judgment of... *cough*... this ridiculous testing ground...
Antonio leans against the wall and does his best to stay upright, wobbling towards the exit.
The alley is dark, and every step takes massive effort.
For some reason, he thinks back to how the guerillas crept through the jungle of Bolívar by night, escaping from the Singas government.
Antonio thinks back to the circles that the noble drunkenly drew on the list, and his severed head when the guerillas cut it off.
He thinks back to Ingrid, Venezia Senior, Leontuzzo, Alberto, Signora Sicilia... all the people he hates.
He will take everything from them. He will draw a big, bloody cross on their names.
It's not over yet—
He stops.
He feels the cold metal of gun muzzle pressed to his forehead.
A-Antonio.
Carle?
I ordered you to get out—
W-Who do you think you are to kick me out?!
You're just the Don's guard tuskbeast, Antonio!
No matter how hard you try to disguise your ears and tail, you—
You want to kill me?
YOU?
Your hands are shaking!
You don't know who my enemies are! You don't know what I'm building! You stupid, worthless coglione—
Carle stares blankly at the lifeless body on the ground. He has never seen such fury as that which is on Antonio's face, even unto death.
It almost seems like it was not the bullet that took Antonio's life, but the fact that it was he, Carle, who fired the bullet.
Antonio's blood seeps through the cracks on the ground, in the shape of a massive, bloody cross.
Hey, you over there.
Eirene?
It's okay.
Before everybody's eyes, Eirene leaves the drivers' line and takes two steps forward, standing in the middle of the road.
Facing off the tense crowd of mafiosi, she takes a deep breath, and lowers her crossbow.
What is the meaning of this?
Look. The first float has rounded the corner...
The parade is here. Are we really going to fight to the death in front of hundreds of thousands of Nuova Volsinii citizens?
Whatever your plan was, it's too late, isn't it?
You want us to lower the weapons?
You think we're stupid?
We're doing the same thing.
The White Wolf scattered this stuff on the plaza. We, as conscientious citizens of Nuova Volsinii, volunteered to clean up the square so that the parade can pass through.
That's what happened, no more, no less. All of you Signori and Signore are witnesses for me and all the drivers behind me.
They, in turn, are your witnesses.
It's Carnevale, after all. Let's give the parade a warm, proper welcome.
*Pant*—*pant*—
Get in, Carle.
The parade is almost here. We can't park here for long.
There's a Carnevale mask on the driver's seat. Put it on and hide that blood on your face.
D-Don Venezia.
Move.
The car starts moving, and the parade crowd passes through behind.
Music and cheering mingle under the bright lights. It has been a good four or five hours since the revelry started, but Nuova Volsinii's excitement shows no sign of abating.
From the expansion plate, through all of Nuova Volsinii's major districts, the procession is about to arrive at its destination. Nobody notices a lone car driving in the opposite direction.
I'm sorry, D-Don Venezia, I didn't get the job done.
You gave me a chance after Antonio ran me out, ordered me to take him out quietly, but I...
If Ingrid didn't already mortally wound him before I got there...
No, Antonio died by your hand.
The Don's permission is required for the execution of a formal associate of the familglia. At minimum, a proper explanation should be given to important members of the famiglia.
Ingrid's methods were not... decent.
Understood, sir.
But now, Antonio is dead... he was your son-in-law, the famiglia's—
Nuova Volsinii has its rules. So does Venezia.
What Anton did was wrong. This is an act of kindness. He is with Bella now.
Besides, his death will not be meaningless.
What next, sir?
We go home and clean the house.
The first thing that City Hall and the courts will see when they get up in the morning tomorrow is Venezia's good faith. We will sit down together, and we will talk like decent people.
It will be a busy night.
Keep up, keep up! Court Square is just ahead! We got to get ahead of that float to get a good place—
Well, don't squeeze!
You're not joining them, Signorina?
Your plain outfit stands out in a crowd of colorful costumes and masks! What an idea, to turn things on its head.
May I take a few photos? I can mail them to you if you give me an address.
Hey, are you listening?
Agnese?
Why don't you say anything, Agnese?
You've never left me before... where are you?
The Lupo girl's eyes harden.
The photographer gazes across the street. The red hood stands out in the crowd, even in this revelry.
A trap? How did you—
You got distracted.
Is Agnese your grandma? You can't find your grandma too?
You're the last one. The last true wolf.
Kill you, and I will know the answer.
Grandma?
I'm here, child.
I won, grandma.
I saw it.
I saw how the entire battle played out. How you zeroed in onto the Fang of Agnese, lured her into a trap, and struck her down with a single blow.
You can see? Have your eyes healed, Grandma?
Have your arms grown back? Is your heart whole again? Why... why can't I see you? Why can't I get close to you?
My sweet child, would you have hunted with such fervor if I didn't say that?
Red killed the true wolf and earned an insipid victory for grandma. Finally.
Now I go off to find another sweet child.
You lied to me?
Why? I don't understand.
Oh, bother.
Alright, let me put it another way. Maybe then you'll know what to do.
You're like the ones you killed, my child.
You're both huntress and true wolf.
Not all the true wolves have been slain. Grandma hasn't been saved yet.
(Claps)
Look at the coordination of the two groups! Truly impressive, isn't it?
In the space of a few minutes, they've hidden all the weapons in the trucks, and stand side-by-side to welcome the parade procession. It's almost like they rehearsed it.
Who would believe me if I said they were pointing weapons at each other just a few minutes ago?
You win again, Texas!
Once again, justice and conscience have taken your side!
Texas does not answer. The White Wolf claps even harder, an ink-black mask on her face.
Actually, I'm more curious why you suddenly put that mask on.
It's Carnevale, after all!
Why the long face, Texas? You won. You should be more cheerful than that.
Look! The parade enters Court Square. That little intermezzo just now never turns into a full-scale riot. Peace and love for all.
Or is it because you just can't help thinking that the only reason it turned out this way was because of a single gunshot out of nowhere, which gave one of the wolves the chance to cool its head?
And Carnevale is nothing but a red carpet laid out on a swamp?
What are you trying to say, Lappland?
What happened tonight is not the same as the civil disorder that happened in the other cities of Siracusa.
...The only difference being the involvement of a bunch of ordinary people, who were pushed to desperation by the mafia's turf wars.
Well... you're not wrong.
But they really did put on a remarkable show.
Especially that driver lady that we saw in court, who seems to have understood a lot of things without having to be taught...
Nuova Volsinii is not a vineyard where you just sow the seeds and wait for the fruits to grow.
Don't forget that it sits right in the middle of this swamp by the name of Siracusa.
The famiglie have never been the only ones who had to crawl through mud and water. Don't underestimate the power of this bog, Texas. It turns everyone into hungry, ravenous wolves.
It has ingrained into the genes of the Siracusans the instinct to turn any new turf into the same swamp.
This endless cycle is what Siracusa is.
Don't try to escape it or change it. The easiest thing to do is to learn to enjoy the swim.
This is what the old man, Signora Sicilia, and you... what all of you have taught me.
Leontuzzo and Lavinia have taken radical measures in order to validate an order that exists outside the mafia... there is a long way to go.
But this is why Nuova Volsinii exists. It offers a new possibility.
But really, did you do all this in order to give this speech?
I didn't know you were so concerned about the future of Siracusa.
Took the words right out of my mouth, didn't you? I was going to ask you that.
Otherwise, why would you have come here all the way from Lungmen the moment you got my invitation?
I know, you're gonna say—
The White Wolf pauses, clears her throat, and imitates Texas' way of speaking.
Calm and even, yet emotion-filled.
Ahem. 'All those lofty ideals are none of my business. I just work for a logistics company.'
'I'm just here because I care about my friend, and because my conscience wouldn't let me turn a blind eye to certain things... just like in Volsinii...'
No, Texas, I haven't forgotten the time that we first met...
You had just came to Siracusa. You were standing at the edge of the mobile platform, holding your Nonno Salvatore's hand, watching my family's workers trim the grapevines.
Your nonno and my father were talking about how Columbia was turning into a dump, about Siracusa's good weather, the new year's harvest, and how you'll be staying a while at our place.
Your eyes were BRIGHT back then, and your expressions were much more interesting than they are now—
That's because the grapes you picked were so sour they almost burned a hole in my tongue.
Everyone on the square is wearing a mask tonight, but none could compare to the one you wear.
Nobody sees things clearer than you. You were the first one to realize that this is a swamp that you needed to get out of.
But it was your disappointment that made you see, Texas. You once had such high hopes for this swamp.
Forget about my speech. You, Texas, are a true blue idealist!
I don't deny it.
Or rather, whether I am or am not makes no difference.
It doesn't change what I have to do at the end of the day.
A very Texas answer. Almost boringly so.
Is that a LANDSHIP?!
Wow, City Hall brought a landship to the show and turned it into a stage?
Wait, look who's on deck...
Is that the Last Texas?
I'm sure, I've seen her in the courts of Volsinii—and the one opposite her...
Lappland? The White Wolf who was disowned by the Don of Saluzzo eight years ago?
Why are they here at the Carnevale?
Must be City Hall's special guests! Think about it, representatives of two major famiglie here at the climax of Carnevale...
It sends an even more powerful message than Signor Bellone's promotional video!
Damn right!
Lappland takes off her mask and gives it to Texas.
What?
You won the bet, didn't you?
In the old days, the famiglie murdered each other on Carnevale, the last surviving wolf taking the masks of the vanquished.
I suppose we need to give the old customs new meaning in this new city... a new life, that kind of stuff, I guess.
As for the question just now, it doesn't matter what your answer is.
Siracusa makes all of us wear masks, after all.
We're part of Carnevale's main event, Texas.
Regardless of why you're here, here and now, you represent part of Siracusa, whether it's the part that's new, or the part that's broken and needs to be thrown out.
Mafiosi and truck driver, city officials and judges, and all the Siracusans on the square, will see in us the meaning that they want to see.
You may, of course, choose to turn around and leave.
Come on, Texas, don't just stand there. All of Nuova Volsinii is watching!
Silence.
Texas gazes at her old friend, and old enemy, for a long time... and finally extends her hand.
A dozen beast floats drive into Court Square, as though it had been previously rehearsed, and the reveling crowd continues to gush in like the tide.
The sound of music and the cheers of the crowd keeps growing louder. The bright lights and fireworks in the sky provide the perfect backdrop for the duo on stage.
Do you hear that? They would not let the good night go gently.
They're cheering for you and me, for this new city, for a new life.
You're still really good at making me feel sick.
I'll take that as a compliment.
But I don't mind being part of the event. I don't mind these people seeing some sort of 'meaning' in me.
Without hope, there would never be disappointment. Whether in Columbia or Siracusa, I do, in fact, hope to see a truly vibrant city emerge from the swamp.
If the challenge and complexity of building it are beyond what I can imagine, then I will face the challenge with my friends, not run away from it.
Alright, I've made up my mind. I'll take care of one more trouble for you and Siracusa!
I'm bringing those stupid wolves back to the wilderness, so that Siracusa won't be affected by their dumb game.
No need to thank me. But when the obstacles have all been pushed out of the way, how will they—you—change this swamp?
There's something I suddenly wanted to ask you, Texas.
What?
Are you disappointed that I didn't come to Nuova Volsinii for you?
No.
Another very Texas answer.
Break a leg, Texas.
Mm-hm.
Texas takes the mask.
The White Wolf lets go, without warning, without hesitation. She turns around and walks into the landship's hold.
Oh, and Texas?
You were fun while it lasted.
Well...
Landship, the White Wolf of Saluzzo and Texas, and the Truckers' Union...
What else did we miss?
S-Signor Bellone...
Hello, Eirene. Haven't seen you in a while.
Perhaps we should have a little chat once the festivities are over.
Go on, Mayor Leontuzzo.
The duo's performance has come to an end. Time to deliver the closing speech for Carnevale.
Thanks for bringing me here, Demetri. And sorry I couldn't give you a satisfactory answer even at the very end.
Since when have you been the type to say useless crap like that?
...I suppose you're right.
Good evening, folks. I'm Leontuzzo.
As you can see, I was a little dinged up, but I'm out of the woods now.
I was actually there in the middle of the parade on the way to Court Square, experiencing this city that we live in together.
I'm sure little needs to be said about the impressive performance of Signorine Lappland and Texas.
But I wanted to see your faces, say a few things, and spend the night together with you guys.
Many things have changed in Nuova Volsinii over the past year, not least the completion of the Nuova Volsinii Port, the biggest cargo port in Siracusa.
Every connection it makes is like a pulse of the city, demonstrating healthy, robust vitality, supporting thousands of enterprises and hundreds of thousands of livelihoods.
We founded the country's first police academy, a new code of laws, and people are increasingly identifying with Nuova Volsinii.
This makes me glad and anxious in equal measure.
The city continues to face many problems that cannot be ignored. For example, the citizen credit system remains inconvenient, and there are many cases where it doesn't achieve its intentions.
We also understand that the uniqueness of New Urban Management Act makes it difficult for our citizens to receive the support they need for cases that span jurisdictions.
A year is a very short period of time. City Hall and the courts still have much to do.
What I want to ask is this: What is Nuova Volsinii to you?
That's also the question that I want to ask other Siracusans in Volsinii, Sicilia, Montelupe... what is Nuova Volsinii to them?
And what is this young city to neighboring Leithanien, distant Ursus, and all the other countries in Terra, for which change is a constant?
Are we a new possibility to them? Not just a different kind of violence, but something stronger, firmer, more close-knit, something worth talking to?
Will they understand that our strength comes from our most earnest desires, that we, and all the others gathered here today, can safeguard our hard-earned new lives?
That's what we have been trying to achieve over the past year.
There is no famiglia in Nuova Volsinii. The reforms associated with this saying doesn't rely on 'Siracusan' methods, as Siracusans know it—
A secret negotiation, an assasination in the dark, a purge of the city, a deadly struggle between famiglie...
All that has happened here, and all that will happen, will be viewed through the lens of the law.
We're giving a new order to our new life. The swamp that Siracusans used to take for granted will no longer faze us.
A friend once said to me that I'm walking down a thorny path.
He's right. But all the challenges that we have faced is exactly why Nuova Volsinii must continue to move forward.
For everyone who has been hurt on this path, for everyone who may be hurt in the future, I'm sorry.
But at the same time, I continue to extend an invitation to all the friends who have something to ask of Nuova Volsinii.
We shall trample the thorns together and make this brand-new, one-of-a-kind city better, little by little, until it's ready to usher in a new era.
As a matter of fact, a new era for Siracusa has already begun, a future filled with infinite possibilities.
Demetri gazes forward silently, at the silhouette of Leontuzzo outlined by the countless lights.
What can I say, Leon?
Saluzzo's wine isn't bad, but I prefer my own.
Bellone will find its way into this city, in my way, by your rules.
I promise that to you.
Demetri turns around.
The mayor's speech continues, but he has received the answer he was looking for. Demetri has accepted the invitation, or perhaps, the declaration of a very long war.
Enjoy the rest of the night!
And, the dawn of a new day.
Grandma...
I don't understand...
You're not getting away this time, bloody pup.
As the Carnevale drew to a close and the dust settled in the square last night, Lyudmila spotted a familiar red silhouette.
She followed the figure through winding alleys—sometimes hitting dead ends, other times standing still. It clearly had no direction.
Lyudmila readied for an ambush, biding her time. Her strike landed true.
She pinned the figure down, pressing her blade against its heart. Just a little more, and she would avenge her mentor and end this haunting ghost.
But the red Lupo offered no resistance. She simply knelt, muttering as she stared into space.
Lyudmila looked into her eyes and saw nothing but emptiness. The silence was brief, and the red lupo spoke—
...True wolf.
What?
Grandma was the true wolf...
I was like them, chosen by the true wolf...
We hunt for the true wolf, die for the true wolf, deliver the true wolf's victory...
The story was a lie... Grandma was a lie...
It's all a lie...
You're talking about your game?
So Maestro had to live in fear all her life with a limp leg, just because she's part of this stupid game?
You killed her for this 'victory'?!
I don't understand—
Lyudmila suddenly feels a force from her counterpart, and hears the sound of blade ripping through fabric.
She takes a step back.
Is this what your Kal'tsit taught you?
Violence, hatred, ideals, fate... your Kal'tsit always has an explanation, doesn't she?
Kal'tsit... Kal'tsit...
The kneeling, red-garbed Lupo searches through her clothes in the rain, eventually pulling out a tooth from where Lyudmila's knife had struck.
It's a small, sharp tooth. The knife has left a clear mark on it.
A baby tooth?
Grandma told me that this kind of tooth falls out because it can't tear the prey's flesh.
But I have always kept it... not sure why.
Kal'tsit said I should keep it safe, until I know the answer...
I didn't listen to Kal'tsit...
I found the girl with the bow.
She's alive. You missed her vital organs.
Did you doubt yourself in the final moments, scum?
Do you want to see this Kal'tsit of yours?
(Waggles ears silently)
Well, I'm going to find her and ask her what all this is about.
Don't you dare die before then.
And don't you dare get my knife dirty.
Is this how Nuova Volsinii's law enforcement does things?
As we have already explained to you multiple times, Signor Saluzzo...
Your request for legal counsel is perfectly lawful and reasonable. We duly notified Saluzzo Vino, but nobody has come... It's not because of any interference on our part.
I want to see your supervisor. No, I want Justice Lavinia, or Mayor Leontuzzo.
Umm...
Uh?
Buongiorno, Signore. Had a bad night?
Lappland?
You have been banished from the Saluzzos forever, traitor. You do not have the right to rescue me.
Oh please, old thing, still so full of yourself.
Do I look like I'm here to rescue you?
Catch.
Lappland throws an elaborate gift box through the cell door window.
Alberto does not catch it. The gift box falls to the ground, its lid coming off and revealing a new suit—briskly cut, elaborately embellished, even more so than Lappland's outfit.
How do you like it? Meant it to be a gift for the Carnevale. The work of a master Siracusan tailor.
We already said goodbye last year, dear father, so I wasn't planning to see you again. I preferred to spend my time on more meaningful things.
But then, it would be ungrateful of me to not present a parting gift, after all the lessons you taught me...

But the little tailor who was supposed to run errands got into a little problem, so I had to make this trip myself.
Unfortunately, the Carnevale has already come to a close...
But from the looks of things, you need something to wear for prison, don't you? Haha.
Are you trying to provoke me?
It won't work, Lappland.
How can you be so clever yet so childish? Every Saluzzo knows that Alberto Saluzzo is never wrong—
Tsk-tsk.
Well, since I'm here, let me tell you one other thing...
Let's just say... your stay will be much longer than you think.
...What?
The vote has been taken, Signorina Lappland.
Despite your mistakes, you are the don's only daughter, and the heir to Famiglia Saluzzo.
We need you to take charge of the famiglia's affairs for now. The most urgent tasks are responding to City Hall's investigation of Saluzzo Vino after the Carnevale and getting the don out.
What? I was just here for a drink.
I thought there are no famiglie in Nuova Volsinii. Are you sure you want to play this inheritance game?
Besides, didn't the old man pick his guy before he went in?
Do you mean...
If you're looking for help, you should look for it from Saluzzo Vino's primary partner.
I am honored to take on this responsibility, Signorina Lappland... and my friends from Saluzzo.
So, what's the plan?
We will immediately audit all our businesses in the city, in accordance with the New Urban Management Act.
In addition to illegal registration, if Signor Saluzzo has indeed been manipulating Saluzzo Vino to engage in prohibited activities, it is our duty to gather materials and self-report to City Hall.
If necessary, I will restructure the leadership of Saluzzo Vino.
A necessary sacrifice, to protect Saluzzo Vino.
Oh my, that sounds like so much hard work. Here's a toast to you.
You...
How dare Bellone... did he plan this from the beginning?
You would allow the Bellones to take the Saluzzos' business in Nuova Volsinii—
Remember what you said? 'Banished from the Saluzzos forever,' right?
Or did you think I was going to come home to roost, like a migratory fowlbeast?
There was always a question on my mind, PAPA.
Why were you so angry when I tried to kill the Bocche al Lupo at Brunello, or when I let Texas go in Columbia?
Were you angry that I acted against the famiglia's interests, violated the rules of Siracusa...
Or were you just angry that I was telling everyone that there are certain things which are outside Alberto Saluzzo's control?
You should probably save your strength for the trial. I doubt the new management of Saluzzo Vino would make his legal counsel available for you.
My dear father, you're not just a fool, you're a boring fool.
It's really addio this time. But cheer up!
You'll be better-dressed than all your fellow inmates, after all...
Hey, ain't you done yet?
I don't know what the paper pushers at City Hall are thinkin'. Same points, same social service, but why I gotta sweep two more streets than you, Capone?
That's SIGNOR Capone to you, Gambino. Don't forget who saved you when you passed out in the alley that night.
Vaffanculo.
Did you hear? Half of Venezia Trucking's management are in police custody right now.
It wasn't just cigar and vinyl sent to Venezia Senior's room that night. There were also detailed business and personnel records of Venezia Trucking for the past year.
Anyone who was closely associated with Venezia Junior got rooted out, and the City Hall cazzi came into work the next day to a thick pile of self-reports lying on their table.

Venezia Senior cut ties so swiftly and decisively that City Hall couldn't find fault. Venezia Trucking will get a full makeover but won't be shut down. Venezia Junior's impact was reduced to a minimum.
The famiglie sure are finding new and... clever ways of dealing with traitors in Nuova Volsinii.
The don really struck like thunder, didn't he? I thought he came for the festival.
We'd've been rotting behind bars by now, if we'd listened to you and stayed there.
What's the difference?
We lost all our citizen points, and we gotta start again from scratch... once again, we got nothing left.
Well, not exactly nothing...
Capone casts his gaze at the intersection, where a familiar yellow cab is parked.
Lappland said we can have the cab... our reward, basically.
How long do you think it'll take before we get a taxi company started in this city? I know what we'll call it. Taxi Siciliano. Whaddya think?
...I think we should get outta here, because that judge is coming.
Wait, I don't think she's coming for us—
You're just going to leave without saying anything?
Lavinia.
I won't stay any longer. Don't want to keep you up at night.
Where will you go?
Rhodes Island, maybe? Haven't seen Lisa in a while.
Now that this old score has been settled, I have the time, and the mood.
I'm not here for small talk, Signora Venezia. We're not that close to begin with.
Earlier in the morning, Venezia Senior came to see me and Leontuzzo...
Whatever is going on with Venezia is none of my business—
He would pay any price for Venezia Trucking's survival, even if it's just one factory but he also had another request—to grant you safe passage out of Nuova Volsinii.
Given the actions of Venezia Junior on the night of the Carnevale, your efforts helped City Hall contain the violence to some extent. Therefore, leniency should be considered.
However, the charges of assault and battery still stand, not to mention that your actions were acts of revenge within a famiglia.
We will issue an arrest warrant for you, Signora Venezia.
Arrest warrant?
I have multiple mafioso bounties on me, and even Signora Sicilia's prohibition... the arrest warrant will be a nice souvenir, I guess.
Do not dismiss it so casually, Signora Venezia.
This arrest warrant is empowered by the New Urban Management Act. Unlike Signora Sicilia's prohibition, there is no room for mediation just because some famiglia comes into power or loses it.
The law is the law, Signora Venezia. We've talked about this before.
Your every move will be watched by Nuova Volsinii, and perhaps all of Siracusa in the future. The arrest warrant will remain valid until you are brought into custody.
I appreciate the reminder, Lavinia.
I'm not dismissing your declaration. I could tell that you're a firm person when I saw you at the winery.
In the Siracusa I knew, you didn't need a reason to kill. But that's not how things should be. Every action needs a reason—a proper reason.
It's true for judges, for mothers... even for killers.
I approve of your reason, which is why I approve of your action. Besides, as I've told you, I don't have anything against your profession.
I'm just thinking... if you issued a warrant for me...
Should I look forward to when we meet again?
Well, I should go.
...Hello, Eirene.
Are you feeling better, Signor Bellone?
Yes. Thanks for asking.
There is something I need to give you.
...A resignation letter?
I-I would like to resign as president of the Truckers' Union.
One year ago, I promised you and Judge Lavinia that no mafioso would infiltrate the Truckers' Union... but I failed to keep the promise.
I stopped them from putting their people in the union, but it didn't prevent them from achieving their goal at all.
Sommer, and all the others whom they used... it's my fault. And I tried to coerce Judge Lavinia when things were exposed.
It's not your fault. Not all of it, at least.
No one can stay calm when they see their best friend die and their family, along with themselves, backed into a corner.
In the end, little harm was done.
But I just can't forgive myself...
I received a joint letter just before you arrived.
It was signed by two hundred full-time drivers of the Truckers' Union, plus several hundred more temporary and contract staff, asking for amnesty towards your actions during the Carnevale.
Th-They shouldn't have...
Rethink your resignation, Eirene.
City Hall will hold an internal hearing next week to discuss integrating the Truckers' Union as a government department alongside City Development and the police.
Together, we will build Nuova Volsinii's logistics and the city itself, Eirene. I look forward to a long and productive partnership with you.
I still trust the decision that Lavinia and I made that day. You may or may not be the best person to lead the Truckers' Union, but you're a decent person.
In the two days since I woke up, I've learned about what I missed, and thought about many things.
Finding the direction is easy. It's the journey that's hard—a long, long journey.
How did it go?
The association will need to cooperate with the investigation and face penalties. However, City Hall won't pursue what happened during the Carnevale or those who helped with the smuggling.
Good.
I thought I asked you to wait for me at the camp.
I came to say goodbye. Didn't want to deal with the commotion if I said it at the camp.
Y-You're leaving?
I found the red one. She's not in great shape...
I thought she was your sworn enemy.
Yes. She needs to go back to somebody, and that somebody is my other sworn enemy, whom I'll have to visit sooner or later...
It's a long story. Anyway, if nothing much is going on with the Union for the time being, I want to visit this place called Rhodes Island.
There's something I want to ask before I leave.
That night, when you ordered us to point our weapons at the Venezia mafiosi... what were you thinking?
That all of the Union's problems will go away if we kill those bastards?
And who smuggled what will remain a secret forever?
Were you confident that it could be done, with so many people behind you, and so many weapons in our hands?
Were you confident that you wouldn't be held responsible?
Were you thinking about protecting the people behind you, or how, like the bastards on the other side, you held the fates of so many in your hands?
Was it the gunshot that made you stop... or was it your judgment from deeper down?
No need to tell me, Eirene. You know the answer.
I told you the story of Talulah, a respected leader who became an evil dragon. I failed to see it happen, and I didn't kill her.
I won't make the same mistake again. I'm watching you, Eirene. I'm the sword that hangs above your head.
If that's what you become, I will come for you, no matter where I am... and I will kill you.
I hope that day never comes. I hope we can truly, sincerely, give each other our blessings... friend.
Eirene is alone. The Reproba with the furrowed brows has vanished without a trace.
The night wind of late autumn is chilly, but Eirene just stands there, lost in thought.
It's me, Sandro.
About the joint letter... thank you.
I thought you promised Texas to take us away from Siracusa.
Haha, I lied.
Besides, did she really win the bet?
Is the Carnevale over, Zaaro? Or has it just begun?
The old wolf of Venezia has hidden his fangs and claws, blending back into the pack. The pup named Eirene has learned the tricks of hunting on her own...
That's why I said you guys are stupid.
You set all sorts of dumb rules, but you can't resist breaking them.
The game becomes increasingly boring with each cycle. The all-powerful Signori dei Lupi are reduced to petty worries, like humans.
From one wolf to another, tell me, Zaaro, what set the revelry beneath us into motion.
Power, obviously.
Yes. A port, a community, a city... wherever there are people, there is power.
Whether one pursues it, detests it, seeks to control it, or grasps it like a drowning man clutching at straws...
They all end up holding power tight and wielding it with fervor.
Power is the true wolfpack. It's the game all Siracusans… no, all HUMANITY was born playing.
There is no judge, no rules... its variety is infinite, and it does not end except by death.
Like the wind on the wilderness, there is no direction, and no one knows where it comes from... but it never stops.
Interesting. Very interesting indeed.
I'll concede that you have discovered this pleasure earlier than us. Do you intend to put an end to the Fangs, and have Lappland lead us into this new game?
That depends on what Vargo, Agnese, and the ones over there who haven't spoken think.
No human has ever been the alpha of the pack in the memory of the Signori dei Lupi... nay, of the Beast Aristocrats.
Do you have a hierarchy over here?
That's a human concept to shackle their own brethren. You call yourselves the Signori dei Lupi; act like one.
If she truly does bring us pleasure...
...There's one more thing you forgot.
Look at the crystals on her leg. She's pretty far gone, and Oripathy is an uncurable disease for humans. Will we, deathless Signori dei Lupi, recognize a dying human as alpha?
True, I probably don't have much time left.
Doesn't that add to the thrill of the game?
I have a feeling.
That this is going to be the best time of my life.
Oh, and Emperor came to talk to me.
It's not just the pack that has sensed this unease... it is a crisis that has put all the Aristocrats of Beasts on guard.
Emperor and our cousins in other races wish to talk to us.
If Lappland is our alpha, then...
There is no rest for the wicked, is there?
Tell them...
The wilderness keeps its promises.
Wait for me.
Please take a look, Signorina Texas. I'll submit it if there's no issue.
There's so much paperwork to become an independent consultant for you guys?
Mi scusi.
The Carnevale drew a large number of tourists and delivered on the promotional expectations. Nuova Volsinii Port has received a large number of orders in recent days...
Moving forward, our logistics systems will reach beyond Nuova Volsinii, connecting with other cities in Siracusa, and even abroad. There is no room for negligence.
Penguin Logistics has the qualities we're looking for, but Lungmen is a long way from here. The process for bringing you on as a special consultant will be a little more complicated.
Just sign here!
Welcome to Nuova Volsinii, Signorina Texas. Things are about to get busy.
Excuse me, could you submit my application with Cellinia's?
We meet again, Cellinia.
Giovanna?
I thought you wouldn't be coming back for a while after you left for Columbia.
Has your journey for inspiration come to a close?
Sora started her second Siracusa tour two months ago, didn't she?
Did you notice the name 'Caterina Culture Ltd.' on the sponsor list?
Since when have you become so close with Sora?
She's a friend, too. One whom you introduced me to.
My injuries have healed, and I've found new inspiration. It was time to come back.
The happy times of the past may be gone, but that doesn't mean we can't create even happier stories ourselves. I needed time to prepare before...
Now, I'm ready.
And none too soon, I see. It seems like you have made a decision, too.
Yes. Things will be different between us now.
I'm looking forward to it, Cellinia.
Zzz...
Father? Father Agenir?
Oh, it's you.
I wouldn't have realized that you were in Nuova Volsinii, if I didn't see your photo in the submissions for memories of the Carnevale.
Well, I didn't tell you. Or anyone else, for that matter.
Are you planning to leave now?
I'll go back to Montelupe with you. There are certain things that I should explain to Signora Sicilia personally.
Why?
Huh?
This city is just entering its second year. Its mayor has many things to attend to.
Take it easy, young man.
I'm loath to admit that we're like concerned parents who quietly follow their children the first time they go out on their own...
But yes, I came to this city at Sicilia's behest.. to see it, deal with some people or declare a decree on her behalf, if it comes to that.
But I did nothing. The Carnevale had its little hiccups, but it's come to a satisfactory conclusion, hasn't it?
In certain ways, it's true that you haven't completely fulfilled your promise to Sicilia, to transform Nuova Volsinii into a new city that's totally different...
I can't deny it.
Well, Sicilia had so much more on her plate when she first came back to Siracusa. It wasn't easy, bringing guns and order to this country.
Of course, you're facing more resistance than she did. That's why she wouldn't pour cold water on the fire of youth... she hasn't gotten that old.
I'm not coming back with you to Montelupe to prostrate myself before Signora Sicilia and beg for her mercy.
I have some plans about the future of Nuova Volsinii, about promoting the New Urban Management Act...
Ah. Very good, then.
I want to ask, though... if you came to deal with problems, why didn't you do anything?
I saw an old friend.
How long has it been, Umberto?
...Father Agenir?
I remembered that you moved to Nuova Volsinii. Just thought I would drop by.
Are you waiting for someone?
Mafia killers? Or the Fangs?
I don't know. But death will find me, one way or another.
I'm old enough.
Sorry, it's been a long time since I walked into a church, or offered a prayer for the dying.
Some priest you are... I still remember the day we met.
It never occurred to me in a thousand years that I would be hired to kill a Sankta, a priest no less. A moment of surprise was enough for you to pull a gun from your robes...
I would never have gone back to being a tailor, if not for you.
All in the past.
Why have you chosen to come to Nuova Volsinii tonight? Is the Carnevale—
Any final wishes, Umberto? I'll see what I can do.
No, Father.
None at all?
I remember you have a grandson... Luchino, is it?
There's only so much I can do for him.
To me, he's my only family. To him, there will be a day when I'm not his only family anymore...
The future awaits the young.
